title: "HowTo: Dual Monitors (Xinerama/TwinView/MergedFB)"
date: 2006-07-22
forum: Multimedia &amp; Video

### Post by Ziox on 2006-07-22
[B][SIZE=4]Features of this HowTo:

[/SIZE][/B]     [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]**The following howto addresses and solves the problems of Linux's,****especially**** (****X****)(****K)Ubuntu's,**[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]**support**** (****or lack of****) ****for dual monitors.****The howto is assorted into four driver-specific categorizes.**[/FONT][/LEFT]
[LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**Xinerama **(open source and binary driver) [/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**    nVidia-only TwinView **(binary driver)[/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**    ATI, Matrox MergedFB** (open source driver)[/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**    ATI-only BigDesktop **(binary driver)[/FONT][/LIST][LEFT][FONT=Verdana]All four options perform the same function.  However, because of the variations of graphics cards in the market, and the different supports provided by the card makers, procedures that enable dual monitor on one computer/card do not work for other computer/card. Each of the four methods has pros and cons of its own.  They are listed below.

[/FONT][/LEFT]
    **[FONT=Verdana]Feature Differences:[/FONT]**[LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana]&#8220;**Xinerama** is an extension to the X Window System which allows applications and window managers to use the two, or more, physical displays as one large virtual display.&#8221; --Wikipedia.  In other words, it allows X, as we'll call the X Window System, to use more than one monitor for display. [/FONT][LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**    Pros**: Since Xinerama is an extension of X, most graphics cards, including Intel Integrated Cards and Matrox Cards, work well with it.[/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**    Cons**: There are several known problems with Xinerama.  It requires that the physical screens to have the same bit depth.  Also in most implementations, OpenGL (3D) direct-rendering only works on one of the screns.  Windows that should show 3D graphics on other screens tend to just appear black.  However, the Solaris SPARC OpenGL implementation allows direct rendering to all screens in Xinerama mode.[/FONT][/LIST][FONT=Verdana]
[/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana]    &#8220;**TwinView** is a mode of operation where two display devices (digital flat panels, CRTs, and TVs) can display the contents of a single X screen in any arbitrary configuration.&#8221; -TwinView Appendix G.[/FONT][LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**    Pros**: Twinview merges two screens into one, &#8220;tricking&#8221; X to think that there is only one screen.  This always provide better OpenGL direct-rendering.[/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana]** Cons**: TwinView requires the binary nVidia Driver, which means only nVidia cards are supported.[/FONT][/LIST][FONT=Verdana]
[/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana] &#8220;**Merged Framebuffer** mode allows you to use 3D acceleration on both heads of a dualheaded Radeon card.&#8221; --MergedFB Wiki[/FONT][LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**    Pros**: Similar to TwinView, MergedFB merges two screens into one, therefore supporting 3D direct-rendering on both screens.[/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana]** Cons**: MergedFB only works with the open sources drivers (mga, ati, , which lack direct-rendering support for many Radeon models. MergedFB also limits 3D contexts to 2048x2048.[/FONT][/LIST][FONT=Verdana]
[/FONT]
[*][FONT=Verdana]**    BigDesktop** allows for dual screen control by merging the 2 screens into one single screen, allowing direct rendering on both monitors.[/FONT][/LIST]**[FONT=Verdana]Common Step for Each HowTo:[/FONT]**

    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]These steps are essential for the HowTos to work, please follow them precisely.
[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][B][FONT=Verdana]All commands give should be executed within terminal.

[/FONT][/B][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][COLOR=Red][I][B][FONT=Verdana]BackUp File:

[/FONT][/B][/I][/COLOR][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][COLOR=Red]*[FONT=Verdana]Perhaps the most important step of them all.  Since we are going to be editing a system file that have the potential, in the instance of an accident, to disable your Xserver (aka No GUI/Mouse Function), it is EXTREMELY important to execute this command:[/FONT]*[/COLOR][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
[COLOR=Black]**sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf_backup**[/COLOR]
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]The function of this command is to make a backup copy of your Xorg.conf file (which we'll be editing soon).

  [/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][COLOR=Red]***[FONT=Verdana]If for any reason, after the guide, you are unable to activate Xserver, execute the following command:[/FONT]***[/COLOR][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
**sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf_backup /etc/X11/xorg.conf**
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT]*[COLOR=Red]**[FONT=Verdana]Now, we always want to have a clean slate, because it makes the work much easier, and there is less of a chance that some other arbitrary item will mess this whole thing up.  [/FONT]**[/COLOR]*[/LEFT]
        [LEFT]*[COLOR=Red]**[FONT=Verdana]So, unplug all your monitors except for one, then execute the following command:[/FONT]**[/COLOR]*[/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
**sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg**
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Just hit enter for the most options, though if you have the binary drivers installed, you might want to select that. This code will generate a new, clean, standard xorg.conf file for us to work on.[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Now, choose the method with which you wish to achieve Dual-Monitor capability:[/FONT][/LEFT]
[LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana] Xinerama: [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773624](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773624)[/FONT][/LIST][LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana] TwinView: [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773584](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773584)[/FONT][/LIST][LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana] MergedFB: [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773710](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773710)
[/FONT][/LIST][LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana] BigDesktop: [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773544](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773544)[/FONT][/LIST][LEFT][FONT=Verdana]If you have any questions, comments, or problems, please post on the corresponding thread.[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Hopefully these guides have been helpful.

[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][COLOR=Red]***[FONT=Verdana]Tips:[/FONT]***[/COLOR][FONT=Verdana][COLOR=Red]These are awesome tips I borrowed from the Paralipsis Blog. So if you have any questions and such, take a look at this section first.[/COLOR][/FONT][/LEFT]
[LIST]
[*][FONT=Verdana]   [COLOR=YellowGreen] **Do not do your config inside of X11. Before you login, press Ctrl-Alt-F1 and login to the text console. There, shutdown the graphical login like this: sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop. This way you can edit the xorg.conf and test your setup one piece at a time. The command startx launches X11 as though you had logged in and drops you back to the text console when you 'logout' of the GUI.**[/COLOR][/FONT][/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=Blue]Make a backup of your starting xorg.conf file. Sometimes you just need to start over.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=Purple]Baby steps. Make small changes at each step along the way and you'll be guaranteed to have less to undo.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]     [COLOR=DarkRed]If something stops working that worked before, go back a step and try something else. Keep notes to guide you.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=Red]Put a post-it note on each monitor with the name you discover it has. It makes life a lot easier to deal with when sorting out what's where.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=DarkGreen]Get one display at a time working from start to finish.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=Navy]If your system appears locked up, first try to use Ctrl-Alt-Backspace to kill X11. Or, you can use Ctrl-Alt-F1, F2, F3, etc to try to access the other text login prompts.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=Orange]If you have configured SSH access to your computer, you can do all your configuration and testing from a laptop.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=Indigo]If you are really, really screwed, first try pressing Ctrl-Alt-Del and wait 30 seconds to see if init manages to shut-down and reboot your computer. This is the safest way of doing things as it cleanly unmounts the filesystems. However, if you have broken your X11 config, you will have to use the &#8220;safe mode&#8221; startup to continue your work. This simply bypasses a few steps in the startup, most notably starting the graphical login. From the text console you can copy your original xorg.conf back into place and reboot.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=LightBlue]If even Ctrl-Alt-Del doesn't work, use the SAK. Hold down Alt-PrintScreen (this is the SysRq key combo), and while holding down those keys, press the letters 'S' to sync the disks, then 'U' to unmount the filesystems and 'B' to reboot the computer. Always try that before going for the power button. It's a bit awkward to do all that while holding Alt-PrintScreen, but it's not something you'll do often.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=DarkGreen]Relax, your computer isn't likely breaking, you're just dealing with sophisticated hardware drivers and sometimes things go into bad states where the computer doesn't know the video card is in the wrong mode.[/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    Finally, Google is your friend. If you get odd error messages in the log file, try pasting them into google. Usually someone else has encountered the very problem you are and often has written about it.[/FONT]**[/LIST][LIST]
[*]**[FONT=Verdana]    [COLOR=Red]Good luck![/COLOR][/FONT]**[/LIST]

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-07-23
twinview is very similar to this howto.  However, twinview is only supported by the nvidia proprietary drivers. Also, under twinview, X views it as have only 1 monitor/screen (even though there are two monitors attached), resulting in much better performance than this howto (Xinerama).  

From your problem with Xinerama, it seems that the "nv" driver doesn't support multiple outputs.  But to tell you the truth, I don't own a nVidia card, so i can't test this Howto personally. I know it works for most ATI cards.

EDIT: this link is guide for TwinView, but again, you have to install nvidida proprietary driver for it to work:

[http://www.ublug.org/ubuntu/twinview/twinview-howto-breezy.html](http://www.ublug.org/ubuntu/twinview/twinview-howto-breezy.html)

EDIT AGAIN: try moving the "ServerFlags" section before the "ServerLayout" section...

### Post by harty83 on 2006-07-24
I found a gentoo wiki article ([http://gentoo-wiki.com/Twinview_Example#X_Setup](http://gentoo-wiki.com/Twinview_Example#X_Setup)) that says the following (bold is my emphasis)

> This article is about how to configure X with one AGP Geforce 6600GT with one DVI and one VGA adapter. A TFT is connected to the DVI adapter and a CRT is connected to the VGA adapter. The following combinations are described:

    * (1) TFT only (called single)
    * (2) TFT and CRT using nvidia's twinview without xinerama (called twinview)
    * (3) TFT and CRT using two screen sections with xinerama (called xinerama) 

The following combinations are not described:

    * (4) TFT and CRT using nvidia'a twinview with xinerama
    * (5) TFT and CRT using two screen sections without xinearama 

The difference between (2) and (4) is the fullscreen behaviour. (2) shows a fullscreen window on one screen. (4) shows a fullscreen window over both screens.

The difference between (3) and (5) is that (3) allows to move a window from one screen to the other and (5) disallows it. For looking videos in fullscreen mode on one screen, (1), (2), (3) and (5) are possible configurations.

I always use the nvidia driver from nvidia. The configurations (2) and (4) will only work with this nvidia driver. **(1), (3) and (5) will also work with the open source nv driver.**

I prefer (3). 

But when I follow his tutorial, I get an error that no screen is found.

I'm beginning to feel that all is hopeless with the nv driver.

### Post by Intangir on 2006-07-25
this seems to work pretty well

i enabled it and noticed it stretched my desktop across both screens which i didnt like
it was a 1024x768 image stretched across two 1280x1024 screens..
so instead i scaled it to 1280x1024 in gimp, and then told the background deal thing to tile it, so it shows the same image in both screens like before

the bad thing is that the transparent parts of my panels dont do transparency right, it was some ugly yellow tint to it for some reason..

to fix that instead of doing transparency, i told it to use the background image as its background, so that fixed that. but still kind of lame that transparency for some reason got messed up in the gnome-panels


the maximazation of windows is exactly how i wanted, maximizes to just that screen


i have a question, how does this behaviour differ from twinview? what does twinview do differently? or is it just that twinview is nvidia only?

i havent tried twinview, does it maximize across BOTH screens? cause that would suck and i wouldnt want that anyway

or does it just use the same video buffers across both screens? cause i notice when i drag from screen to screen its doing alot of redrawing of the area moving across, which seems like a pretty good clue that its using two seperate buffers with the xinerama

### Post by Ziox on 2006-07-26
> **Intangir said:**
> this seems to work pretty well

i enabled it and noticed it stretched my desktop across both screens which i didnt like
it was a 1024x768 image stretched across two 1280x1024 screens..
so instead i scaled it to 1280x1024 in gimp, and then told the background deal thing to tile it, so it shows the same image in both screens like before

the bad thing is that the transparent parts of my panels dont do transparency right, it was some ugly yellow tint to it for some reason..

to fix that instead of doing transparency, i told it to use the background image as its background, so that fixed that. but still kind of lame that transparency for some reason got messed up in the gnome-panels


the maximazation of windows is exactly how i wanted, maximizes to just that screen


i have a question, how does this behaviour differ from twinview? what does twinview do differently? or is it just that twinview is nvidia only?

i havent tried twinview, does it maximize across BOTH screens? cause that would suck and i wouldnt want that anyway

or does it just use the same video buffers across both screens? cause i notice when i drag from screen to screen its doing alot of redrawing of the area moving across, which seems like a pretty good clue that its using two seperate buffers with the xinerama

glad that it worked out for you. As for twinview, i'm no expert at it (I don't own a nvidia card), but I can try to explain it.  Twinview only works on nVidia cards, (MergeFB works only on ATI cards) and Twinview (and MergeFB) requires the use of proprietary drivers. As for how TwinView works, I'll just quote from the book "Ubuntu Hacks":

"TwinView is a bit like Xinerama because it binds multiple displays together into a single virtual display, but the difference is that it's done at a very low level within the video driver and the hardware itself.  Both heads share a single framebuffer, which basically tricks Xorg into think you only have a single monitor, giving it much better performance than Xinerama, as well as allowing OpenGL to operate across both heads at full speed. As fare as Xorg is concerned, it's only driving one screen: it just happens to be a screen with very odd dimensions."

hope this helps

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-07-26
are you using two cards? I don't think Xinerama works with two cards, because Xinerama sees the two monitors as one screen, that's spread over two monitors. So Xinerama has to use the same BusID for both screens...if you want to get two screen working with two cards, try commenting out the whole "ServerFlags" section...

EDIT: sorry didn't see your previous post, when you commented out the "serverFlags" section, it told Xorg to use both cards, where Xinerama, as i said only uses on card...So technicially, you can have 4 monitors enabled...:p

---

EDIT: sorry didn't see your previous post, when you commented out the "serverFlags" section, it told Xorg to use both cards, where Xinerama, as i said only uses on card...So technicially, you can have 4 monitors enabled...:p

### Post by Ziox on 2006-07-26
> **gorilla_king said:**
> okay, when i don't comment out hte screen 0 and screen 1 lines and put that in the terminal i get 
```
0000:00:0c.0 VGA compatible controller: S3 Inc. ViRGE/DX or /GX (rev 01)
0000:01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: nVidia Corporation NV15 [GeForce2 GTS/Pro] (rev a4)

```

and when i comment out the lines and then restart it with ctrl+alt+backspace it just stays blank until i restart my computer and edit the xorg.conf file in joe with a shell

I noticed in your xorg.conf file on page 2, that you don't have a busID in your device sections:

Add this for your nVidia card section:

BusID "PCI:1:0:0"

and this for your other card:

BusID "PCI:0:12:0"

now make sure that the "serverflag" section is commented out and that screen 1 and screen 2 are commented out...restart X and it *should* work...

---

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildd@rothera)  Thu Jan  5 15:32:31 UTC 2006

### Post by Rosenrot on 2006-07-28
Heres my Xorg.conf file ; )




# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 NVIDIA Corporation NV31 [GeForce FX 5600]"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
	VideoRam	262144
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
 	Screen      0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 NVIDIA Corporation NV31 [GeForce FX 5600]"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
	VideoRam	262144
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Monitor1"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Monitor2"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	44-67
	VertRefresh	49-76
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen1"
	Device		"0 NVIDIA Corporation NV31 [GeForce FX 5600]"
	Monitor		"Monitor1"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen2"
	Device		"1 NVIDIA Corporation NV31 [GeForce FX 5600]"
	Monitor		"Monitor2"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
   Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Screen1"
	Screen		"Screen2" RightOf "Screen1"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

however, it didnt quite work for me.
the two monitors are "cloned" ( <-i think the term is, same image on both screens). how might i modify my settings to get one big screen?

heres my xorg file

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Xpress 200M (RS480)"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Screen		0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Xpress 200M (RS480)"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Screen		1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Xpress 200M (RS480)"
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1440x900" "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Xpress 200M (RS480)"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1440x900" "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default layout"
	Screen		0"Main Screen"
	Screen		1"Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option	"Xinerama"	"On"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
   Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

make your section look like this:

	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"

But with my card...it's weird...when I plug in my card, both screens are identicle.  However, when I restart X, only one of the screens comes on, while the other one seems to "die" Does this happen to yours?

And for the ATI driver, you can trying changing "ati" to "radeon", though i doubt that i'll do anything...but it just *might* :)

already...googling time...brb :)

EDIT: can you provide to me both of the monitors' information? such as CRT/LCD... Thanks

### Post by Ziox on 2006-07-31
@xolot1

see if adding this line would help in your "serverflags" section:

Option "DefaultServerLayout" "Multihead"

EDIT: also, can you either post your Xorg.0.log at /var/log/Xorg.0.log or add it as an attachment?

is this the key?
(WW) RADEON(0): Direct Rendering Disabled -- Dual-head configuration is not working with DRI at present.

i don't that's the problem...it just means that direct rendering is not available on the second monitor...that's all. I knew that (guess i should've add that to the HowTo)

but i think it is this line:

(WW) RADEON(0): Failed to detect secondary monitor, MergedFB/Clone mode disabled

did you start you computer with the monitor plugged it?
if not, try...

---

### Post by xolot1 on 2006-07-31
the monitor's always connected cause it works well with windows, so yes...

inserting radeon didnt work, neither did the multihead option.

i have no idea how to test how the monitor is detected/how to troubleshoot it. i can say that when i close the lid of my laptop, ubuntu locks itself.  but if i keep the external monitor on, then i can continue using ubuntu even if the laptop monitor is shut down.  that could be hardwired though.

ideas? im willing to experiment. i almost need to clean install ubuntu anyways, so i dont mind being... haphazard(?) if necessary.

edit: to test this multimonitor stuff, ive been ctl-alt-bkspacing to reload xorg. is this correct?

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-07-31
> **xolot1 said:**
> the monitor's always connected cause it works well with windows, so yes...

inserting radeon didnt work, neither did the multihead option.

i have no idea how to test how the monitor is detected/how to troubleshoot it. i can say that when i close the lid of my laptop, ubuntu locks itself.  but if i keep the external monitor on, then i can continue using ubuntu even if the laptop monitor is shut down.  that could be hardwired though.

ideas? im willing to experiment. i almost need to clean install ubuntu anyways, so i dont mind being... haphazard(?) if necessary.

edit: to test this multimonitor stuff, ive been ctl-alt-bkspacing to reload xorg. is this correct?

some people (me) would say that ctrl+alt+backspacing would be enough, but some say that for such an advanced editing procedure, you should do a complete shut down and a new boot...you can try this...actually you should try this...

anyone with the knowledge of anything similar is encouraged for their input...

thanks in advnace :)

### Post by kebabbert on 2006-08-01
I am running on Solaris X86 v10. I have almost got dual screen on my ATI mobility 9000 (Dell laptop D600) working. The laptop screen is perfect, but my 20" TFT is not really good. It shows like 1280x1024 or something. Care to look at my xorg.conf file?

PS. This HOWTO is the best I found on the internet. Keep up the good work!

************************************************


#Section "ServerFlags"
#	Option "Xinerama"	"true"
#EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "X.org Configured"
	Screen       0 "Screen0" 0 0
	Screen	 1 "Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"			
	InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
# Option "Clone" "On"
 Option "Xinerama" "On"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	RgbPath      "/usr/X11/lib/X11/rgb"
	ModulePath   "/usr/X11/lib/modules"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/TrueType/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/Type1/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/Type1/sun/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/F3bitmaps/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/misc/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/100dpi/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/75dpi/"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "IA"
	Load  "Xst"
	Load  "dbe"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "record"
	Load  "xtrap"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "bitstream"
	Load  "type1"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Keyboard0"
	Driver      "keyboard"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Mouse0"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/mouse"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	#DisplaySize	  290   210	# mm
	Identifier   "Monitor0"  #DELL LAPTOP SCREEN
	VendorName   "SEC"
	ModelName    "3450"
EndSection
Section "Monitor"
	DisplaySize	  433   271	# mm
	HorizSync	31.5 - 81.1
	VertRefresh	56.0 - 75.0
	Identifier   "Monitor1"   #NEC 20WGX2
	VendorName   "SEC"
	ModelName    "3450"
EndSection


Section "Device"
        ### Available Driver options are:-
        ### Values: <i>: integer, <f>: float, <bool>: "True"/"False",
        ### <string>: "String", <freq>: "<f> Hz/kHz/MHz"
        ### [arg]: arg optional
        #Option     "NoAccel"            	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "SWcursor"           	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "Dac6Bit"            	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "Dac8Bit"            	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "PanelOff"           	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "DDCMode"            	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "MonitorLayout" "AUTO,AUTO"     	# [<str>]
        #Option     "IgnoreEDID"         	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "UseFBDev"           	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "VideoKey"           	# <i>
        Option     "MergedFB" "true"          	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "CRT2HSync"          	# [<str>]
        #Option     "CRT2VRefresh"       	# [<str>]
        #Option     "CRT2Position"       	# [<str>]
        #Option     "MergedDPI"          	# [<str>]
        Option     "MetaModes" "1400x1050,1680x1050"          	# [<str>]
        #Option     "NoMergedXinerama" "true" 	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "MergedXineramaCRT2IsScreen0" 	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "DisplayPriority"    	# [<str>]
        #Option     "PanelSize"          	# [<str>]
        #Option     "ForceMinDotClock"   	# <freq>
        #Option     "RenderAccel"        	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "SubPixelOrder"      	# [<str>]
        #Option     "ShowCache"          	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "DynamicClocks"      	# [<bool>]
#	Option  "MonitorLayout" "LCD,CRT"
        #Option  "OverlayOnCRTC2" "true"
        #Option  "CRT2Position" "RightOf"
	Identifier  "Card0"
	Driver      "ati"
	VendorName  "ATI Technologies Inc"
	BoardName   "Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
#	Screen	0
EndSection
Section "Device"
        #Option     "MonitorLayout" "AUTO,AUTO"     	# [<str>]
        Option     "MergedFB" "true"          	# [<bool>]
        Option     "MetaModes" "1400x1050,1680x1050"          	# [<str>]
	#Option  "MonitorLayout" "LCD,CRT"
        #Option  "OverlayOnCRTC2" "true"
        #Option  "CRT2Position" "RightOf"
        #Option     "NoMergedXinerama"  "true"  	# [<bool>]
        #Option     "MergedXineramaCRT2IsScreen0" 	# [<bool>]
	Identifier  "Card1"
	Driver      "ati"
	VendorName  "ATI Technologies Inc"
	BoardName   "Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen 1
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen0" #DELL LAPTOP SCREEN
	Device     "Card0"
	Monitor    "Monitor0"
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     1
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     4
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     8
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     15
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     16
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Virtual 1400 1050
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen1" #NEC 20WGX2

First of all, thanks for the encouragement.  I've been amending this HowTo everyday, so check back often! :)

And to make the code dipslay in "blocks", use the code brackes 
```
 

``` 

or hit the hash mark when you are posting...

now to the more serious problem...

I've noticed that you apply your own touch to your xorg.conf file :p which is good! But i'm wondering why do you have Xinerama and MergedFB on?

```
Option     "MergedFB" "true"          	# [<bool>]
        Option     "MetaModes" "1400x1050,1680x1050"
```

I don't know if this matters or not, but unless you are extremely skillful with xorg.conf and linux, then I don't suggest to have both of them on at the same time.

Now I noticed under neath the "screen" section, that you have many other display subsections.  So why not just take out those sections and just keep the display you want...;) 

So erase/hash out MergedFB comment, and see if it helps.

if what Ziox suggests doesn't work
try playing around with the numbers

HorizSync	31.5 - 81.1
	VertRefresh	56.0 - 75.0

under your second monitor
thats what i did to fix the resolution issue i had with my second monitor

don't forget to save the Xorg.conf and then restart the computer completley to see changes

just shutting down x server and then restarting it would stay the same no matter what

### Post by Rosenrot on 2006-08-01
Code:

Option "TwinView" "True" Option "TwinViewOrientation" "RightOf" Option "UseEdidFreqs" "True" Option "MetaModes" "1280x1024,12801024; 1024x768,1024x768"


    * The first two lines are self-explainary.
    * "UseEdidFreqs" allows the NVIDIA X driver to use the valid HorizSync and VertRefresh frequency ranges from the EDID whenever possible.
    * "The MetaModes tells the driver what resolutions to use for the two monitors, with the resolution reported back to Xorg being the sum of the dimensions. In this case, 2560x1024 or 2048x768" -Ubuntu Hacks


That's it. Restart the X and it should work.
__________________________________________________ ___________________________




"1280x1024,12801024; 1024x768,1024x768"

add a x between 1280x1024 ;) this will probably save a few people from asking for help if they just copied and pasted without reading what they did

and the guide looks much better than it was when i used it(and it was already excellent ^_^)

ill do more proofreading 8]

---

However, there is still a slight problem. I have a 20" TFT with 1680x1050. It wont work. If I have both screens 1400x1050, it works great. Any suggestions, so I can run 1680x1050?

Here is my xorg file:



Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Dual Screen"
	Screen       	0 "LAPTOP_SCREEN" 0 0
	Screen	 	1 "NEC20WGX2_SCREEN" RightOf "LAPTOP_SCREEN"
	InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
	Option 		"Xinerama" "On"
EndSection

	RgbPath      "/usr/X11/lib/X11/rgb"
	ModulePath   "/usr/X11/lib/modules"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/TrueType/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/Type1/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/Type1/sun/"

	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/100dpi/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/75dpi/"
EndSection

	Load  "Xst"
	Load  "dbe"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "record"
	Load  "xtrap"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "bitstream"
	Load  "type1"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Keyboard0"
	Driver      "keyboard"
EndSection

	Option	    "Device" "/dev/mouse"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	#DisplaySize	  290   210	# mm
	Identifier   "LAPTOP_MONITOR"  
	VendorName   "SEC"
	ModelName    "3450"
EndSection
Section "Monitor"
	DisplaySize	  433   271	# mm
	HorizSync	31.5 - 81.1
	VertRefresh	56.0 - 75.0
	Identifier   "NEC20WGX2_MONITOR"  
	VendorName   "NEC"
	ModelName    "20WGX2"
EndSection

        Option     "MetaModes" "1400x1050,1400x1050"
	Option	"MonitorLayout" 	"TFT,TFT" 
	Identifier  "0Radeon9000"
	Driver      "ati"
	VendorName  "ATI Technologies Inc"
	BoardName   "Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen	0
EndSection
Section "Device"
        Option     "MetaModes" "1400x1050,1400x1050"

	Identifier  "1Radeon9000"
	Driver      "ati"
	VendorName  "ATI Technologies Inc"
	BoardName   "Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen 1
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier "LAPTOP_SCREEN"

change your second screen section to

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "NEC20WGX2_SCREEN"
	Device     "1Radeon9000"
	Monitor    "NEC20WGX2_MONITOR"
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes 	"1680x1050"
                HorizSync       28.0 - 64.0
                VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
                Option         "DPMS"
		#Virtual 1680 1050
	EndSubSection


i wouldn't recommend this though because unless your laptop supports the same resolution

ive had a lot of issues trying to run seperate resolutions and still haven't gotten it to work

you might have to use twinview or mergefb but i don't have a lot of knowledge about the two and don't know if that will work

I can see why your annoyed though because of the widescreen resolution
im assuming your getting those annoying bars on the side of the screen : /

so try messing around with the vert Refresh and HorizSync numbers i gave you

its hard for me to expalin how it works but it solved my problem
just spend a couple hours editing and saving those numbers then restarting the comp

hope it works ^_^

### Post by kebabbert on 2006-08-02
Ok, I got everything working now. A Dell Laptop D600 with ATI mobility Radeon 9000 running dual screen, internal 15" 1400x1050, external 20" TFT 1680x1050, on Solaris 10 x86. Thanx very much for your help!

What I did to get different resolutions working, was that I looked into /var/log/Xorg.0.log file, and added those numbers into the "Monitor" section:

...
(II) RADEON(0): Supported additional Video Mode:
(II) RADEON(0): clock: 146.2 MHz   Image Size:  433 x 270 mm
(II) RADEON(0): h_active: 1680  h_sync: 1960  h_sync_end 2136 h_blank_end 2240 h_border: 0
(II) RADEON(0): v_active: 1050  v_sync: 1053  v_sync_end 1059 v_blanking: 1089 v_border: 0
(II) RADEON(0): Ranges: V min: 56  V max: 75 Hz, H min: 31  H max: 83 kHz, PixClock max 170 MHz
(II) RADEON(0): Monitor name: 20WGX2 
....

transforms to: 	
ModeLine 	"1680x1050" 170.00 1680 1960 2136 2240 1050 1053 1059 1089




However, there is still a problem. On my external 20", the image is slightly blurred. The font are not razor sharp, which it is on my laptop screen. Maybe the problem is specific for Solaris?

*********************************
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Dual Screen"
	Screen       	0 "LAPTOP_SCREEN" 0 0
	Screen	 	1 "NEC20WGX2_SCREEN" RightOf "LAPTOP_SCREEN"
	InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
	Option 		"Xinerama" "On"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	RgbPath      "/usr/X11/lib/X11/rgb"
	ModulePath   "/usr/X11/lib/modules"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/TrueType/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/Type1/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/Type1/sun/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/F3bitmaps/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/misc/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/100dpi/"
	FontPath     "/usr/X11/lib/X11/fonts/75dpi/"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "IA"
	Load  "Xst"
	Load  "dbe"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "record"
	Load  "xtrap"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "bitstream"
	Load  "type1"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Keyboard0"
	Driver      "keyboard"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Mouse0"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/mouse"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	#DisplaySize	  290   210	# mm
#	Modeline "1400x1050"  155.80  1400 1464 1784 1912  1050 1052 1064 1090 +hsync +vsync 
	Identifier   "LAPTOP_MONITOR"  
	VendorName   "SEC"
	ModelName    "3450"
EndSection
Section "Monitor"
	DisplaySize	  433   271	# mm
	HorizSync	31.5 - 81.1
	VertRefresh	56.0 - 75.0
	ModeLine 	"1680x1050" 170.00 1680 1960 2136 2240 1050 1053 1059 1089
	Identifier   "NEC20WGX2_MONITOR"  
	VendorName   "NEC"
	ModelName    "20WGX2"
EndSection


Section "Device"
	Option	"MonitorLayout" 	"TFT,CRT" 
	Identifier  "0Radeon9000"
	Driver      "ati"
	VendorName  "ATI Technologies Inc"
	BoardName   "Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen	0
EndSection
Section "Device"
	Option 	"MonitorLayout" 	"TFT,CRT" 
	Identifier  "1Radeon9000"
	Driver      "ati"
	VendorName  "ATI Technologies Inc"
	BoardName   "Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen 1
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier "LAPTOP_SCREEN" 
	Device     "0Radeon9000"
	Monitor    "LAPTOP_MONITOR"
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes 	"1400x1050"
		#Virtual 1680 1050
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "Screen"
	Identifier "NEC20WGX2_SCREEN"
	Device     "1Radeon9000"
	Monitor    "NEC20WGX2_MONITOR"
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes 	"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Im sorry, but you should not give me credit to looking into the /var/log/Xorg.0.log file, as I found that information in:

BTW, my second screen showing a blurred image, I think I didnt really succeed with the configuration. Because my monitor on screen display, says it is 1280x1024. If I force my 20" to show a lower resolution, the image get fuzzy. I think that is the problem. The strange thing, is that if I do a window that spans the entire laptop screen which is 1400x1050 and move it to the external screen, it can not span the screen (indicating that the external screen IS using 1680x1050).

### Post by Rosenrot on 2006-08-03
your fuzzy resoluion is because you are stretching a 1400x1050 resolution 1680x1050
but Solaris is still treating everything on both monitors as 1400x1050 even though the second monitor is set to 1680x1050

its a solaris issue i believe because im able in ubuntu to hook up a widescreen and a regular screen just fine with the settings you have in your Xorg.conf file

my dad uses Solaris 10 at his work and uses multiple monitors
ill ask him when he gets home

---

Here's my Xorg.conf:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. M9+ 5C61 [Radeon Mobility 9000 (AGP)]"
	Driver		"radeon"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
        Screen          0
        Option "MergedFB" "true"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "AUTO, AUTO"
        Option "CRT2Position" "LeftOf"
        Option "MetaModes" "1024x768-1024x768"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. M9+ 5C61 [Radeon Mobility 9000 (AGP)] 2"
	Driver		"radeon"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
        Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Laptop Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"External VGA Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Laptop Screen"

	Monitor		"Laptop Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"

	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24

	SubSection "Display"

	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-06
OMG, OMG, I finally have Dual Monitors. :) :) All thanks to this post:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=181501&postcount=3](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=181501&postcount=3)

I simply copied and pasted what he/she said to put in the device and screen section. This is for ATI MergedFB **only**. I believe that this line is what made the difference:

Virtual 2048 768

I think that is the combination is the total dimension of the two screens:

meaning: 2048 = 1024 + 1024

768 is the same because the placement of my screen doesn't add the vertical dimensions up. If that makes sense, so...I'm going to update my HowTo. Hopefully this will help those who have trouble with ATI MergedFB.

### Post by fogster74 on 2006-08-07
Hey guys

Just thoguht I'd share my experiences. I'm new to Linux - first installed Kubuntu then, on a whim, removed it and installed ubuntu. The change has been staggering - I would have thought they were about the same but SO many of the problems I had with Kubuntu have dissapered with Ubuntu!

Just a quick note on Kubuntu / dual screens on my Dell machine before I share my working solutions. In Kubuntu I "lost" the abolity to change the resolution through the control panel - I'd get a message saying "The control module cannot be loaded". It would appear that this is the equivalent in Ubuntu of the error message already mentioned 

```
Error:
The X Server does not support the XRandR extension. 
Runtime resolution changes to the display size are not available.
```

I guess that Xinerama just doesn't support on-the-fly resolution changes.

As I need resolution changes on times (I run my laptop away from my desk at 1920 x 1200, at my desk I run it at 1280 x 800 with a second external display running at 1440 x 900) I've created two xorg files. Ok, so I have to manually choose to use one of them but this isn't a bad compromise as the dual screen thing is now working perfectly. The most amazing thing is the effect Xinerama has on the performance of the card - with Xinerama my "Ant" OpenGL screensaver works on one dispaly only and I can;t have much mroe then 0.5 fps frame rate. Without it, using my "ATI" solution below, when my "Ant" OpenGL screensaver kicks in both screens dim - and then, truly incredibly, both screens display the Ant screensaver - with a different version running independently on both screens at a superb frame rate! It's superb to see - even the texture backgrounds that the ant "walks" around on are different!

So here's the pros and cons for me of using Xinerama or not

**Using Xinerama**
I can drag a window from one monitor to another.
I can have a firefox window (or any other application) running on both windows.
I CANNOT change the resolution of the screens outside of the xorg file
The frame rate is APPALING for OpenGL

** Not using Xinerma (using my "ATI" solution)**
I can't drag a window from one screen to another. Can be frustrating at first
I can't run firefox in both screen 1 and screen 2 - I get a message saying an instance of Firefox is already running. Can be a nuisance
I CAN change the resolution on the fly (wihtout even having to restart the Xserver)
The frame rate is fantastic!


**What I did:**

Before messing with the Xorg file, I downloaded the ATI driver from the ATI website and installed this on the laptop (with my second screen connected).

This site ([http://craig.copi.org/computers/ms-1013/breezy.html](http://craig.copi.org/computers/ms-1013/breezy.html)) proved incredibly useful, and this led me to this site ([http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Installation_Guide](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Installation_Guide))

I would highly recommend you visit this site of you're trying to get this to work on an ATI card - but here's the code I entered:
```
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install linux-restricted-modules-$(uname -r) #Okay if it is already installed
sudo apt-get install xorg-driver-fglrx
sudo depmod -a
sudo aticonfig --initial
sudo aticonfig --overlay-type=Xv
```

I'd been trying to get this to work for two weeks - I cannot STRESS enough the importance of the last line for me with the overlay command in it. This was the breakthrough that got my laptop graphics card working properly!

After installing the ATI driver (I installed it whilst I had the second monitor attached - think this is probably a good idea), I was able to go into System - Preferences - Screen Resolution and, without any fiddling whatsoever, I now had the ability to set the resolution for both screens independently!

Here's my xorg.conf.ATI file:


```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	Screen         "aticonfig-Screen[1]" RightOf "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"
	Driver      "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection
```



You'll notice in the code above that the suggestions in this wiki about duplicating the screen resolutions etc become unnecessary as the ATI driver install does it all for you!

To get Xinerama working (gain moving windows from one screen to another but lose ability to change resolution plus kills the frame rate):



```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerFlags"  
        Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	Screen         "aticonfig-Screen[1]" RightOf "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Option         "Xinerama" "On"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"
	Driver      "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen	    0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection
```


Here the only changes are right at the top of the file - the new *Section "ServerFlags"* part and the *Option "Xinerama" "On"* setting. And that's it!

Someone did say that Xinerama may only work well with screens of the same resoltuion. In my case it's working fine at 1920 x 1200 on one screen and 1440 x 900 on the other.

I'm delighted to have this working at last - I really feel I can now use Ubuntu for most of my work. Just got to get my TV tuner working now....!

I hope this is of help to someone!

PS Haven't tried MergedFB yet - will try that some other time. Possibly offers the advantages of Xinerama whilst retaining hardware acceleration?

---

> 
PS Haven't tried MergedFB yet - will try that some other time. Possibly offers the advantages of Xinerama whilst retaining hardware acceleration?

and you are one of the lucky few who is able to use the fglrx driver :)

	EndSubSection

		Depth     8
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1920x1200"
	EndSubSection

		Depth     16
		Modes    "1920x1200"

		Modes    "1920x1200"

	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"

	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection


```


Stevo

There a total of three different HowTo's within this guide. Xinerama/TwinView/MergedFB.  None of them should be combined together unless you know exactly what you are doing.  They are all seperate HowTos.  (Thought I put up a warning...).

I can see that you have heavy customization in your xorg file.  So I would suggest, if you wish to try out mergedFB, to save your current xorg.conf file as a different file:

sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf_old

Then disconnect the second monitor, and do this command:

sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

which would generate a clean xorg.conf file.

Now follow the MergedFB HowTo (just a couple pasting), then it should work for you.

Hope this helps.

---

### Post by The Belgain on 2006-08-07
Well, I'm still not having any luck here.  I've read through the threads you linked to, and have tried adding a 2048x768 mode.  I've also tried adding a "Virtual 2048 768".

Neither of these have worked.  My X log file looks similar to what I posted earlier: it says it can't find the second monitor, and also claims that none of the modes it's trying are supported - I'm just looking for 1024x768@60Hz here....

Why is this so hard?  I really have to say that while I've been thoroughly impressed with the Linux/Ubuntu experience overall, it's niggles like this that do make it a real pain to fully get a system up and running.  Compare to windows where setting up dual monitors on either ATI or nVidia is a real breeze.  There really needs to be some work going into a good GUI (or at least a decent set of commandline scripts) to set up various monitor configurations on common hardware.

Anyway, any suggestions here?  Could someone who's got a definitely-working ATI mergedfb setup using the opensource radeon drivers please post their complete Xorg.conf?  That would be a real help.

---

Since you asked...here's my working MergedFB Xorg.conf file:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Option 		"TVOutput" 	"NTSC"
#	Option "AccelMethod" "EXA"
#Option "AGPMode" "4"
#Option "EnablePageFlip" "true"
#Option "DDCMode"
#Option "RenderAccel" "true"
#Option "SubPixelOrder" "NONE"
#Option "ColorTiling" "false"
Option "MergedFB" "True"
Option "CRT2HSync" "31.5 - 60.0"
Option "CRT2VRefresh" "60-85"
Option "MonitorLayout" "LCD,CRT" #use LCD,CRT even if you have 2 CRTs
Option "OverlayOnCRTC2" "true"
Option "MetaModes" "1024x768-1024x768"
Option "CRT2Position" "RightOf"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
		Virtual 2048 768
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

#Section "Extensions"
 #       Option  "Composite" "Enable"
#EndSection
```

---

### Post by wishes on 2006-08-07
> **wishes said:**
> I have the same video card as the demo, have copy/pasted exactly and still cant seem to get the laptop screen showing up. I have an external 19" LCD. Ive currently set it up for 1024x768 as I know the laptop will do this however the laptop screen just blanks and the external screen works fine.

This is my config 
<snip>


Found my problem,
    Option "DDC" "Off"
in my Main Monitor (DDC was auto detection of screen)

Now i have clone view only (even when its turned off).

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-07

Uh...did you get my Private Message? Well, since we have the same card, this xorg.conf file works for me w/ MergedFB:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
	Driver		"radeon"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Option 		"TVOutput" 	"NTSC"

#Option "DDCMode"
#Option "RenderAccel" "true"

Option "MergedFB" "True"
Option "CRT2HSync" "31.5 - 60.0"
Option "CRT2VRefresh" "60-85"
Option "MonitorLayout" "LCD,CRT" #use LCD,CRT even if you have 2 CRTs
Option "OverlayOnCRTC2" "true"
Option "MetaModes" "1024x768-1024x768"
Option "CRT2Position" "RightOf"

### Post by Lorian on 2006-08-08
I have an nVidia card and am running it with TwinView.

```
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Driver		"nvidia"

	Option		"TwinView"	"true"
	Option		"UseEdidFreqs"	"true"

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-08
> **Lorian said:**
> I have an nVidia card and am running it with TwinView.

```
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Option		"NoLogo"
	Option		"TwinView"	"true"
	Option		"UseEdidFreqs"	"true"
	Option		"MetaModes"	"1024x768,1024x768;800x600,800x600;640x480,640x480"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
EndSection
```

I don't think it can be done w/ TwinView (or MergedFB) because X sees the two monitors as one "screen", therefore, the "screen" has to be rectangular.  Xinerama is the only one that allows two different resolution for two monitors, but it still works better with same resolutions.  

Sorry, I couldn't be of more help.
