### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-08
> **Lorian said:**
> Hm, how would I go about using Xinerama then?

haha...that i need to research a bit...I have never used Xinerama before, cuz my card doesn't work with Xinerama.

For now, just generate a new Xorg.conf file, and then follow my Xinerama HowTo.

EDIT:

But I would assume to change the Modes underneath the "Screen" Sections:

here's someone else's Xorg.conf file, model it after this:

```
Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Screen0"
        Device     "Card0"
        Monitor    "Monitor0"
        DefaultColorDepth 24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     24
                [B]Modes       "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1152x864"
                Modes       "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"[/B]
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Screen1"
        Device     "Card1"
        Monitor    "Monitor1"
        DefaultColorDepth 24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     24
                [B]Modes       "2048x1536" "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1152x864"
                Modes       "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"[/B]
        EndSubSection
EndSection
```

I believe that the bolded lines are what makes it work.

```

How is your computer configured? I don't have any experience with multiple graphic cards, but are you connect one monitor to each card? Or multiple monitors to each card?

A second question is: Did you use TwinView?  Because, from my understanding, TwinView has to be configure in a "box" style, meaning that you have to have screens of the same resolution. But this may be different with multiple cards...

But after you have used the HowTo, can you post your xorg.conf file? Because, I'm more of a visual person...:confused: 

> (WW) NVIDIA: No matching Device section for instance (BusID PCI:2:0:0) found

This line is telling you that you are either missing a Device Section or that you have misspelled the identifier...

Now after viewing this line, it seems like you have Xinerama setup, but I can't be sure unless I can see the Xorg.conf file.

So cyould you post it? Thanks.  I'll do my best to solve this...

---

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"

EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	# Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA GeForce 6600 1"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	  Screen 0
	Option 		"TwinView" "True"
	Option		"TwinViewOrintation" "RightOf"
	Option		"UseEdidFreqs" "True"
	Option		"MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1204x768,1204x768"	  
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA GeForce 6600 2"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
	  Screen 1
	Option 		"TwinView" "True"
	Option		"TwinViewOrintation" "RightOf"
	Option		"UseEdidFreqs" "True"
	Option		"MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1204x768,1204x768"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	24-60
	VertRefresh	60-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	24-60
	VertRefresh	60-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA GeForce 6600 1"
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA GeForce 6600 2"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Main Screen"
	 Screen		  1 "Second Screen" RightOf "MainScreen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option		"Xinerama" "Off"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

From my quick scanning it seems that it is failing to see the second monitor, could this be due to using two cards??

it would be nice to use the 2 cards but can go to one if gets this working.

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-11
here's what I'm understanding from your xorg.conf file...you are trying to use TwinView, however, TwinView is for two monitors on one card.  Meaning that TwinView is trying find the "other monitor" on the **same** card, so my guess is that's where the error is coming from. I think this **should** work:

comment (#) out anything that's related to TwinView, (or anything that's below the "screen 0/1" lines.

Then add this section to the end of the file:

Section "ServerFlags"
   Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

also remove the "0" and "1" in the lines:

Screen		0 "Main Screen"
Screen		  1 "Second Screen" RightOf "MainScreen"

As I said before, this **should** work.  However, I am uncertain, since I have no way of testhing this method, nor do I own any nVidia cards :( .  If this doesn't work, then I would suggest trying TwinView by itself (using one card).

That leads to my question, how many PCI/VGA outputs does one of your nVidia have?

---

### Post by Paul_G on 2006-08-12
thanks I'll give that a try.

The outputs on the cards are a VGA and a DVI with a TV-OUT

---

### Post by Paul_G on 2006-08-12
Ziox

Thanks for al your help, it has been very much appreciated in this area.

I tried commenting out what you suggested but that failed to do anything, so using the config file I posted up I put the two monitors on the one card and now it works fine!
Anyone want a 256mb nVivdia PX6600LE PCI-E graphics card, VGA,DVI,TV-OUT?

thanks again.

I now have a complete resolution of 2048x768, was wondering if it is possible on Linux to do what you can on the Mac G5 and have the monitors set up so that you can have different wallpapers on each??
or would the simpliest thing be to desigin a wallpaper at the resolution of
 the two monitors??

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-12
> **Paul_G said:**
> Ziox

Thanks for al your help, it has been very much appreciated in this area.

I tried commenting out what you suggested but that failed to do anything, so using the config file I posted up I put the two monitors on the one card and now it works fine!
Anyone want a 256mb nVivdia PX6600LE PCI-E graphics card, VGA,DVI,TV-OUT?

thanks again.

I now have a complete resolution of 2048x768, was wondering if it is possible on Linux to do what you can on the Mac G5 and have the monitors set up so that you can have different wallpapers on each??
or would the simpliest thing be to desigin a wallpaper at the resolution of
 the two monitors??

Don't just throw your card away yet!!! ;)  If you feel like experimenting, you can combine the methods of Xinerama and TwinView together, so that'll be 4 monitors :D .

As for the different wallpaper, first check to see under changing wallpaper, whether there is an option for individual wallpapers.  If not, then whip out GIMP, and combine two wallpapers with the resolution of 1024x768 each.  

I personally like how XFCE handles dual screen better.

---

### Post by Paul_G on 2006-08-12
yeah here u go

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	# Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA GeForce 6600 1"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	  Screen 0
	Option 		"TwinView" "True"
	Option		"TwinViewOrintation" "RightOf"
	Option		"UseEdidFreqs" "True"
	Option		"MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1204x768,1204x768"	  
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA GeForce 6600 2"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
	  Screen 1
	Option 		"TwinView" "True"
	Option		"TwinViewOrintation" "RightOf"
	Option		"UseEdidFreqs" "True"
	Option		"MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1204x768,1204x768"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	24-60
	VertRefresh	60-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	24-60
	VertRefresh	60-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA GeForce 6600 1"
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA GeForce 6600 2"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Main Screen"
	 Screen		  1 "Second Screen" RightOf "MainScreen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option		"Xinerama" "Off"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Found out that the problem is more with compiz than xgl.

---

Editing the Xorg.conf file shouldn't delete anything on menus or anything like that, perhaps something else has deleted it...

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-14
> **KlausU said:**
> Hi, I installed Xinerama as described and I have the problem that always the wrong monitor is chosen as primary screen (login, bars, etc) Montior. I have tried swapping everything in the xorg.conf and even switching the connectors once installed does not help.

How can I toggle the primary monitors?

Best Regards

here's how I understand Xinerama...the Line(s) "Screen 0" means the primary screen, and "Screen 1" means the secondary screen, so..try switching that in your xorg.conf file.

It's best if you post your xorg.conf file...

thanks :p

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
#	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"de"
	Option		"XkbVariant"	"nodeadkeys"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 NVIDIA GeForce 7800 GT"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		1
        Option          "RenderAccel"           "true"
        Option          "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 NVIDIA GeForce 7800 GT"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		0
        Option          "RenderAccel"           "true"
        Option          "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
EndSection


Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"TFT"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-50
	VertRefresh	43-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"CRT"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-50
	VertRefresh	43-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"0 NVIDIA GeForce 7800 GT"
	Monitor		"TFT"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8

	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
#		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"1 NVIDIA GeForce 7800 GT"
	Monitor		"CRT"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection

	EndSubSection

		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
#		Modes		"800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	"Xinerama"	"true"
EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Main Screen"
	Screen		"Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15

EndSection

	Monitor    "SyncMaster CRT"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1

**MergedFB**

I also tried MergedFB. Like in Xinerama i also used the drivers 'fglrx', 'ati' and 'radeon'. The result was that both screens worked, but they display the same. One third of the screen (at the bottom) showed a scrambeled screen. The other two third was one big screen that slided to the side when i moved my mouse to the right. So got a big desktop, but it al was still on one screen (both screens display the same).

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-16
> **Bultot said:**
> So am I right that with my ATI 9800XT I can't have Xinerama with 3d(fglrx) support?

If that's so, I want to sell my videocard en buy a new one. Which one is recommended for having Xinerama (2 Monitors, 1 TV) with 3d support?

I'd like to run XGL with Xinerama...

So you are planning on running Xinerama with 2 monitors + 1 TV? Wow, I never tried it with TV outputs, so I have absolutely no idea how to make it work.

However, yes I believe that you are correct.  Xinerama only support 3d acceleration on one screen :mad: :( .  However, you can try cleverly combining MergedFB with Xinerama, thus enabling 3d acceleration on the two monitors and regular display on the TV...But buy a new card would not help you with 3d support. Since Xinerama is a function built into Xorg, it is more of a software than a hardware...

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-16
> **miketech said:**
> Hi,

maybe you can help me. I have a ATI Radeon 9100 IGP Card, with DVI and VGA. I have two monitors, one with 1024x768 (VGA) and another with 1280x1024 (DVI). Now I wanna have Xinerama, but I don't get it to work.

Here is my xorg.conf:

[http://www.ubuntuusers.de/paste/2871/](http://www.ubuntuusers.de/paste/2871/)

Both screens are blank. Here is my xorg.log:

[http://www.ubuntuusers.de/paste/2872/](http://www.ubuntuusers.de/paste/2872/)


Do you have any ideas? 

Greetings

Mike

I noticed that you have Option "Xinerama" "true" in the ServerLayout Section.

I don't think you need that there...

Other than that I don't see any problem with you Xorg.conf file, but you just might want to double check again (for spelling, especially Identifiers), because I'm only human ;) 

As for the Xorg.0.log file, there aren't any errors that I can see that would affect your how your display goes, however these two lines got my attention:

```
1121	(II) Screen 0 shares mem & io resources
1122	(II) Screen 1 shares mem & io resources
```

This is just speculation, but perhaps there aren't enough mem allocated for the card.  Try increasing the mem from you bios...

Other than that I can't think of any other way to solve your problem, but feel free to post back.  I love challenges :p 

PS What does the two monitors display before and after you edit xorg?

Also, you can try letting xorg to automatically configure xorg for you, just plug in your second monitor, and the execute this command:

```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

make sure you backup first...

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-16
> **mcewanbr said:**
> I have been trying to get any of these posted methods to work with no such luck.  I have a Dell Lattitude D620 that uses the Nvidia card.  When I have it on my Advanced Port Replicator, I have two video out's, one DVI, one VGA.

The weird thing is that if I use the "nvidia" driver, it sends video out the VGA port.  If I use the "nv" driver, it sends it through the DVI port.  I have tried using one of each in the device section in many configurations, no luck.

Any suggestions?

Can you post your xorg.conf file? and also the xorg.0.log file?

```
gedit (or kate or mousepad) /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

and

```
gedit /var/log/Xorg.0.log
```

```


**MergedFB**

I also tried MergedFB. Like in Xinerama i also used the drivers 'fglrx', 'ati' and 'radeon'. The result was that both screens worked, but they display the same. One third of the screen (at the bottom) showed a scrambeled screen. The other two third was one big screen that slided to the side when i moved my mouse to the right. So got a big desktop, but it al was still on one screen (both screens display the same).

This is my xorg.conf when i tried MergedFB:

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"

	# path to defoma fonts

EndSection

	Load  "bitmap"

	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"

	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "SyncMaster"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 81.0
	VertRefresh  56.0 - 75.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R350 NH [Radeon 9800 Pro]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Option 		"MergedFB" "true" 
	Option 		"MonitorLayout" "LCD, CRT" # Use LCD and CRT even if you have 2 LCD's or CRT's 
	Option 		"CRT2Hsync" "30-65" #Horizontal Sync of the Monitor 
	Option 		"CRT2VRefresh" "50-75" #Vertical Refresh rate of the Monitor 
	Option 		"OverlayOnCRTC2" "true" 
	Option 		"CRT2Position" "LeftOf" 
	Option 		"MetaModes" "1280x1024-1280x1024"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R350 NH [Radeon 9800 Pro]"
	Monitor    "SyncMaster"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Virtual  2048 768
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Virtual  2048 768
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Virtual  2048 768
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Virtual  2048 768
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Virtual  2048 768
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
		Virtual  2048 768
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

```

-- edit --

Dual screen works in Windows, so i don't have to wonder if my videocard is capable of support two screens.

Xinerama:

i think you should remove (or hash # out) the horizsync and vertrefresh lines. Then reboot and try again, if it still doesn't work, post your xorg.conf file (if you had edited further) and your Xorg.0.log file:

```
gedit /var/log/Xorg.0.log
```

as for MergedFB:

from what I can see (I'm assuming that your monitors resolutions are 1280x1024), you should change this line:

Virtual 2048 768

to

Virtual 2560 2048

then reboot and see if it works [-o< 

if not, then post Xorg.0.log file again...

---

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log)

```
(WW) fglrx(0): Option "MergedFB" is not used
(WW) fglrx(0): Option "CRT2Hsync" is not used

(WW) fglrx(0): Option "CRT2Position" is not used

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-16
> **KenSentMe said:**
> [QUOTE=Ziox;1385799]

I've tried MergedFB. I've replaced all the Virtual 2048 768 with Virtual 2560 2048, and rebooted. Now i have an overstretched desktop on two screens. Both screens display the same.

This is my new Xorg.0.log:

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log)

I notice these lines:

```
(WW) fglrx(0): Option "MergedFB" is not used
(WW) fglrx(0): Option "CRT2Hsync" is not used
(WW) fglrx(0): Option "CRT2VRefresh" is not used
(WW) fglrx(0): Option "CRT2Position" is not used
(WW) fglrx(0): Option "MetaModes" is not used
```

Why is that?

it appears that mergedFB isn't supported by fglrx...because from those lines, fglrx doesn't recognize those options...that's not good...

I have a question, the drivers provided by ATI, are they fglrx?

### Post by KenSentMe on 2006-08-16
> **Ziox said:**
> [QUOTE=KenSentMe;1385857]

it appears that mergedFB isn't supported by fglrx...because from those lines, fglrx doesn't recognize those options...that's not good...

Don't know really. 

I installed my graphics card using the BinaryDriverHowto. The first method on this page:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI#head-5ead174a0b3294527486cd4d71ded66b40003f25](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI#head-5ead174a0b3294527486cd4d71ded66b40003f25)

When i look at the installation of the real ATI drivers i notice this:

```
sudo module-assistant build,install fglrx-kernel
```

> [QUOTE=Ziox;1385871]

Don't know really. 

I installed my graphics card using the BinaryDriverHowto. The first method on this page:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI#head-5ead174a0b3294527486cd4d71ded66b40003f25](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI#head-5ead174a0b3294527486cd4d71ded66b40003f25)

When i look at the installation of the real ATI drivers i notice this:

```
sudo module-assistant build,install fglrx-kernel
```

I suggest you try the ati driver provided by ATI, considering ATI want customers, their driver should support MergedFB...

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-16
For get my previous post, I just discovered that MergedFB is only supported by open source drivers, however, "Big Desktop" is supported by the binary drivers, I'm going to see if I can add that to my HowTo...Stay Tune :)

---

### Post by KenSentMe on 2006-08-16
> **Ziox said:**
> [QUOTE=KenSentMe;1385897]

I suggest you try the ati driver provided by ATI, considering ATI want customers, their driver should support MergedFB...

Tried the ATI drivers, but they use fglrx too, so no MergedFB. So it looks like dual screen with one 3D acc will not work :(

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-16
HowTo Updated including Big Desktop for binary ATI drivers.  fglrx driver doesn't work for my card, so that I can't be too much of a help.  But I would greatly appreciate if for those who try Big Desktop to post any problems here...

### Post by KenSentMe on 2006-08-16
> **Ziox said:**
> HowTo Updated including Big Desktop for binary ATI drivers.  fglrx driver doesn't work for my card, so that I can't be too much of a help.  But I would greatly appreciate if for those who try Big Desktop to post any problems here...

Thanks :)

I've tried that one, but it doesn't work completely for me. I have dual screen and i can slide windows from one screen to another etc. The problem is that the display is all messed up. All windows seem scrambled and when i move an icon it leaves a trace on my desktop. When i open e.g. a website i can't see the contents, but when select the whole text with my mouse i can read the text. This is hard to explain how it looks, but it doesn't look good.

Here is my xorg.conf

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/xorg.conf](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/xorg.conf)

and my Xorg.0.log

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log)

I feel like i'm one step before i'm there.

Also, what is a bit confusing in the Howto is when you run the 'Big Desktop' setup. Is that after you've done the MergedFB Howto (you mention MergedFB in the Big Desktop howto), or is it standalone?

Oh, and i couldn't do the aticonfig part because the option '--iagp=off' doesn't work. I get an error when i use it, something like 'unknown parameter'. That's why i did the config manually as in the howto.

### Post by mcewanbr on 2006-08-16
> **Ziox said:**
> Can you post your xorg.conf file? and also the xorg.0.log file?

```
gedit (or kate or mousepad) /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

and

```
gedit /var/log/Xorg.0.log
```

Here are my current ones.  Please note that the xorg.conf is a fresh one from using nvidia-xconfig plus some small mods.

Thanks for your help!

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon May 15 13:23:42 PDT 2006

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0"
    Screen      1  "Screen1" leftOf "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

#Section "Files"

EndSection

    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"

EndSection

    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-17
> **KenSentMe said:**
> I've tried that one, but it doesn't work completely for me. I have dual screen and i can slide windows from one screen to another etc. The problem is that the display is all messed up. All windows seem scrambled and when i move an icon it leaves a trace on my desktop. When i open e.g. a website i can't see the contents, but when select the whole text with my mouse i can read the text. This is hard to explain how it looks, but it doesn't look good.

Here is my xorg.conf

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/xorg.conf](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/xorg.conf)

and my Xorg.0.log

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log)

I feel like i'm one step before i'm there.

Also, what is a bit confusing in the Howto is when you run the 'Big Desktop' setup. Is that after you've done the MergedFB Howto (you mention MergedFB in the Big Desktop howto), or is it standalone?

Oh, and i couldn't do the aticonfig part because the option '--iagp=off' doesn't work. I get an error when i use it, something like 'unknown parameter'. That's why i did the config manually as in the howto.

As I said before, I can't personally test any of these codes, so I don't know why '--iagp=off -v' doesn't work.  But I've searched a little bit, and see if this helps your Big Desktop:

Change **MonitorLayout** to **DesktopSetup**

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-17
> **mcewanbr said:**
> Here are my current ones.  Please note that the xorg.conf is a fresh one from using nvidia-xconfig plus some small mods.

Thanks for your help!

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon May 15 13:23:42 PDT 2006

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0"
    Screen      1  "Screen1" leftOf "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

#Section "Files"
#    RgbPath         "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
#EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 150.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor1"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 150.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Option         "RenderAccel" "true"
    Option         "NvAGP"        "2"
    Screen         0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
#    BusID         "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen         1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
#        Modes      "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Device1"
    Monitor        "Monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
Option "Xinerama" "True"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
Mode    0666
EndSection

I think you should try TwinView with a fresh Xorg file:

sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

As for your current xorg file, I do not know why nVidia chose to use Xinerama, when TwinView works so much better. Also, you have to remove that hash mark from you this line:

#    BusID         "PCI:1:0:0"

A down side of TwinView is that both monitors have to use the same resolution :(

As for your Xorg log file, I don't see any problems...

### Post by KlausU on 2006-08-17
YIPEE! I found it. Its quite obvious, simple, documented but hard to find.

[http://download.nvidia.com/solaris/1.0-8762/README/appendix-d.html](http://download.nvidia.com/solaris/1.0-8762/README/appendix-d.html)

> 
Option "UseDisplayDevice" "string" 
When assigning display devices to X screens, the NVIDIA X driver by default assigns display devices in the order they are found (looking first at CRTs, then at DFPs, and finally at TVs). This option can be used to override this assignment. For example, if both a CRT and a DFP are connected, you could specify:
    Option "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP"

to make the X screen use the DFP, even though it would have used a CRT by default.
Note the subtle difference between this option and the "ConnectedMonitor" option: the "ConnectedMonitor" option overrides what display devices are actually detected, while the "UseDisplayDevice" option controls which of the detected display devices will be used on this X screen.


It would be good If you could include this in your EXCELLENT Howto!

### Post by KlausU on 2006-08-17
> Xinerama (first HowTo) allows direct rendering on the main monitor only, so if you want DRI to work on both monitors, then please try the second or third HowTo.

I have made the following experience with Xinerama:

```

72461 frames in 5.0 seconds = 14487.837 FPS  <- main monitor
12514 frames in 5.0 seconds = 2502.475 FPS   <- In the middle
60886 frames in 5.0 seconds = 12177.190 FPS
74549 frames in 5.0 seconds = 14909.768 FPS  <- Second monitor

```

Hardware accel seems to work fine for both monitors.

Maybe this helps aswell ;)

Best Regards

### Post by mcewanbr on 2006-08-18

Okay, I did as you said.

1.  Ran sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg.  Below is the xorg.conf it created.
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Dell LCD"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA"
	Monitor		"Dell LCD"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```
This sent video through my DVI port which is my left LCD.

2.  I then followed your instructions on twinview and changed the driver to nvidia.  Below is my xorg.conf for this.
```

This placed my video through the VGA port which is my right LCD.  However the resolution appeared to be what it would be over the two LCD's, except just on the one.  Which means I had panning ability.

3.  I commented out your recommendations, then duplicated what was given on the post you recommended ([http://www.ublug.org/ubuntu/twinview/twinview-howto-breezy.html)](http://www.ublug.org/ubuntu/twinview/twinview-howto-breezy.html)).  Below is the xorg.conf for it.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

####    YOUR WAY AFTER THE sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
##Section "Device"
##	Identifier	"NVIDIA"
##	Driver		"nvidia"
##	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
##	Option "TwinView" "True"
##	Option "TwinViewOrientation" "RightOf"
##	Option "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
##	Option "MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1024x768,1024x768"
##EndSection


Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "NvAGP" "2"
	Option "NoLogo" "1"
	Option "RenderAccel" "0"
	Option "CursorShadow" "1"
	Option "Coolbits" "1"
	Option "ConnectedMonitor" "dfp, dfp"
	Option "NoPowerConnectorCheck"
	Option "TwinView" "1"
	Option "Metamodes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1280x960,1280x960; 1152x864,1152x864; 800x600,800x600; 1024x768,1024x768; 1280x1024,NULL; 1024x768,NULL"
	Option "SecondMonitorHorizSync" "31-82"
	Option "SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "56-76"
	Option "TwinView" "True"
	Option "TwinViewOrientation" "RightOf"
	Option "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
	Option "MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1024x768,1024x768"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Dell LCD"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA"
	Monitor		"Dell LCD"
	DefaultDepth 24
	SubSection "Display"
	Viewport 0 0
	Depth 24
	Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
	Option "Xinerama" "Off"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Now it is pushing video back through the DVI port (left LCD) and same panning issue.

Any thoughts?

	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Dell LCD"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA"
	Monitor		"Dell LCD"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```
This sent video through my DVI port which is my left LCD.

2.  I then followed your instructions on twinview and changed the driver to nvidia.  Below is my xorg.conf for this.

		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024"

EndSection

	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

	Device		"NVIDIA"
	Monitor		"Dell LCD"
	DefaultDepth 24
	SubSection "Display"
	Viewport 0 0
	Depth 24
	Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
	Option "Xinerama" "Off"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Now it is pushing video back through the DVI port (left LCD) and same panning issue.

Any thoughts?

yeah, just a thought, found this information:

> NOTE: anything attached to a 15 pin VGA connector is regarded by the driver as a CRT. "DFP" should only be used to refer to digital flat panels connected via a DVI port.

so change one of them to CRT:

```
Option "ConnectedMonitor" "dfp, dfp"
```

so change one of them to CRT:

```


Okay, so I changed it to read...

```
Option "ConnectedMonitor" "dfp, crt"
```

and

```
Option "ConnectedMonitor" "crt, dfp"
```

Both of which sent everything through VGA (right lcd) with panning.

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-18
try adding this this:

> Option "UseDisplayDevice" "string" 
When assigning display devices to X screens, the NVIDIA X driver by default assigns display devices in the order they are found (looking first at CRTs, then at DFPs, and finally at TVs). This option can be used to override this assignment. For example, if both a CRT and a DFP are connected, you could specify:
Option "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP"

to make the X screen use the DFP, even though it would have used a CRT by default.
Note the subtle difference between this option and the "ConnectedMonitor" option: the "ConnectedMonitor" option overrides what display devices are actually detected, while the "UseDisplayDevice" option controls which of the detected display devices will be used on this X screen.

### Post by m*i*l*e*s on 2006-08-20
OK this did not work as is for me and I was using the following setup:
Stock Dapper with Nvidia drivers installed and all working OK
Graphics card: Geforce Ti 4200
One 19" TFT Neovo F-419 on the DVI (H 24-80)(V 50-75) *save you looking it up*
One 15" TFT Samsung L1515s on the VGA (H 31-63)(V 56-75)
After some messing about I ended up with this xorg.conf that works great for me, as follows: 15" is the main desktop on my right, 19" on my left gets the apps open on it. Dragging windows between screen works fine. All in all I am very happy with this setup.
So here's my xorg.conf in case you happen to have the same setup:-

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section	"Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NV28 [GeForce4 Ti 4200 AGP 8x]"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"NvAgp" "2"
	Option		"RenderAccel" "1"
	Option		"TwinView" "1"
	Option		"TwinViewOrientation" "LeftOf"
	Option		"SecondMonitorHorizSync" "31-63"
	Option		"SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "56-75"
	Option		"MetaModes" "1024x768,1024x768; 1024x768,1024x768"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"F-419"
	Option		"Horizsync" "24-80"
	Option		"VertRefresh" "50-75"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NV28 [GeForce4 Ti 4200 AGP 8x]"
	Monitor		"F-419"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
	Viewport 0 0
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"TwinView Configuration"
	Screen 0	"Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

there are new ATI drivers (8.28.8). Now with a new mode: Pairmode.

Here you can have several desktops with different resolutions. Maybe this is helpful for some of you.

Greetings

Mike

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-21
> **KenSentMe said:**
> Okay, these pictures to see what i mean:

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/ds_startup.jpg](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/ds_startup.jpg)

This is my startup screens when i run MergedFB. Notice the right screen.

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/messedup.jpg](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/messedup.jpg)

This is what i get when i try to move a terminal window to the second desktop.

Further, here are my xorg.conf and Xorg.0.log:

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/xorg.conf](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/xorg.conf)
[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/Xorg.0.log)

In the log there is this line:

```

(WW) fglrx: No matching Device section for instance (BusID PCI:1:0:1) found

```

Should i fix that, and how?

Perhaps it has something to do with the refreshrates and stuff, try adding these two lines to your "Device" Section:

```
Option "HSync2" "65" - This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
Option "VRefresh2" "60" - This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
```

change the values according to your user manual

and comment out the "virtual mode" lines...

---

### Post by KenSentMe on 2006-08-21
> **Ziox said:**
> Perhaps it has something to do with the refreshrates and stuff, try adding these two lines to your "Device" Section:

```
Option "HSync2" "65" - This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
Option "VRefresh2" "60" - This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
```

change the values according to your user manual

and comment out the "virtual mode" lines...

Great! It works now and i even have 3d support. I don't know if i can run 3d games on both screens. I also don't know what made it work now. I've uncommented the virtual lines and added the both options. I'll try to figure out what made the messed up screens go away.

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-21
> **KenSentMe said:**
> Great! It works now and i even have 3d support. I don't know if i can run 3d games on both screens. I also don't know what made it work now. I've uncommented the virtual lines and added the both options. I'll try to figure out what made the messed up screens go away.

I think your monitor was messed up because either the horizontal or vertical refresh rates were too high or too low, so the two options are to override what ATI has automatically detected...

btw, those pictures really helped, I'm going to add that "trouble shoot" method to my HowTo...

### Post by fpwind on 2006-08-22
I was able to get the large desktop up and going that spans my two 1280x1028 lcd displays.  WOOHOO!
I followed the directions in this thread for the merged display.  I have a Radeon 9800 pro.  I'm having an annoyance though.  Some programs when maximized span both monitors (Picasa is one) which makes using it very irritating.  But the show stopper is that all my videos are playing back super wide in totem.  My wallpaper is stretched across two screens.  I read in on of the previous posts how to take care of that but I need help with the video play back.  I know it's becaus the xine backend is seeing the giant 2560x1024 resolution.  I've been messing around with my xorg.conf for almost a month.  I really like the having the big desktop and 3d acceleration working but what alternative do I have to get my video to play back correctly.  As a side note all xine based video players exhibit the same WIDE aspect.
Here is my xorg.conf

Thanks a million!
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "SyncMaster"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 65.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 75.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R350 NH [Radeon 9800 Pro]"
	Driver      "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:2:0:0"
	Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal"
	Option "Mode2"         "1280x1024"
	Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO"
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" 
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal"
	Option "Mode2"         "1280x1024"
	Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO"
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" 
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R350 NH [Radeon 9800 Pro]"
	Monitor    "SyncMaster"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24	
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSectio
```n

```
Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24	
        EndSubSection
EndSection
```

I not sure if "Viewport" line is the cause of it, I would change it to something like this:

```
Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
            Modes "1280x1024"
		Depth     24	
            Virtual 2560 1024
        EndSubSection
EndSection
```

And I would suggest to remove all duplicate sections that aren't used...

### Post by miketech on 2006-08-25
Hi again,

I am currently trying to get the newest fglrx driver to work. 

My current problem is: Since the newest version xorg can't find both monitors plugged in. I have a VGA and DVI port. Only the VGA port is detected.

Then I set EnableMonitors and ForceMonitors to crt1,tmds2.

With tmds2 the dvi seems to be activated. But xorg can't detect the monitor. The vendor is "X", the model is "3", very strange. And the next problem is, that X can't recognize the supported resolutions. X says only 640x480, 800x600, 1024x768 is supported. But I wanna have 1280x1024. Any idea how I can change this? I've set the Modeline in the Monitor section. But X doesn't accept that. I am looking for a way to get X to detect the monitor as in the older versions.

Does anybody have similar problems with the new version? 

Is tmds2 the right port? I get a desktop, but not with the correct resolution. Do you have any ideas, what I could try out?

Greetings

Mike

### Post by miketech on 2006-08-26
> **Ziox said:**
> Also it seems like that SamSung is detected as Display #2, not Philips, so perhaps change your xorg to match with the display?

Hi,

yeah this is a bit strange. On the one hand samsung is detected as #2, but later this is changed to:

(II) fglrx(0): Primary Controller - DFP on external TMDS
(II) fglrx(0): Secondary Controller - CRT on primary DAC

Here it is turned around. This is the reason why I had to set HSync2 and VRefresh2 to the Philips settings, instead to the samsung settings. 

How should I change this? With EnableMonitor and ForceMonitors?

Instead "crt1,tmds2" "tmds2,crt1"?


In earlier versions both monitors were detected correctly. 


```

(II) fglrx(0): Connected Display2: DFP on external TMDS
(II) fglrx(0): Display2 EDID data ---------------------------
(II) fglrx(0): Manufacturer: XXX  Model: 3  Serial#: 0
(II) fglrx(0): Year: 1990  Week: 0
(II) fglrx(0): EDID Version: 1.3
(II) fglrx(0): Analog Display Input,  Input Voltage Level: 0.700/0.300 V
(II) fglrx(0): Sync:Serration on. V.Sync Pulse req. if CompSync or SyncOnGreen
(II) fglrx(0): Max H-Image Size [cm]: horiz.: 40  vert.: 30
(II) fglrx(0): Gamma: 1.00
(II) fglrx(0): DPMS capabilities: StandBy Suspend Off; Non RGB Multicolor Display
(II) fglrx(0): First detailed timing not preferred mode in violation of standard!(II) fglrx(0): redX: 0.000 redY: 0.000   greenX: 0.000 greenY: 0.000
(II) fglrx(0): blueX: 0.000 blueY: 0.000   whiteX: 0.000 whiteY: 0.000
(II) fglrx(0): Supported VESA Video Modes:
(II) fglrx(0): 640x480@60Hz
(II) fglrx(0): 800x600@60Hz
(II) fglrx(0): 1024x768@60Hz
(II) fglrx(0): Manufacturer's mask: 0
(II) fglrx(0): Supported Future Video Modes:
(II) fglrx(0): #0: hsize: 640  vsize 480  refresh: 60  vid: 16433
(II) fglrx(0): #1: hsize: 800  vsize 600  refresh: 60  vid: 16453
(II) fglrx(0): #2: hsize: 1024  vsize 768  refresh: 60  vid: 1
```

(II) fglrx(0): Manufacturer: XXX  Model: 3  Serial#: 0

In earlier versions of fglrx the Model and Serial# were correct.
```

(II) fglrx(0): 640x480@60Hz
(II) fglrx(0): 800x600@60Hz
(II) fglrx(0): 1024x768@60Hz
```

The monitor also supports 1280x1024. This is not listed here. 


And the pairmode settings: I guess, that the problem is, that the monitor doesn't support 1280x1024 at all for X. First I had to get the monitor working with 1280x1024. Maybe I can set pairmode then.

```
(EE) fglrx(0): The new pair mode should not have size bigger than 2048x768. Please try new setting.
```

I wanna have 1280x1024 on the samsung and 1024x768 on the philips monitor. So I have to set it to 1280x1024+1024x768 or 1024x768+1280x1024. Don't know, what is the correct one. But in both cases this is bigger than 2048x768. So I guess X first needs to have the correct possible resolutions for the samsung monitor.


Greetings

Mike

	Screen 0
	Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop

		Depth 24
		Modes "1280x1024"
		Virtual 2560 1024
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen "Dual Monitors"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

also when i run

```
aticonfig --dtop=horizontal
```

i get:

```
Warning: Option 'DesktopSetup' doesn't affect running session.
Using /etc/X11/xorg.conf
Saved back-up to /etc/X11/xorg.conf.fglrx-2
aticonfig: Writing to '/etc/X11/xorg.conf' failed. Bad file descriptor.
```


If a copy of my functional xorg.conf (which runs 2 monitors but doesnt allow cross-monitor window dragging) would help, let me know and Ill post it.

Thanks guys.

Tim

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

# package.
#

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"

	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"

EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 NVIDIA Corporation NV18 [GeForce4 MX 4000 AGP 8x]"

	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		0
	Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS, TMDS"
EndSection

	Identifier	"Default Layout"

	Screen		"Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"

	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"

	Mode	0666

    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
    Mode    0666
EndSection

```

What is your problem?  I'm not psychic :redface:.  However from your xorg.conf file, this line bothers me:
```

Section "ServerFlags"  Option "Xinerama" "true" EndSection
```

change it to

```
 Section "ServerFlags"  
Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection
```

aka, make it into three lines...

Don't know what to do with the last problem, but as for your screen, make sure that these:

Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
    Option "VRefresh2" "75" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.

values are correct for the monitor...look in your owner's manual to see what is the best values.

also if you can, upload a image of how your screens/monitors look, either with a actual camera or the screen capture tool (usually the key Print Screen)

### Post by Ziox on 2006-08-31
> **Webspot said:**
> ```

andrew@ubuntu-desktop:~$ glxinfo | grep direct
direct rendering: No
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect

``` 
```

andrew@ubuntu-desktop:~$ fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: www.mesa3d.org
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.4.1)

``` 
```

andrew@ubuntu-desktop:~$ glxgears -printfps
Usage:
  -display <displayname>  set the display to run on
  -stereo                 run in stereo mode
  -fullscreen             run in fullscreen mode
  -info                   display OpenGL renderer info

```

first of all, are you using the "ati/radeon" driver? or the fglrx driver?

also, from the outputs you've posted, it appears that fglrx isn't installed correctly, or it isn't working...please refer to this website for the methods on installing ATI binary driver: [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Installation_Guide](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Installation_Guide)

### Post by Porch on 2006-08-31
I found the key. This fixed it. 

Option 		"MonitorLayout" 	"CRT,LFP"

```

II) I810(0): Display Info: CRT: attached: TRUE, present: TRUE, size: (640,400)
(II) I810(0): Display Info: TV: attached: FALSE, present: TRUE, size: (1024,768)
(II) I810(0): Display Info: DFP (digital flat panel): attached: FALSE, present: FALSE, size: (0,2315)
(II) I810(0): Display Info: LFP (local flat panel): attached: TRUE, present: TRUE, size: (1280,800)
(II) I810(0): Display Info: CRT2 (second CRT): attached: FALSE, present: FALSE, size: (0,2315)
(II) I810(0): Display Info: TV2 (second TV): attached: FALSE, present: FALSE, size: (0,2315)
(II) I810(0): Size of device LFP (local flat panel) is 1280 x 800
(II) I810(0): Currently active displays on Pipe A:
(II) I810(0): 	CRT
(II) I810(0): Currently active displays on Pipe B:
(II) I810(0): 	LFP (local flat panel)
(II) I810(0): Lowest common panel size for pipe B is 1280 x 800
(==) I810(0): Primary head is using Pipe B


```

Option 		"MonitorLayout" 	"<pipeA>,<pipeB>"

The X log file told me the built in LCD is located on Pipe B. On normal systems, the primary display is located on Pipe A, but I guess this laptop just has to be different.  The log also says to use LFP for Pipe B.

It also shows CRT as the other option, so I added that to Pipe A. And it workes. I not have two screens. 

I have not tested this, but according to the docs, you can clone a display be using  

Option 		"MonitorLayout" 	"<pipeA>,LFP+CRT"


I noted it also shows that I have TV out, but I don't on this laptop. Maybe they didn't just put the jack in or something.
