### Post by Webspot on 2006-09-02
***edit***

I'd thought i'd edit my post as to remove confusion...

Right... I just had a major breakthrough!!!

All is up and running! I've got a dual screen ati setup with dri!

I stumbled across [this thread]("http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=241163") and my xorg.conf now consists of:

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"device0"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"monitor0"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "screen0"
	Device     "device0"
	Monitor    "monitor0"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Dual Head Layout"
	Screen      0  "screen0" 0 0
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Thanks for all of your help here. You've been great!

### Post by Pawel Holeksa on 2006-09-02
Hi 
I work on Toshiba Satellite L10-119. 
It's equipped with Intel Graphics video card: Intel® 852GM.  
I connected one external CRT monitor to VGA output of my laptop.
I modified my xorg.conf according to this HowTo.
Initially it didn't want to work but finally after modifying 
lines with MonitorLayout option to:
```

  Option          "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"

```
and two lines in ServerLayout section to:
```

  Screen          0 "Main Screen" 0 0
  Screen          1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"

```
it works perfectly.
Thanks a lot for this HOWTO.

---

### Post by kairu0 on 2006-09-02
For a dual-head set up on a i915 chipset with 2 displays running at different resolutions, I found the following howto very helpful:

### Post by gusto5 on 2006-09-07
I'm using the twinview option and it works fine except for one oddity. When i connect to an LCD via DVI, the LCD on my laptop takes place as Main screen, whereas the DVI LCD takes as second screen.

However, when i pump out s-video output, the TV takes Left, and the laptop LCD takes right.

Is it just suppose to be like that? It's weird, because when i, say, make a openoffice presentation, i will display the presentation on the left screen. This works when TV is left, but not when LCD is right.

Any suggestions? \\:D/

### Post by Ziox on 2006-09-07
> **gusto5 said:**
> I'm using the twinview option and it works fine except for one oddity. When i connect to an LCD via DVI, the LCD on my laptop takes place as Main screen, whereas the DVI LCD takes as second screen.

However, when i pump out s-video output, the TV takes Left, and the laptop LCD takes right.

Is it just suppose to be like that? It's weird, because when i, say, make a openoffice presentation, i will display the presentation on the left screen. This works when TV is left, but not when LCD is right.

Any suggestions? \\:D/

I'm guessing that Xorg sees the outputs in this order (from primary to lesser...)

S-Video, laptop LCD, DVI LCD

I'm sure that you can force xorg to pipe screens to specific ones, but for that I need to see your xorg.conf file...

### Post by tsiMental on 2006-09-09
I got dualscreens working using i810 and GMA900 on my laptop using your guide.

Had to change the
Option MonitorLayout "LFP, CRT"
into:
Option MonitorLayout "LFP, LFP+CRT"

Or else it wouldn't even start X.

### Post by DAaaMan64 on 2006-09-09
I was able to get it working, however I think maybe a tip or something should be added if you are trying to set up xinerama-nvidia.  Because you have to have a BusID param in both device sections.  A fresh xorg.conf doesn't always apply a BusID to nvidia cards.

Does that make sense?

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-09-09
> **DAaaMan64 said:**
> I was able to get it working, however I think maybe a tip or something should be added if you are trying to set up xinerama-nvidia.  Because you have to have a BusID param in both device sections.  A fresh xorg.conf doesn't always apply a BusID to nvidia cards.

Does that make sense?

Thanks for the tip.  I've made the assumption that the BusID is always included in the default xorg.conf, apparently not....so I'll add a step in the HowTo.

Thanks Again!!!=D> :biggrin:

#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

#Section "InputDevice"

#  # for USB
#  Identifier "stylus"

#  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
#  option "Type" "stylus"
#  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#  
#  # /dev/input/event
#  # for USB
#  Identifier "eraser"
#  Driver "wacom"
#  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
#  option "Type" "eraser"
#  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#  
#  # /dev/input/event
#  # for USB
#  Identifier "cursor"
#  Driver "wacom"
#  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
#  option "Type" "cursor"
#  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "Monitor"
#  identifier "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
#  vendorname "Plug 'n' Play"
#  modelname "Plug 'n' Play"
#  modeline  "1400x1050@60" 122.6 1400 1488 1640 1880 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
#  gamma 1.0
#EndSection
#  Identifier "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"

Section "ServerLayout"

  #  InputDevice "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
  #  InputDevice "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
  #  InputDevice "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "Screen[0]" 0 0
	Screen      1  "screen1" RightOf "Screen[0]"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

  # path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "dbe"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "v4l"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Monitor[0]"
	VendorName   "Plug 'n' Play"
	ModelName    "Plug 'n' Play"
	Gamma        1
	ModeLine     "1400x1050@60" 122.6 1400 1488 1640 1880 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
 # 
	Identifier   "monitor1"
	VendorName   "Generic"
	ModelName    "640x480 @ 60 Hz"
	HorizSync    31.5 - 31.5
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 61.0
	Gamma        1
	ModeLine     "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	VendorName  "ATI"
	BoardName   "ATI Radeon (fglrx)"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
 # 
	Identifier  "device1"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	VendorName  "ATI"
	BoardName   "ATI Radeon (fglrx)"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen[0]"
	Device     "Device[0]"
	Monitor    "Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Virtual   1400 1050
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1400x1050@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
 # 
	Identifier "screen1"
	Device     "device1"
	Monitor    "monitor1"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection


```

### Post by jis on 2006-09-10
Ziox, I have some critisism for this howto, althought it is the best that I have seen. 

You write that "sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg" will create a whole new Xorg.conf file. The command does not clean the file, but opens a X server configuration program that has poor UI and recuires knowing a lot of your hardware. For example, how do you cancel/quit the setup before it is ready? During install of Xubuntu (Dapper) most of the settings were done without user interaction. I am not sure if I can give as good settings as the installation program. Why should I clean the xorg.conf, and do e.g. keyboard setting again just to configure dual monitor support with Xinerama? Can I use the program or some other utility to change/autodetect only monitor settings, for instance? Do I have to clean xorg.xonf even if I have not changed it after installation?

BTW. I shut down the graphical login as you told in the tips section. When I later ran startx, my desktop used smaller font than before. Even if I had restored the backup of xorg.conf before. Strange. If I rebooted my system, I got the normal sized font back. 

You advice to Probe for the BusID of the Graphics card by "lspci -X | grep". The command outputs
"Usage: grep [OPTION]... PATTERN [FILE]...
Try `grep --help' for more information." 
Is this what you ment?

There is one thing I would like to change in my one monitor configuration: During startup (and during virtual console) the screen is barrel distorted and my monitor (MAG DJ707) displays some hints about preferred graphic resolution and frequency on top of everything else displayed on the screen. This is propably, because I had different monitor connected during installation of Xubuntu.

### Post by Ziox on 2006-09-10
> **jis said:**
> Ziox, I have some critisism for this howto, althought it is the best that I have seen. 

You write that "sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg" will create a whole new Xorg.conf file. The command does not clean the file, but opens a X server configuration program that has poor UI and recuires knowing a lot of your hardware. For example, how do you cancel/quit the setup before it is ready? During install of Xubuntu (Dapper) most of the settings were done without user interaction. I am not sure if I can give as good settings as the installation program. Why should I clean the xorg.conf, and do e.g. keyboard setting again just to configure dual monitor support with Xinerama? Can I use the program or some other utility to change/autodetect only monitor settings, for instance? Do I have to clean xorg.xonf even if I have not changed it after installation?

BTW. I shut down the graphical login as you told in the tips section. When I later ran startx, my desktop used smaller font than before. Even if I had restored the backup of xorg.conf before. Strange. If I rebooted my system, I got the normal sized font back. 

You advice to Probe for the BusID of the Graphics card by "lspci -X | grep". The command outputs
"Usage: grep [OPTION]... PATTERN [FILE]...
Try `grep --help' for more information." 
Is this what you ment?

There is one thing I would like to change in my one monitor configuration: During startup (and during virtual console) the screen is barrel distorted and my monitor (MAG DJ707) displays some hints about preferred graphic resolution and frequency on top of everything else displayed on the screen. This is propably, because I had different monitor connected during installation of Xubuntu.

Thanks for the critisim...I've made couple mistakes on the HowTo (I'm human too, that's why I use Ubuntu ;) ) I'll fix them promptly. The "lspci" command is suppose to be "lspci -X | grep VGA"

As for the "sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg"...it's the only command that I can think of that'll generate a faily correct xorg.conf file...If you know another program...I will glad intergrate that into the HowTo. 

Another reason why I chose "sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg" is because alot of people have tried to edit xorg.conf file using another HowTo, and sometimes, the file gets really messy.  Also, but starting w/ a faily clean xorg.conf makes the howto easier to follow...

As for the startx problem, I have the problem too, and I don't know why...but most people probably don't login via terminal...so...

for your monitor problem, it's probably because of improper horizontal and vertical refresh/sync rates...

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection


```

I believe that DRI is OpenGL's way of using the graphic card to directly render graphics, so if Xinerama diables DRI, then openGL is also disabled...

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-09-10
> **winter_mute said:**
> What should I use to get Xgl running under dual monitors?  I've got a Mobility X1400, laptop monitor running at up to 1680x1050, and LCD running at up to 1440x900.  Using the FGLRX drivers, too, I should add.

Are you using Xinerama or Big Desktop? Because Xinerama disables DRI, which is required by Xgl.  In other words, with Xinerama, you can't use Xgl, not matter what graphic card/driver you are using. 

BigDesktop, on the other hand, should be able to start Xgl without any manual tweaking...

---

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    28.0 - 50.0
	VertRefresh  43.0 - 75.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. ATI Default Card"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	VideoRam    131072
	Option	    "UseFBDev" "true"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support
	Option	    "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. ATI Default Card"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Virtual   2880 900
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection


```

When I try to run it with XGL, the second monitor is a black screen where my mouse cursor turns into a X.

ok...I'm not sure why XGL is doing that...you might want to google something about XGL and Dual Screen together...but the screen with X cursor happens to me too when I'm on the login screen...but after I login, I get both screens working....

that reminds me...it might be that XGL doesn't support Dual Screen setup...also....BigDesktop should have two sessions of gnome, can you post your xorg.conf file?

### Post by winter_mute on 2006-09-10
```
 #/etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	Screen         "aticonfig-Screen[1]" RightOf "Default Screen"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"

as for BigDesktop...you forgot these lines:

```
Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
Option "Mode2"         "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
```

	Option 	    "Mode2"         "1440x900" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option 	    "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO

	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1

	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
	Option 	    "Mode2"         "1440x900" #Resolution for second monitor
	Option 	    "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support
	Option	    "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. ATI Default Card"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Virtual   2880 900
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection


```

you are combining Xinerama and MergedFB together...back up the file:

sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup

then create a new xorg.conf file:

sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

and then follow my BigDesktop Guide in my first post...then if you any more problems...just post...

---

### Post by winter_mute on 2006-09-10
Yeah, I followed the BigDesktop guide, which put me right back where I started.  Laptop screen, with the LCD attached just reflecting the laptop screen.  I figured out the magic line that gets dual monitors working for me, but I still can't drag across or anything, which is rather irritating.  Here's the xorg.conf from what your instructions tell me to do:

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 70.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. ATI Default Card"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	VideoRam    131072
	Option	    "UseFBDev" "true"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	"Mode2" "1440x900"
	Option	"DesktopSetup"	"LVDS,AUTO"
	Option	"EnablePrivateBackZ"	"Yes"
	Option	"HSync2" "65"
	Option	"VRefresh2"	"60"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. ATI Default Card"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1440x900" "1280x800" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Virtual	2880 900
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection


```

The only difference between this and what I have set up to work like I had it before - seperate GNOME sessions, it seems, with no interconnectability, and a few bugs on the secondary monitor's screen, such as the pager not getting the icons in it, is that after the first 'screen blah blah blah' line, I put in:

	Screen	       "aticonfig-Screen[1]" LeftOf "Default Screen"

Which makes it so that the cloning of the laptop screen stops, and sets up the seperate session.

	Screen	       "aticonfig-Screen[1]" LeftOf "Default Screen"

Which makes it so that the cloning of the laptop screen stops, and sets up the seperate session.

to have Xinerama enabled you need to add this line to your ServerLayout section:

[PHP]Option "Xinerama" "true"[/PHP]

Heres my xorg.conf file.  Please let me know what I have to change to get the screens swapped from left to right.  Thanks.

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Dual Monitors"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R200 QL [Radeon 8500 LE]"
	Driver      "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:3:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "Mode2" "1280x1024"
	Option 	    "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO"
	Option	    "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes"
	Option	    "HSync2" "65"
	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R200 QL [Radeon 8500 LE]"
	Monitor    "Dual Monitors"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Virtual   2560 1024
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

	Option	    "DPMS"

	Option	    "DPMS" "true"

	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R200 QL [Radeon 8500 LE]"

	Option	    "VRefresh2" "60"

	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection
```


Try changing that line to something like: "CRT, LVDS"

This is the format for it: "<primary display>, <secondary display>"....

---

I suggest changing the Horizontal and Vertical refresh rates, because that sometimes can make Ubuntu not see the other monitor....

HorizSync       30-65
        VertRefresh     50-75

that's what I would suggest....

### Post by murfman on 2006-09-13
> **yacine said:**

Yacine

Yaccine try removing the Screen flags from the Device Sections and the ServerLayout section.  I had to do this in order to get the second monitor to initialize, before hand I was getting a parse error similar to yours.

---

### Post by yacine on 2006-09-13
> **murfman said:**
> Yaccine try removing the Screen flags from the Device Sections and the ServerLayout section.  I had to do this in order to get the second monitor to initialize, before hand I was getting a parse error similar to yours.

---

### Post by murfman on 2006-09-13
> **yacine said:**
> Hi murfman,
I tried your suggestion, as shown in message # 274. But still the same error message...
Thanks for your help,
Yacine

Just to be certain we are talking about the same thing, I don't think I was very clear in that other post.  Remove the Screen 0 and Screen 1 from both of thedevice sections.  Then in the server layout as opposed to having the section like this

```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Main Screen"
        Screen          1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection
```

Make it look more like this by removing the 0 and 1 from the Screen lines...

```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Main Screen"
        Screen          "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection
```

That is what actually made my second monitor pop while using the dual video cards of varying types.  Hope this is more clear.

---

        Identifier      "0 Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"
#	Screen		0
#        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, LVDS"
EndSection
 
Section "Device"
        Identifier      "1 Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:1"
#        Screen          1
#        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, LVDS"
EndSection
 
Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Main Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       30-65
        VertRefresh     50-75
EndSection
 
Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Second Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       30-65
        VertRefresh     50-75
EndSection
 
Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Main Screen"
        Device          "0 Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
        Monitor         "Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection
 
Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Second Screen"
        Device          "1 Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
        Monitor         "Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"  
	Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection 
 
Section "ServerLayout"
   	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		 "Main Screen"
	Screen		 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
	
Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

---

### Post by blue_chili on 2006-09-14
Hi,

Just wanted to post my results using this. My computer has the following specs:

- iBook G4 12" 800 MHZ
- video card: ATI Radeon 9000

External monitor:
- LG Flatron T710BH

Software:
- Ubuntu Dapper (I originally had Hoary so I upgraded to Breezy, rebooted, and then upgraded to Dapper)

I successfully have my ibook running as the primary monitor in 1024x768 and the external secondary monitor is running in 1280x1024 using Xinerama \\:D/ 

The main thing that didn't work for me was the update saying to use the line:
Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, CRT"

The command /var/log/Xorg.0.log came in VERY handy. This is how I found out the MonitorLayout option was causing issues. I can't remember the exact error, something about it not being a recognised variable or something. It basically halted after the comma after LVDS. So I just didn't put that line in.

I also here other people saying you need to specify the refresh rate for your external monitor. This wasn't needed for my monitor.

Finally, there is a slight typo in the ServerLayout section. The final two lines you add in should be:

Screen 0 "Main Screen" # the example given doesn't show the 0
Screen 1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"

And also to reiterate (as I didn't think it was quite clear in the instructions) that the final section, I THINK it has to be left in there but with the Mode bit commented out, eg:

Section "DRI"
#	Mode	0666
EndSection

Finally, anyone reading this, whatever you do..... BACKUP YOUR XORG.CONF. It was a lifesaver.

A big thanks to all those people out there who have brought Ubuntu such a long way!! Last time I used it on the iBook I couldn't get the monitor working at all (or maybe I'm just wiser now?)

Cheers,
Olivia 


--
Here is my complete xorg.conf:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. M9+ 5C63 [Radeon Mobility 9000 (AGP)]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:0:16:0"
# Not sure what below does but I commented it out anyway
#	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
	Screen		0
#	Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, CRT"#	Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, CRT"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. M9+ 5C63 [Radeon Mobility 9000 (AGP)]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:0:16:0"
	Screen		1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"CRT"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"iBook LCD"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-49
	VertRefresh	43-72
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. M9+ 5C63 [Radeon Mobility 9000 (AGP)]"
	Monitor		"iBook LCD"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. M9+ 5C63 [Radeon Mobility 9000 (AGP)]"
	Monitor		"CRT"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Default Screen"
	Screen		1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
#	Mode	0666
EndSection

	EndSubSection

		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection

		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1024x768"

                Modes           "1024x768"

   	Identifier	"Default Layout"

	Screen		 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
	
Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Sorry it took me this long to reply...I'm wondering why you are commenting out the "Screen" lines? aka "Screen 1" and "Screen 0"...there must be a screen 0 line in order for Xinerama to work...

also you might want to try adding this option to your "Device Section"

[PHP]Option "DDCMode" "true"[/PHP]

another help file would be the xorg log file...after you've reproduced the problem, use this command to check for errors in xorg.log file:

[PHP]cat /var/log/Xorg.0.log | grep EE

cat /var/log/Xorg.0.log | grep WW[/PHP]

### Post by rjg1021 on 2006-09-16
I followed the ATI Merged FB guide. I am using a laptop Dell Inspiron 5100 and a 19 in LCD TV with D-Sub input. I am trying to stretch the laptop to the TV. I followed the guide and made a few additional tweaks (idea from comments), but all I got was a widescreen resolution on both monitors and no sharing of the screen. I'll post my xorg.conf and maybe Ziox or someone else might be able to give me a tip. Thanks a bunch in advance. I'd also do troubleshooting over AIM if that is easier (AIM: rjg1021)

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility M7 LW [Radeon Mobility 9000]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option "MergedFB" "true" 
	Option "MonitorLayout" "LCD, CRT" # Use LCD and CRT even if you have 2 LCD's or CRT's 		Option "CRT2Hsync" "30-65" #Horizontal Sync of the Monitor 
	Option "CRT2VRefresh" "50-75" #Vertical Refresh rate of the Monitor 
	Option "OverlayOnCRTC2" "true" 
	Option "CRT2Position" "RightOf" 
	Option "MetaModes" "1280x1024-1280x1024"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility M7 LW [Radeon Mobility 9000]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
		Virtual 	2048 768
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

### Post by wpollans on 2006-09-18
Sorry if this has already been covered - if it has, I missed it.

I have a dell 700m laptop (1280x800 widescreen) running the current stable ubuntu and I'm trying to use a new Hanns-G 19" widescreen monitor (model JW199) as an external monitor.  The recommended resolution of this monitor is 1440x900, so it should also support the same 1280x800 resolution as the laptop screen.

I have been unable to use the monitor either in clone mode (it only displays the upper left quadrant of the laptopscreen) or with xinerama on (the resolution of the monitor is 1024x768 ).  In either case, I can't get the monitor to work with a resolution of 1280x800.  The discouraging thing, other than not being able to use the monitor, is that it works on (1) a 700m running XP and (2) a mackbook - in both cases, with no trouble and very little 'fiddling around'.

cat /var/log/Xorg.0.log | grep EE

[php](WW) warning, (EE) error, (NI) not implemented, (??) unknown.
(II) Loading extension MIT-SCREEN-SAVER
(EE) I810(0): [dri] DRIScreenInit failed. Disabling DRI.
(EE) xf86OpenSerial: Cannot open device /dev/wacom
(EE) xf86OpenSerial: Cannot open device /dev/wacom
(EE) xf86OpenSerial: Cannot open device /dev/wacom
(EE) xf86OpenSerial: Cannot open device /dev/wacom
(EE) xf86OpenSerial: Cannot open device /dev/wacom
(EE) xf86OpenSerial: Cannot open device /dev/wacom[/php] 
cat /var/log/Xorg.0.log | grep WW
[php](WW) warning, (EE) error, (NI) not implemented, (??) unknown.
(WW) I810(0): Invalid Monitor type specified for Pipe B
(WW) I810(0): config file hsync range 30-65kHz not within DDC hsync ranges.
(WW) I810(0): config file vrefresh range 50-75Hz not within DDC vrefresh ranges.
(WW) module minor version (0) is less than the required minor version (1)
(WW) I810(0): Direct rendering is not supported when Xinerama is enabled
(WW) I810(0): Extended BIOS function 0x5f05 failed.
(WW) I810(0): Option "DDCMode" is not used
[/php] 
By the way... The previous warnings and problems are due to a typo in line 48! It was my fault.

Thanks for help

Yacine

From the errors it seems like i810 driver uses another option for monitor layouts...

after checking out "man i810" I would suggest change the lines to:

Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, DFP (or DFP2, LFP, LFP2, CRT2)"

From man file:

[PHP] Valid monitors are  CRT,  LFP,  DFP,  TV,  CRT2,
              LFP2,  DFP2, TV2 and NONE.[/PHP]

EndSection

Section "Screen"

	DefaultDepth	24

EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"

	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

### Post by wpollans on 2006-09-21
OK, I solved the xinerama part of my problem.  There was no 1440x900 mode in the video bios.  I added it by overriding an existing mode:

   sudo 915resolution 41 1440 900

and made this permanent by sticking it in the 915resolution startup script.

I also added the following to the Monitor section of the ExternalMonitor:
  HorizSync range
  VertRefresh range
  DisplaySize values
  ModeLine - generated by gtf


> **wpollans said:**
> Sorry if this has already been covered - if it has, I missed it.

I have a dell 700m laptop (1280x800 widescreen) running the current stable ubuntu and I'm trying to use a new Hanns-G 19" widescreen monitor (model JW199) as an external monitor.  The recommended resolution of this monitor is 1440x900, so it should also support the same 1280x800 resolution as the laptop screen.

I have been unable to use the monitor either in clone mode (it only displays the upper left quadrant of the laptopscreen) or with xinerama on (the resolution of the monitor is 1024x768 ).  In either case, I can't get the monitor to work with a resolution of 1280x800.  The discouraging thing, other than not being able to use the monitor, is that it works on (1) a 700m running XP and (2) a mackbook - in both cases, with no trouble and very little 'fiddling around'.

I've posted my xorg.conf and X.log.0 files, for both of my clone and xinerama attempts, at [http://meyer-pollans.net/xorg](http://meyer-pollans.net/xorg)

Any help would be appreciated - I've already wasted a good bit of time on this - mainly, because I don't know what I'm doing 8-) 

Thanks

	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Well...this has certainly taken me long enough to reply...try adding this line to your xorg.conf file:
[PHP]
Option "ConnectedMonitor" "CRT, DFP"[/PHP]

or something similar

---

### Post by jedbro on 2006-09-26
I got mine working, on two 1680x1050 LCD's with an ATI X300.

Both normal dual head and the 'big desktop' but bigdesktop I hated because everything is loaded in the middle, so I went back to using 'Xinerama', and can drag and drop between monitors just fine.

Anyhow, here is my config. The main problem is it doesn't seem to like Totem on the right/second monitor.

In theory I'm using the ati binary driver, but I'm not convinced because the tutorial says you can't.

[PHP]
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load    "i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option "Buttons" "7"
 	Option "ButtonMapping" "1 2 3 6 7"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. RV370 5B60 [Radeon X300 (PCIE)]"
#	Driver		"ati"
	Driver      	"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen          0
	Option          "MonitorLayout" "TMDS, TMDS"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. RV370 5B60 [Radeon X300 (PCIE)]"
#	Driver		"ati"
	Driver      	"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen          1
	Option          "MonitorLayout" "TMDS, TMDS"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
#	Option	    	"DPMS"
	Option	    	"DPMS" 		"true"
	HorizSync	30-83
	VertRefresh	56-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
#	Option	    	"DPMS"
	Option	    	"DPMS" 		"true"
	HorizSync	30-83
	VertRefresh	56-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. RV370 5B60 [Radeon X300 (PCIE)]"
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1

	EndSubSection

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. RV370 5B60 [Radeon X300 (PCIE)]"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"

	EndSubSection

		Depth		24
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section 
	"ServerFlags"  Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0                "Main Screen"
	Screen          1   		 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

If anyone sees any issues, or how i could improve it, that would be awesome! Thanks.

nothing seems to be wrong with your xorg.conf file.  However, the problem with BigDesktop maybe caused by Gnome incapability to handle dual monitor correctly.  I suggest you try XFCE4, which is a great desktop environment for dual monitor.  

Also, by uxing Xinerama, you are disabling 3d rendering, so you're desktop won't be as responsive as when you use BigDesktop...but that's all personal preference...

---

		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. R420 JI [Radeon X800PRO]"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"  
	Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Main Screen"
	Screen  	1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection


This is what a screen shot looks like
[http://img135.imageshack.us/img135/1747/screenshothu7.png](http://img135.imageshack.us/img135/1747/screenshothu7.png)
Thanks

### Post by jedbro on 2006-09-26
> **Ziox said:**
> nothing seems to be wrong with your xorg.conf file.  However, the problem with BigDesktop maybe caused by Gnome incapability to handle dual monitor correctly.  I suggest you try XFCE4, which is a great desktop environment for dual monitor.  

Cool. Any down falls to using XFCE4? i.e. Will all my programs work, etc?
Any cool things XFCE4 has that gnome doesn't?

> **Ziox said:**
> 
Also, by uxing Xinerama, you are disabling 3d rendering, so you're desktop won't be as responsive as when you use BigDesktop...but that's all personal preference...
Ah, hmm.. so for BigDesktop or 3d rendering for that matter, do I need to ditch Ximerama and go BigDesktop?

Only problem I had with big desktop is that it really thought it was one huge desktop, so splash screens and applications would always start in the middle of the two monitors, which makes it a bit annoying. 

When Beryl is released, I definitally will want 3d rendering for my desktop (would use compiz now, but I don't like the vanilla, and the mods are all gone from the repos). Should I stick to Big Desktop then or do you think XFCE4 will play well with Beryl?

Sorry for all my noob questions :)

### Post by Ziox on 2006-09-26
> **jedbro said:**
> Cool. Any down falls to using XFCE4? i.e. Will all my programs work, etc?
Any cool things XFCE4 has that gnome doesn't?


Ah, hmm.. so for BigDesktop or 3d rendering for that matter, do I need to ditch Ximerama and go BigDesktop?

Only problem I had with big desktop is that it really thought it was one huge desktop, so splash screens and applications would always start in the middle of the two monitors, which makes it a bit annoying. 

When Beryl is released, I definitally will want 3d rendering for my desktop (would use compiz now, but I don't like the vanilla, and the mods are all gone from the repos). Should I stick to Big Desktop then or do you think XFCE4 will play well with Beryl?

Sorry for all my noob questions :)

XFCE4 has a lighter footprint than Gnome.  Whether you like it or not is your personal opinion.  But for me, I found XFCE4, after using Gnome for 2 months, very easy to use and very customizable.  Your applications should work, because XFCE4 is also based on GTK applications, which Gnome uses as well...give XFCE4 a try, and if you don't like it, just uninstall it.

BigDesktop is basically merging two screens into one, and tricking xorg into thinking that only one screens is present.  I can see how splash screens and applications would start within the middle. I'm sure adding another option in xorg.conf file can cure the problem.

I'm not sure what "Beryl" is, but if BigDesktop bothers you, then use Xinerama +  XFCE4. Again it is about personal opinion and preference.

btw, all people were noobs before :-D

---

### Post by slimb on 2006-09-27
so i've got xinerama working (i wish two separate desktops that i can move between easily instead of one large desktop) sort of.

I've got the laptop screen on the left and a flatpanel on the right.  the laptop screen is fine, the flatpanel screen has a background and i can drag things into it, however my cursor is a large block and i get artifacting and the like.

here's my xorg.conf

```

Section "Files"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # path to defoma fonts
        FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load    "bitmap"
        Load    "ddc"
        Load    "dri"
        Load    "extmod"
        Load    "freetype"
        Load    "glx"
        Load    "int10"
        Load    "type1"
        Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver          "kbd"
        Option          "CoreKeyboard"
        Option          "XkbRules"      "xorg"
        Option          "XkbModel"      "pc104"
        Option          "XkbLayout"     "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
        Driver          "mouse"
        Option          "CorePointer"
        Option          "Device"                "/dev/input/mice"
        Option          "Protocol"              "ExplorerPS/2"
        Option          "ZAxisMapping"          "4 5"
        Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Driver          "synaptics"
        Option          "SendCoreEvents"        "true"
        Option          "Device"                "/dev/psaux"
        Option          "Protocol"              "auto-dev"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"

        Option          "DPMS"          "true"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Main Screen"
        Device          "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. M22 [Radeon Mobility M300]"
        Monitor         "Main Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
        Screen          1
        Option          "UseFBDev"              "true"
        Option          "MonitorLayout"         "LVDS, TMDS"
        Option          "VideoOverlay"          "on"

(EE) RADEON(0): Failed to open framebuffer device, consult warnings and/or errors above for possible reasons
(EE) Unable to find a valid framebuffer device
(EE) RADEON(1): Failed to open framebuffer device, consult warnings and/or errors above for possible reasons
(EE) Synaptics Touchpad no synaptics touchpad detected and no repeater device
(EE) Synaptics Touchpad Unable to query/initialize Synaptics hardware.
(EE) PreInit failed for input device "Synaptics Touchpad"
tmyoung@delta:~$

try hashing out (#) the lines:

Option "UseFBdev" "true"

that suggestion goes to **hlavkb** as well

I've tried messing with the depth and resolution settings in the xorg.conf, but to no avail. ](*,)
