### Post by jedbro on 2006-09-28
Sweet-***.
I finally got a Dual setup going with 3D Acceleration (with Big Desktop)!!!
You were right Ziox, it's way faster with Acceleration and without Xinerama bogging me down.

I'm actually not 100% sure as to how I did it, as I did alot of messing around with aticonfig and it's parameters.

However there are 6 key things I did to get it to work
1) Make sure that Xinerama is NOT enabled!
2) Generated a clean xorg.conf file
3) Ran aticonfig --desktop-setup=horizontal
  ( I also ran sudo aticonfig --dtop=horizontal --overlay-on=1 
    and sudo aticonfig --initial=dual-head, but I'm pretty sure I undid my        
    changes and just ran sudo aticonfig --desktop-setup=horizontal )

4) Added my final size to the Display mode
   (i.e. I have two 1680X1050, so I added a 3360x1050 mode)

5) When you restart, and if you second monitor looks like it's going to work (not screwed up, maybe grey or checkerboard), but you cannot move your cursor into it, you HAVE to configure gnome, and set it to the new resolution (in my case 3360x1050). I did this in "System -> Preferences -> Screen resolution". This happens because the ATI driver knows what you want to do, but gnome is limiting you to your current set resolution size (this took me forever to figure out).

6) Once you have Dual Head working right, add 	
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" 

..to your Device section in your xorg.conf file to get 3D rendering going 
Restart and you should be good to go!!

A good way to test 3D rendering is by running 'fgl_glxgears'.
It will bring up a window with spinning cubes and in the terminal tell you what the FPS are. At first I got a black window until I realized I was missing "EnablePrivateBackZ"

Many, many Thanks to  Ziox, without his how-to and his help in this thread, there is no way I would have figured it out.

My xorg.conf file is below, hope it is usefull for others as well!!

[PHP]

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
	Option 	    "Buttons" "7"
 	Option 	    "ButtonMapping" "1 2 3 6 7"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Monitor 1"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 130.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"

#	Driver		"ati"
#	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV370 5B60 [Radeon X300 (PCIE)]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV370 5B60 [Radeon X300 (PCIE)]"
	Monitor    "Monitor 1"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "3360x1050" "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection
[/PHP]

My next goal is to get Compiz and XGL working on both monitors, but I might wait for Beryl ([http://compiz.net/](http://compiz.net/)) before I do that :)

### Post by srafx on 2006-09-29
This may help some of you understand this article..I had to get help myself:

My configureation is just two monitors hooked to a ATI Radeon 9550.

Look for this in the Modified File under device:
```

Option 		"Mode2"         "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
Option "Mode2"         "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support
Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.

```

**Below is the Org. File and Mod. File:**

**Org File:**
```

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AS [Radeon 9600]"
	Driver      "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option 		"Mode2"         "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
Option "Mode2"         "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support
Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AS [Radeon 9600]"
	Monitor    "VA902b"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

EndSection

EndSection
 
Section "Device"

        Modes        "1024x768"

        Depth        24
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

EndSection

    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"

    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection
 
Section "DRI"
    Mode    0666
EndSection
 

```

[php]HorizSync    28-51     
VertRefresh    43-60
[/php] 
If that doesn't work, increase the numbers...

This is what a screen shot looks like
[http://img135.imageshack.us/img135/1747/screenshothu7.png](http://img135.imageshack.us/img135/1747/screenshothu7.png)
Thanks
 
After closer examination of your xorg file, i think you need to change this in order for the screens to be "right" or not reversed:
 
```
 
Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Main Screen"
    Device        "**0** ATI Technologies, Inc. R420 JI [Radeon X800PRO]"
    Monitor        "Main Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24

```
 
```
 
Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Second Screen"
    Device        "**1** ATI Technologies, Inc. R420 JI [Radeon X800PRO]"
    Monitor        "Second Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24

```

---

	Identifier	"Laptop Screen"
	Device		"1 NVIDIA Corporation NV17 [GeForce4 420 Mac 32M/GeForce 440 Go 64M]"
	Monitor		"Laptop Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Desktop Screen"
	Device		"0 NVIDIA Corporation NV17 [GeForce4 420 Mac 32M/GeForce 440 Go 64M]"
	Monitor		"Desktop Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0	"Laptop Screen"
	Screen		1	"Desktop Screen" LeftOf "Laptop Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

PS.  My Laptop is on the left of the normal LCD monitor.

The reason I want this fixed is because when I unplug the normal monitor, Ubuntu sets my laptop monitor as the primary and sets the resolution to what the desktop monitor should be.  So this is pretty annoying if I have to change my xorg.conf file everytime a change monitors.  so thanks in advance to anyone who helps out.

---

My main monitor is the regular LCD Monitor, not the laptop. I've tried switching them around, but I keep getting the same results.  Maybe someone can look at it and tell my what my problem is.  When reading my code, keep in mind that when it says Laptop Screen and Laptop Monitor, that really is the Desktop Screen and monitor (I think).  I know, it's a little confusing.  That's why I need some help.

Section "Device"

Section "ServerLayout"

    Screen        1    "Desktop Screen" LeftOf "Laptop Screen"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"

EndSection

PS.  My Laptop is on the left of the normal LCD monitor.

The reason I want this fixed is because when I unplug the normal monitor, Ubuntu sets my laptop monitor as the primary and sets the resolution to what the desktop monitor should be.  So this is pretty annoying if I have to change my xorg.conf file everytime a change monitors.  so thanks in advance to anyone who helps out.

try this:

replace [PHP]Screen 0[/PHP] with [PHP]Screen 1[/PHP]

and replace [PHP]Screen 1[/PHP] with [PHP]Screen 0[/PHP]

### Post by Ziox on 2006-09-30
> **CeeDub said:**
> I just tried it.  There didn't seem to be any affect.  Any other ideas?

Keep that change, then change these in bold:

```
Section "Device"
Identifier "**0** NVIDIA Corporation NV17 [GeForce4 420 Mac 32M/GeForce 440 Go 64M]"
...
Screen 0
EndSection
```

```
Section "Device"
Identifier "**1** NVIDIA Corporation NV17 [GeForce4 420 Mac 32M/GeForce 440 Go 64M]"
...
Screen 1
EndSection
```

and this:


```
Section "Screen"
Identifier "Laptop Screen"
Device "**0** NVIDIA Corporation NV17 [GeForce4 420 Mac 32M/GeForce 440 Go 64M]"
Monitor "Laptop Monitor"
```

```
Section "Screen"
Identifier "Desktop Screen"
Device "**1** NVIDIA Corporation NV17 [GeForce4 420 Mac 32M/GeForce 440 Go 64M]"
Monitor "Desktop Monitor"
```

### Post by stani on 2006-10-01
Hi,
I used this howto as a starting point for my ati x1300. I managed to get both xinerama and big desktop working.However I noticed some issues in the howto. For big desktop I found out that
```
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support
```
is not necessary and even it is better turned of, otherwise you can't run beryl. 
```
Virtual 2048 768
```
Adding this code is in my opinion incorrect. Only provide a virtual size when the two resolutions of your screen are different to fake that they are equal."If your secondary screen has a smaller resolution as your primary screen then you don't need this workaround." ([http://www.ailis.de/~k/docs/atilinux/](http://www.ailis.de/~k/docs/atilinux/))

Here are my two files, which both work very well. I prefer the second as it allows me to run beryl on one screen (beryl on ati dual head doesn't seem possible yet) or dual head on a normal x session. To switch between single and dual head I use xrandr, which works better than the kde control panel.

xorg.conf.xinerama

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "stylus"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "eraser"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "cursor"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"  
	Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"

#	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         0 "Default Screen" 
	Screen         1 "Default Screen 2" RightOf "Default Screen"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 70.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor 2"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 70.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI RADEON 1300"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"

	Option      "MonitorLayout" "CRT, TMDS"
	Screen      0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI RADEON 1300 2"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0"
	Option	    "TripleBuffer" "true"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"

	Option      "MonitorLayout" "CRT, TMDS"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI RADEON 1300"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen 2"
	Device     "ATI RADEON 1300 2"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor 2"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "disable"
EndSection


```

xorg.conf.dual (my preference)

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "stylus"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "eraser"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "cursor"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"

#	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 70.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI RADEON 1300"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"

	Option      "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal"
	Option      "Mode2"         "1280x1024"
	#Option     "EnablePrivateBackZ" "off" -> dit mag blijkbaar niet aanstaan
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI RADEON 1300"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "disable"
EndSection


```

---

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

Section "ServerFlags"

#	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         0 "Default Screen"

	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"

Section "Module"

	Load  "ddc"

	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 70.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor 2"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 70.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI RADEON 1300"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"

	Option      "MonitorLayout" "CRT, TMDS"
	Screen      0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI RADEON 1300 2"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0"
	Option	    "TripleBuffer" "true"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"

	Option      "MonitorLayout" "CRT, TMDS"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI RADEON 1300"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen 2"
	Device     "ATI RADEON 1300 2"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor 2"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "disable"
EndSection


```

xorg.conf.dual (my preference)

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "stylus"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "eraser"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection
#Section "InputDevice"
#  Driver        "wacom"
#  Identifier    "cursor"
#  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
#                                                      # /dev/input/event
#                                                      # for USB
#  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
#  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
#EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"

#	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 70.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI RADEON 1300"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"

	Option      "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal"
	Option      "Mode2"         "1280x1024"
	#Option     "EnablePrivateBackZ" "off" -> dit mag blijkbaar niet aanstaan
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI RADEON 1300"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "disable"
EndSection


```

thanks, i've ammended the howto

### Post by yacine on 2006-10-02
Hi,
I finally got dual display work on my Toshiba Tecra A2 with Samsung SyncMaster 21GLs monitor, Thanks to the Howto and the further help of Ziox!
There are several issues I want to share with users who are trying to make their dual display system work. It maight help them:

1. Although running lspci command yields: 
[PHP]0000:00:02.0 VGA compatible controller: Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device (rev 02)
0000:00:02.1 Display controller: Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device (rev 02)
[/PHP]
The command:
[PHP]        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"[/PHP] does not work
2. In the monitor Layout, if I leave space between as in this example: "CRT, LFP", the command is interpreted : [COLOR="Red"]"CRT,NONE"[/COLOR]

In any case, as I managed to get it to work and I thought I post the xorg.conf that worked with the errors it is still generating nonetheless!

[PHP]# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

### Post by yacine on 2006-10-02
> **yacine said:**
> 
[/PHP]
The command:
[PHP]        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"[/PHP] does not work


Sorry,
I meant the command [PHP] BusID     "PCI:0:2:1"[/PHP] I have to setup both devices to BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"

---

### Post by stani on 2006-10-02
> **yacine said:**
> Sorry,
I meant the command [PHP] BusID     "PCI:0:2:1"[/PHP] I have to setup both devices to BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"

The second PCI:*:*:1 should be ignored by Linux users. It is a flag which is only needed by Windows 2000 to set up dual screen support. Always use twice your PCI:*:*:0 BusID.

Stani

---

### Post by mathewc on 2006-10-03
Hello,

Thanks for the help. I have a Dell Inspiron 8500 with a Radeon Mobile 9000.

I used your HowTo for the ati section and had success however I'm trying to run a secondary monitor at 1280x1024 as my laptop runs at 1280x800. I replaced my original xorg.conf file and started over with the HowTo for Xinerama but my display came up as a mirror (which it does with an unmodified xorg.conf) Here is my xorg.conf file.

Thanks a million!

```
Section "Files"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # path to defoma fonts
        FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load    "i2c"
        Load    "bitmap"
        Load    "ddc"
        Load    "dri"
        Load    "extmod"
        Load    "freetype"
        Load    "glx"
        Load    "int10"
        Load    "type1"
        Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver          "kbd"
        Option          "CoreKeyboard"
        Option          "XkbRules"      "xorg"
        Option          "XkbModel"      "pc104"
        Option          "XkbLayout"     "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
        Driver          "mouse"
        Option          "CorePointer"
        Option          "Device"                "/dev/input/mice"
        Option          "Protocol"              "ExplorerPS/2"
        Option          "ZAxisMapping"          "4 5"
        Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Driver          "synaptics"
        Option          "SendCoreEvents"        "true"
        Option          "Device"                "/dev/psaux"
        Option          "Protocol"              "auto-dev"
        Option          "HorizScrollDelta"      "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
        Driver          "ati"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
         Screen         0
        Option          "AGPFastWrite" "false"
        Option          "EnablePageFlip" "true"
        Option          "AccelMethod" "XAA"
        Option          "DMAForeXv" "off"
        Option          "MonitorLayout" "LVDS"
EndSection


Section "Device"
        Identifier      "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
        Driver          "ati"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
         Screen         1
        Option          "AGPFastWrite" "false"
        Option          "EnablePageFlip" "true"
        Option          "AccelMethod" "XAA"
        Option          "DMAForeXv" "off"
        Option          "MonitorLayout" "TMDS"
        # Option          "TVOutFormat" "Composite" #or SVIDEO etc
        # Option          "TVStandard" "PAL-G" #or NTSC, PAL-I for uk etc
        # Option          "ConnectedMonitor" "TV"
EndSection




Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Laptop Display"
        Option          "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Desktop Display"
        Option          "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Main Screen"
        Device          "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
        Monitor         "Laptop Display"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x800"
        EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Second Screen"
        Device          "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
        Monitor         "Desktop Display"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option          "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Screen 0        "Main Screen"
        Screen 1        "Main Screen" Rightof "Second Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection
```

### Post by chiefofthejojos on 2006-10-03
I was able to get mergedfb working, but I had to do several other things to get it to work.
I had to make duplicate device, monitor, and screen sections for the second monitor as was described in the Xinerama howto.  I also had to look up my hsync and vsync rates because the correct values didn't get detected right.
After fixing the hsync and vsync values I was able to change the resolution to 1920x1440. :D

---

I replaced my original xorg.conf file and started over with the HowTo for Xinerama but my display came up as a mirror (which it does with an unmodified xorg.conf) Here is my xorg.conf file.

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection
 
Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection
 
Section "Device"
        Identifier      "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
        Driver          "ati"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
         Screen         0
        Option          "AGPFastWrite" "false"
        Option          "EnablePageFlip" "true"
        Option          "AccelMethod" "XAA"
        Option          "DMAForeXv" "off"
        Option          "MonitorLayout" "LVDS"
EndSection
 
 
Section "Device"
        Identifier      "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
        Driver          "ati"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
         Screen         1
        Option          "AGPFastWrite" "false"
        Option          "EnablePageFlip" "true"
        Option          "AccelMethod" "XAA"
        Option          "DMAForeXv" "off"
        Option          "MonitorLayout" "TMDS"
        # Option          "TVOutFormat" "Composite" #or SVIDEO etc
        # Option          "TVStandard" "PAL-G" #or NTSC, PAL-I for uk etc
        # Option          "ConnectedMonitor" "TV"
EndSection
 
 
 
 
Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Laptop Display"
        Option          "DPMS"
EndSection
 
Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Desktop Display"
        Option          "DPMS"
EndSection
 
Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Main Screen"
        Device          "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
        Monitor         "Laptop Display"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x800"
        EndSubSection
EndSection
 
 
Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Second Screen"
        Device          "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon R250 Lf [Radeon Mobility 9000 M9]"
        Monitor         "Desktop Display"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection
 
Section "ServerFlags"
        Option          "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection
 
Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Screen 0        "Main Screen"
        Screen 1        "Main Screen" Rightof "Second Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
 
Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection
```
 
change MonitorLayout "LVDS" and MonitorLayout "TMDS" to
 
```
Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,TMDS"
```
 
also, add your vertical sync rates and horizontal rates to your Monitor Section**s**

---

### Post by jedbro on 2006-10-03
> **stani said:**
> Hi,
I used this howto as a starting point for my ati x1300. I managed to get both xinerama and big desktop working.However I noticed some issues in the howto. For big desktop I found out that
```
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support
```
is not necessary and even it is better turned of, otherwise you can't run beryl. 

Are you sure about that?
Two questions for you
1) Without "EnablePrivateBackZ" enabled When you run fgl_glxgears (for opengl rendering), does it work for you?
When I have berly enabled (single head) and "EnablePrivateBackZ" commented out. I get an error stating 
"extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":1.0" "

So I can't run 3d rendered games like scorched3D, etc.


> **stani said:**
> 
```
Virtual 2048 768
```
Adding this code is in my opinion incorrect. Only provide a virtual size when the two resolutions of your screen are different to fake that they are equal."If your secondary screen has a smaller resolution as your primary screen then you don't need this workaround." ([http://www.ailis.de/~k/docs/atilinux/](http://www.ailis.de/~k/docs/atilinux/))


I agree with that, if both monitors are the same, that will mess you up.

> **stani said:**
> 
Here are my two files, which both work very well. I prefer the second as it allows me to run beryl on one screen (beryl on ati dual head doesn't seem possible yet) or dual head on a normal x session. To switch between single and dual head I use xrandr, which works better than the kde control panel.


Awesome, thank link you gave is great, as well as the xrandr script he has.
Thanks.

Huge favor to ask, can you describe exactly what you did to get Beryl to work on dual heads (working on the first, but the second atleast usable without beryl).

Thanks so much, I think I must be missing something when configuring beryl or something.
-Jed

EndSection

#Section "Monitor"

	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Dell Screen"
	Device		"Intel1"
	Monitor		"Dell fpw2005"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"  
#	Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
#	Option	"MonitorLayout"	"LVDS,TMDS"	
	Screen	0	"Laptop Screen"
	Screen	1	"Dell Screen" LeftOf "Laptop Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

```

Is there anyway that you can take a screenshot of it or provide some sort of picture?

I would suggest adding Horizontal rates and vertical refresh rates to your "Laptop" Monitor Section...because even though it might be labeled as "Laptop" Xorg might view it as the other monitor.

also it doesn't seem like you have xinerama turned on, which might or might not contribute to the black bands. Remove the (#) in the ServerFlags section

### Post by willca on 2006-10-20
Here is a sample xorg.conf for a 3 head system I got using xinerama.
1xi810 2xnv

0000:00:02.0 VGA compatible controller: Intel Corporation 82865G Integrated Graphics Controller (rev 02)

0000:01:02.0 VGA compatible controller: nVidia Corporation NV18 [GeForce4 MX 440 AGP 8x] (rev c1)


Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier     "X.org Configured"
        Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
        #Screen      1  "Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"
        Screen      2  "Screen2" LeftOf "Screen0"
        InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
        InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
EndSection

Section "Files"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/misc/"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/TTF/"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/Type1/"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/75dpi/"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/100dpi/"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load  "extmod"
        #Load  "dri"
        Load  "dbe"
        Load  "record"
        Load  "xtrap"
        Load  "glx"
        Load  "type1"
        Load  "freetype"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Keyboard0"
        Driver      "kbd"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Mouse0"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "Protocol" "auto"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "yes"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "Monitor0"
        VendorName   "Monitor Vendor"
        ModelName    "Monitor Model"
        #HorizSync   30.0-85.0
        #VertRefresh 50.0-160.0
        Option       "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier  "Monitor1"
        VendorName  "Monitor Vendor"
        ModelName   "Monitor Model"
        Option      "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "Card0"
        Driver      "nvidia"
        VendorName  "nVidia Corporation"
        BoardName   "Unknown Board"
        BusID       "PCI:1:2:0"
        Option  "HWcursor"
        Option  "CursorShadow"
        Option  "CursorShadowAlpha" "32"
        Option  "CursorShadowXOffset" "3"
        Option  "CursorShadowYOffset" "3"
        Option  "AllowGLXWithComposite"
EndSection

#Section "Device"
#       Identifier "Card1"
#       Driver     "nvidia"
#       VendorName "nVidia Corporation"
#       BoardName  "Unkown Board"
#       BusID      "PCI:1:2:0"
#       Option  "HWcursor"
#        Option  "CursorShadow"
#        Option  "CursorShadowAlpha" "32"
#        Option  "CursorShadowXOffset" "3"
#        Option  "CursorShadowYOffset" "3"
#       Option  "AllowGLXWithComposite"
#       Screen 1
#EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier "Card2"
        Driver     "i810"
        VendorName "Intel"
        BoardName  "Unkown Board"
        BusID      "PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Screen0"
        Device     "Card0"
        Monitor    "Monitor0"
        DefaultDepth 24
        Option "CoolBits"               "1"
        Option "TwinView"               "True"
        Option "TwinViewOrientation"    "RightOf"
        Option "UseEdidFreqs"           "True"
        Option "MetaModes"              "1280x1024,1280x1024"
        #Option "SecondMonitorHorizSync"                "30.0-85.0"
        #Option "SecondMonitorVertRefresh"      "50.0-160.0"
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport  0 0
                Depth     24
                #Modes    "1280x1024"
                Modes     "2560x1024" "2048x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

#Section "Screen"
#       Identifier "Screen1"
#       Device     "Card1"
#       Monitor    "Monitor0"
#       DefaultDepth 24
#       Option     "CoolBits" "1"
#       Option     "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
#       SubSection "Display"
#               Viewport 0 0
#               Depth 24
#               Modes     "1280x1024"
#       EndSubSection
#EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Screen2"
        Device     "Card2"
        Monitor    "Monitor1"
        DefaultDepth 24
        Option     "CoolBits" "1"
        Option     "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
        Option     "BiosLocation" "/root/intel.bio"
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport 0 0
                Depth 24
                Modes     "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option "Xinerama" "True"
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option "Composite" "True"
        Option "RENDER" "True"
EndSection

---

Section "Files"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/misc/"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/TTF/"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/Type1/"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/75dpi/"
        FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/100dpi/"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load  "extmod"
        #Load  "dri"
        Load  "dbe"
        Load  "record"
        Load  "xtrap"
        Load  "glx"
        Load  "type1"
        Load  "freetype"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Keyboard0"
        Driver      "kbd"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Mouse0"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "Protocol" "auto"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "yes"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "Monitor0"
        VendorName   "Monitor Vendor"
        ModelName    "Monitor Model"
        #HorizSync   30.0-85.0
        #VertRefresh 50.0-160.0
        Option       "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier  "Monitor1"
        VendorName  "Monitor Vendor"
        ModelName   "Monitor Model"
        Option      "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "Card0"
        Driver      "nvidia"
        VendorName  "nVidia Corporation"
        BoardName   "Unknown Board"
        BusID       "PCI:1:2:0"
        Option  "HWcursor"
        Option  "CursorShadow"
        Option  "CursorShadowAlpha" "32"
        Option  "CursorShadowXOffset" "3"
        Option  "CursorShadowYOffset" "3"
        Option  "AllowGLXWithComposite"
EndSection

#Section "Device"
#       Identifier "Card1"
#       Driver     "nvidia"
#       VendorName "nVidia Corporation"
#       BoardName  "Unkown Board"
#       BusID      "PCI:1:2:0"
#       Option  "HWcursor"
#        Option  "CursorShadow"
#        Option  "CursorShadowAlpha" "32"
#        Option  "CursorShadowXOffset" "3"
#        Option  "CursorShadowYOffset" "3"
#       Option  "AllowGLXWithComposite"
#       Screen 1
#EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier "Card2"
        Driver     "i810"
        VendorName "Intel"
        BoardName  "Unkown Board"
        BusID      "PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Screen0"
        Device     "Card0"
        Monitor    "Monitor0"
        DefaultDepth 24
        Option "CoolBits"               "1"
        Option "TwinView"               "True"
        Option "TwinViewOrientation"    "RightOf"
        Option "UseEdidFreqs"           "True"
        Option "MetaModes"              "1280x1024,1280x1024"
        #Option "SecondMonitorHorizSync"                "30.0-85.0"
        #Option "SecondMonitorVertRefresh"      "50.0-160.0"
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport  0 0
                Depth     24
                #Modes    "1280x1024"
                Modes     "2560x1024" "2048x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

#Section "Screen"
#       Identifier "Screen1"
#       Device     "Card1"
#       Monitor    "Monitor0"
#       DefaultDepth 24
#       Option     "CoolBits" "1"
#       Option     "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
#       SubSection "Display"
#               Viewport 0 0
#               Depth 24
#               Modes     "1280x1024"
#       EndSubSection
#EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Screen2"
        Device     "Card2"
        Monitor    "Monitor1"
        DefaultDepth 24
        Option     "CoolBits" "1"
        Option     "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
        Option     "BiosLocation" "/root/intel.bio"
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport 0 0
                Depth 24
                Modes     "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option "Xinerama" "True"
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option "Composite" "True"
        Option "RENDER" "True"
EndSection

very interesting...i think i've seen people with 5 head systems...very jealous of them...but congratulations :KS

---

### Post by discord on 2006-10-22
So I moded the 915resolution init script to accept another mode and added it.  I also figured out the problem with the black bars, reseting the monitor to defaults fixed that. 


But i have another problem. My gnome panel is located on  
my laptop screen. When I choose something from the menu it creates a window on the larger monitor. But I can only drag the window partially across the laptop screen. It will not let me use the laptops entire display. It's really weird because the gnome panel seems to all be there and I can move my mouse past the point but the window will not move any further.I notice that I can move the image off the side of the larger display. Also I have trouble using the full large display, it seems maybe the windows only get as large as the laptop display.


I'm also wondering how to make this whole setup more portable since I move around with my laptop alot.


```

I got my two monitors working pretty much how I want them to. I disabled Xinerama which does not allow me to drag windows across. But it works. Also I will probably not have problems when I am using one display by itself. It's pretty sweet and I am able to rotate my second display also. I suggest the original thread be modded with mention a forth possibility of dual monitors without xinerama and the advantages of such. If anybody knows why my xinerama did not work I am still curious.

### Post by Snail Wins! on 2006-10-22
Hi! I recently was successfully able to get my laptop working with two monitors using the advice given in this thread. But I have a question... When I use xinerama does it disable OpenGL? None of my 3D apps work correctly when I'm using both monitors.

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-10-23
> **Snail Wins! said:**
> Hi! I recently was successfully able to get my laptop working with two monitors using the advice given in this thread. But I have a question... When I use xinerama does it disable OpenGL? None of my 3D apps work correctly when I'm using both monitors.

You are correct, Xinerama, currently does NOT support Direct Rendering on both Heads.  I believe it only allows direct rendering to be on one screen, though people say that rendering is diabled over all...

---

> You are correct, Xinerama, currently does NOT support Direct Rendering on both Heads.  I believe it only allows direct rendering to be on one screen, though people say that rendering is diabled over all...
Yes. On one of my monitors an application that uses direct rendering shows up as just a black window. But even when I move it to my other monitor it shows up correctly but isn't hardware accelerated (is that the right term?) and runs very slow.

### Post by KenSentMe on 2006-10-27
I run the Big Desktop option from ATI and in Dapper everything worked fine. Now i've updated to Edgy, but my fglrx doesn't work anymore. Here are my Zorg.0.log and xorg.conf

[http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/xorg](http://lab.vandenieuwenhof.nl/files/xorg)

Any ideas?

---

### Post by KenSentMe on 2006-10-27
Okay, found the solution.

Seems that in Edgy you have to add this to your xorg.conf:

```

Section &#8220;Extensions&#8221;
     Option &#8220;Composite&#8221; &#8220;disable&#8221;
EndSection

```

---

                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Main-lcd"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	VideoRam	64000
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
        Screen            0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 Sec-lcd"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	VideoRam	64000
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
        Screen            1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"lcd1"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-100
	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier     "lcd2"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-70
	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"0 Main-lcd"
	Monitor		"lcd1"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second"
	Device		"1 Sec-lcd"
	Monitor		"lcd2"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"  
    Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection  

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Option "Xinerama" "on"
        Option "Clone" "off"
        Screen          0 "Default Screen"
        Screen          1 "Second" RightOf "Default Screen"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by lzydba on 2006-11-04
Well....I think I've hit the end of my rope with this one. Did my best to figure this out on my own but failed, so now I need to ask for some help.

I am having the problem where my primary monitor is flip flopped. My setup is  a 19" Samsung LCD connected using the DVI jack as my primary, and a 20" Samsung LCD connected using the VGA jack as my secondary to the right of the primary. (I know it's odd but the DVI jack is busted on the 20" so I got it for nothing that's why its the secondary instead of the primary)

X11 for whatever reason has this turned around so that the 20" is the primary monitor where the login prompt comes up, and the 19" is the secondary. I have been scouring forum posts from all over the web about this all day and have tried a dozen different combinations of xorg.conf entries trying to fix it with no luck.

I am posting the relevant sections of my xorg.conf hoping one of you guys will be able to help me out. (the 19" is the 930 B, and the 20" is the 204 B)

I started out using the instructions found in this post and what you see below is where I eventually ended up after trying several different xorg.conf entries that I found in many different posts from people having a similar problem. So far nothing has worked. Please help...

```
#Section "Device"
#	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA 7600 GS"
#	Driver		"nvidia"
#	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
#EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA 7600GS"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
	Option "TwinView" "TRUE" 
	Option "TwinViewOrientation" "CRT-1 RightOf DFP-0"
	Option "CursorShadow" "1"
	Option "Coolbits" "1" 
	Option "UseEdidFreqs" "TRUE" 
	Option "MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024;1280x1024,NULL"
	Option "SecondMonitorHorizSync" "30-81"
	Option "SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "56-75"
	#Option "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP-0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	DisplaySize 376.22 301.056
	Identifier "Monitor0"
	ModelName "SyncMaster 930B"
	VendorName "Samsung"
	HorizSync 31.0 - 81.0
	VertRefresh 56.0 - 75.0
	Option "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	DisplaySize 376.22 301.056
	Identifier "Monitor1"
	ModelName "SyncMaster 204B"
	VendorName "Samsung"
	HorizSync 31.0 - 81.0
	VertRefresh 56.0 - 75.0
	Option "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen0"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA 7600 GS"
	Monitor		"Monitor0"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen1"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA 7600 GS"
	Monitor		"Monitor1"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen 0 "Screen0"
	Screen 1 "Screen1" rightOf "Screen0"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
```

Perhaps this will work, add this line to your device section:
```
Option "ConnectedMonitor" "CRT, DFP"
```

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    # path to defoma fonts
    FontPath    "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load    "bitmap"
    Load    "ddc"
    Load    "dri"
    Load    "extmod"
    Load    "freetype"
    Load    "glx"
    Load    "int10"
    Load    "type1"
    Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "CoreKeyboard"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "us"
    Option        "XkbOptions"    "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
    Option        "Protocol"        "ExplorerPS/2"
    Option        "ZAxisMapping"        "4 5"
    Option        "Emulate3Buttons"    "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "0 Main-lcd"
    Driver        "ati"
    BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
    VideoRam    64000
    Option        "UseFBDev"        "true"
        Screen            0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "1 Sec-lcd"
    Driver        "ati"
    BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
    VideoRam    64000
    Option        "UseFBDev"        "true"
        Screen            1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "lcd1"
    Option        "DPMS"
    HorizSync    30-100
    VertRefresh    50-160
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier     "lcd2"
    Option        "DPMS"
    HorizSync    30-70
    VertRefresh    50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Device        "0 Main-lcd"
    Monitor        "lcd1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        8
        Modes        "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        15
        Modes        "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1680x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Second"
    Device        "1 Sec-lcd"
    Monitor        "lcd2"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"  
    Option "Xinerama" "true" 
EndSection  

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Option "Xinerama" "on"
        Option "Clone" "off"
        Screen          0 "Default Screen"
        Screen          1 "Second" RightOf "Default Screen"
    Screen        "Default Screen"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
    Mode    0666
EndSection

```

Play around with your Resolutions a bit, because why you label as the second screen's resolution might be the resolution for your main screen

### Post by kreedtek21 on 2006-11-06
For those of you who are having troubles after upgrading to Edgy being able to drag windows all the way across with xinerama, I found out how to fix it.  Delete the following directory from your home folder:

.gonf/desktop/gnome/screen

After that, log out of gnome and back in and you should have the full desktop to drag and open windows in.  You will notice the difference if you have the pager in your taskbar and the size will change from square to rectangle representing the usable window size.

---

### Post by montgoja on 2006-11-06
Thanks for this thread!  I used the Big-Desktop method to get my dual monitors going (I was a little stupid at first and tried the ATI MergedFB since ATI is my card -- Sapphire 1900 Crossfire.  Couldn't get the graphic login and had to recovery mode it... annoying but fixed.) and it worked great!

Ironically, I didn't realize that it really *did* work until my roomate switched my screen res to 2560X1024 and voila, dual screens.  I smacked my head off the desk for a minute after that one...  *is a n00b*

Thanks for all the help!

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

#

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"

	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"

EndSection

EndSection

Section "Device"

	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"

	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"

### Post by tatare99 on 2006-11-11
Hi!

I have a Dell 700m laptop.
I bought an LCD monitor this week and I've been fighting with xorg.xonf since.
What I'd like to do is to get a 1280x1024 resolution on the LCD screen. I don't want to use the xinerama mode, I already tried and it's not what I'm looking for... do you have any idea ??

Besides, is it possible that everything is sent to the VGA port ; i.e. everything displayed on the external LCD monitor and nothing on the laptop ? If yes, how does this work ?

Thanks so much in advance!

-D

---

### Post by tatare99 on 2006-11-12
I finally managed to get my external monitor working ! (without xinerama) :-) I'm happy !
I have a Dell 700m and an external L1919S.
Here is what I do:
when GRUB shows up, I press Fn+F8, then the output is redirected to my external monitor. The important thing is press Fn+F8 when GRUB shows up.

Then, I just have to change the resolution of the screen by adding in the modes: "1280x1024"
i.e.
Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x800" "1024x768"
And then, it just works !
I have the output on my external LCD monitor (with a 1280x1024 resolution) and nothing on the laptop... exactly what I wanted :-)
And when I restart my laptop without pressing Fn+F8 when GRUB shows up, the display is as usual on my laptop at 1280x800... it's great !
