### Post by theorem_hunter on 2006-11-15
worked using big desktop how to because i want 3D acceleration. thanks for the easy to use how to. so im not sure if i read the how to properly, is big desktop the only way to get dual monitors working with 3D acceleration or will mergedFB also work?

i have a ATI RADEON X700, 256mb DDR. & i wouldnt mind having XGL running with dual monitors as well :) i saw it working in a google video. [http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=8350072959777475145&q=linux+dual+screen](http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=8350072959777475145&q=linux+dual+screen)
but that is with a nVidea card :( 

the exact steps i followed was 
1.1. sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf_backu
2.Unplug the second monitor.
3.sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
4.horizontal refresh rate set to 31-81
5.vertical refresh rate set to 60-75
6.cat /etc/X11/xorg.conf | grep BusID
7.output was BusID       "PCI:5:0:0" :)	
8.aticonfig --dtop=horizontal
9.Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
Option "Mode2"         "1280x1024" #Resolution for second monitor
Option "DesktopSetup" "LVDS,AUTO" #the types of monitors that is connected LVDS = LCD, CRT, AUTO
Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
Option "HSync2" "65" #This sets the horizontal sync for the secondary display. 
Option "VRefresh2" "60" #This sets the refresh rate of the secondary display.
10.rebooted

it is working, i have dual monitors but... its dead slow. it feels like im using a vesa driver

fglrxinfo
Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: [www.mesa3d.org](www.mesa3d.org)
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.5.1)

glxgears
Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".


^^^ is painfully slow & looks like its doing 1 frame every 10 seconds :(

so my first question is how can i get things to speed up? or maybe use the fglrx driver?
secondly has anyone gotten XGL to work on a ATI card using dual monitors like in the video?

thanks

---

### Post by dutchmega on 2006-11-15
(Re)Install the following: xserver-xorg-video-all, libgl1-mesa-dri and libgl1-mesa-glx.
Also make sure that dri and glx are loaded in your xorg.conf. That should fix the problem of missing extensions.

---

### Post by TheMeanGreen on 2006-11-15
Hi All, 
   I just wanted to say that this is one of the most helpful threads I have read in a while. I learned so much from it I thought I would post my Xorg.conf to give back to someone with the same issues.

   I have a IBM Thinkpad X22. The internal LCD at 1024x768 and external monitor at 1280x1024 with xinerama enabled.

   The largest issue was getting the different resolutions to be happy with each other.
I found out it was the default depth I was trying to use with the external monitor forcing it back down to 1024x768.
    Here is my current Xorg.conf with multiple resolutions and xinerama.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"

### Post by isleib on 2006-11-15
I have been trying to get Xinerama to work on my Acer laptop with an Intel card off and on for quite some time now.  I feel like I am so close, but am stuck again.  At this point my laptop LCD will come up blank but the back light will strobe on and off.  The connected monitor is just blank.  I can log in and all at this point (can hear the login chimes), just cant see anything.  If anyone has any suggestion I would really appreciate it.

### Post by Mr.Vitale on 2006-11-16
Hey All, I'm new to forum and it seems like a great place.

I had dual monitor configuration working perfectly fine before i updated to the ATI drivers from their website in an attempt to get 3D working (it now is by the way), however, after i restarted the X server.... My one monitor went down.. so i reverted to a back-up copy of mt xorg.conf file.  

The dual desktop is working though, i dragged and droped things on the black desktop, and my background is the full width of the two monitors and its not resized for just one. I am really stuck here and would gratly appreciate some help.

Thanks 
---Alex

Here is my Xorg file 

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#Section "DRI"
#	Mode         0666
#EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection



Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "CMC 17 AD"
	HorizSync    30.0 - 65.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 75.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "IntelATI RADEON 9200 Series "
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse"
	Option	    "OverlayOnCRTC2" "1"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:2:9:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:2:9:1"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "IntelATI RADEON 9200 Series "
	Monitor    "CMC 17 AD"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection
```

But i would rather like to be the monitor connected to DVI to be the primary monitor, and the (smaller one) connected to VGA the secondary. Can anyone tell how to accomplish that?

current xorg.conf:
```


Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"

	Load	"glx"

                                                      # for USB

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY

  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 NVIDIA Corporation NV40.2 [GeForce 6800 LE]"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
Screen      0
Option "DDCMode" "True"
#Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS, TMDS" 
EndSection
Section "Device"
        Identifier      "1 NVIDIA Corporation NV40.2 [GeForce 6800 LE]"
        Driver          "nvidia"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
Screen      1
Option "DDCMode" "True"
#Option "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor, secondary monitor" 
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       30-65
        VertRefresh     50-75
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"

	EndSubSection

		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Secondary Screen"
        Device          "1 NVIDIA Corporation NV40.2 [GeForce 6800 LE]"

        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1

        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"

                Modes           "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1280x854" "1280x800" "1280x768" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection

        EndSubSection

        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
#	Option "DefaultServerLayout" "Multihead"
	Screen	0	"Main Screen"
	Screen	1   	"Secondary Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666

                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Secondary Screen"
        Device          "1 NVIDIA Corporation NV40.2 [GeForce 6800 LE]"
        Monitor         "Secondary Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1280x854" "1280x800" "1280x768" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1280x854" "1280x800" "1280x768" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1280x854" "1280x800" "1280x768" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           15
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1280x854" "1280x800" "1280x768" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1280x854" "1280x800" "1280x768" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1280x854" "1280x800" "1280x768" "1200x800" "1152x864" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
#	Option "DefaultServerLayout" "Multihead"
	Screen	0	"Main Screen"
	Screen	1   	"Secondary Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection


```

first of all, please post in the different howto's corresponding thread, which makes it easier to identify the method used, and it's easier to look for problems. now to see if I can solve your problem:
modify these two lines:
```
Screen	0	"Main Screen"
	Screen	1   	"Secondary Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
```
switching around main screen and secondary screen...

### Post by mbvlist on 2006-12-12
I'm having some odd troubles on my laptop (using ATi mobility X300 with MergedFB): If I add multiple MetaModes, the login screen looks almost fine (both screens at 1280x1024), but once I login, it switches to clone mode. Where does that come from? If only one mode is listed, it works fine, but makes it a little hard to switch between single and dual screen (I now have 2 bash scripts copying xorg.single and xorg.dual to xorg.conf...). 
Other problem is that MergedNotRectangular doesn't seem to have effect. If I change the metamode to 1400x1050-1280x1024, the smaller screen scrolls up and down. How can I solve this? Do I have to disable 3d-acelleration by hand? 

And last, but most annoying, but this is probably a bug in the videocard driver or firmware: If I use a singlescreen mode (no mergedfb set up), the videocard shows the same image on both screens (and I can switch between them using the function key), although the smaller screen doesn't scroll. It just drops the bars above and below, and some left and right pixels. This is most annoying in bash on TTY 1-6: it doesn't show the first and last lines. How can I fix this? :(

```
Section "Device"
	Identifier "Radeon Mobility M300 DualScreen" 
	Driver "ati"
	BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
#	Option "MetaModes" "1400x1050-1280x1024 1400x1050 1280x1024 1024x768"
	Option "MetaModes" "1400x1050-1280x1024 1280x1024-1280x1024 1280x1024-1400x1050 1280x1024"
	Option "MergedNotRectangular" "true"
	Option "CRT2Position" "LeftOf"
	Option "CRT2HSync" "30-83"
	Option "CRT2VRefresh" "56-76"
	Option "DesktopSetup" "horizontal,reverse"

	Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,CRT"

	Option "DynamicClocks" "true"
	Option "AccelDFS" "1"
	Option "GARTSize" "64"
	Option "EnablePageFlip" "1"
	Option "ColorTiling" "1"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier "Laptop Monitor"
	Option "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Dual Screen"
	Device "Radeon Mobility M300 DualScreen"
	Monitor "Laptop Monitor"
	DefaultDepth 24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 24
#		#Virtual 2680 1050
#		#Modes "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier "Default Layout"
	Screen "Dual Screen"
	InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

> Nice!!! I'll add that to my howtos.  Does this GUI work for MergedFB or Xinerama using other driver? If so that would be sweet! I installed the latest (9631) Nvidia drivers and they work great! The New GUI is very handy indeed. It automates messing with the Xorg.conf and would have saved me hours if this was out before I had to do it all manually. It automatically detects all of the screens connected and lets you setup TwinView and the layout and resolution of each screen.

It seems that this GUI config is built into the new Nvidia driver so it only sets up TwinView. The options are either to use TwinView or to Seperate the X screen which requires an X restart.

Unfortunately I still can't figure out how to change the "primary" screen where I type my login. The "primary" screen is my CRT-1 connected through VGA and I'd like the "primary" screen to be my right DFP-0 connected through DVI. Oh well...

OK.... Here she is:


# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

  Identifier    "eraser"

                                                      # /dev/input/event

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection
#############################################################################################################

	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NV41.1 [GeForce 6800]1"
	Driver		"nvidia"

### Post by Cene on 2006-12-22
Hi

I got a small question to what I couldn't find any answers anywhere.
I have nVidia FX5200 and two CRT's (1280*1024 both = 2560*1024 @ 24bit) using TwinView. When I play any fullscreen game (i.e. DoD 1.3 thru Steam using Cedega), the other screen turns off, so I got only 1 screen with fullscreen game running.

Is it possible that when I run any fullscreen app, the CRT 1 would run the game at fullscreen in the resolution it is set at the game, while screen 2 would keep 1280*1024 and show everything during the game there was before too.
What I mean, is it possible to "cheat" fullscreen apps to think that there exists resolution 800*600 when it really is 800*600 in first screen (where the game runs) and 1280*1024 in second screen?

I usually use 1024*768 or 800*600 resolution in games. Here's my xorg.conf:

```

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "NVIDIA[0]"
        Driver          "nvidia"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
        Option          "TwinView"      "true"
        Option          "UseEdidFreqs"  "true"
        Option          "MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024;1024x768,NULL;800x600,NULL;640x480,640x480"
        Option          "NvAGP" "1"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Monitor[0]"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       30-65
        VertRefresh     50-120
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Device          "NVIDIA[0]"
        Monitor         "Monitor[0]"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           15
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "2560x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "1280x480"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

```

What do I need to change or add if it's possible?

### Post by chainzz on 2006-12-25
First of all, merry christmas to you all.

Yesterday I worked hard to get my two monitors with different resolutions working in dual-head mode with native resolutions on both screens. I finally managed to do that with the xorg.conf you can see below, but now I don't have video acceleration by the ATI proprietary drivers. I can't drag windows between my two monitors with this xorg.conf by the way, but that's not a very big problem.

Here's my xorg.conf:


```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
  Identifier "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "LeftScreen" 0 0
  screen 1 "RightScreen" rightof "LeftScreen"
  InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
  InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
  InputDevice "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"
  
  # path to defoma fonts
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
  FontPath "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
  Load "i2c"
  Load "bitmap"
  Load "ddc"
  Load "extmod"
  Load "freetype"
  Load "int10"
  Load "type1"
  Load "vbe"
  load "glx"
  load "GLcore"
  load "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Generic Keyboard"
  Driver "kbd"
  option "CoreKeyboard"
  option "XkbRules" "xorg"
  option "XkbModel" "pc105"
  option "XkbLayout" "us"
  option "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Configured Mouse"
  Driver "mouse"
  option "CorePointer"
  option "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
  option "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
  option "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
  option "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
  Identifier "stylus"
  Driver "wacom"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "stylus"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
  Identifier "eraser"
  Driver "wacom"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "eraser"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
  Identifier "cursor"
  Driver "wacom"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "cursor"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
  identifier "MonitorLeft"
  vendorname "Samsung"
  modelname "SyncMaster 940BW"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
  identifier "MonitorRight"
  vendorname "LG"
  modelname "L1730S"
EndSection

Section "Device"
  identifier "Left"
  busid "PCI:1:0:0"
  driver "fglrx"
  screen 0
EndSection

Section "Device"
  identifier "Right"
  busid "PCI:1:0:0"
  driver "fglrx"
  screen 1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
  Identifier "LeftScreen"
  Device "Left"
  Monitor "MonitorLeft"
  DefaultDepth 24
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 1
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 4
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 8
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 15
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 16
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 24
    Modes "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	Virtual 2720 1024
  EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
  Identifier "RightScreen"
  Device "Right"
  Monitor "MonitorRight"
  DefaultDepth 24
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 1
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 4
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 8
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 15
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 16
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 24
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	Virtual 2720 1024
  EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
  Mode 0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
  option "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

```

Any ideas?

---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-12-25
> **chainzz said:**
> First of all, merry christmas to you all.

Yesterday I worked hard to get my two monitors with different resolutions working in dual-head mode with native resolutions on both screens. I finally managed to do that with the xorg.conf you can see below, but now I don't have video acceleration by the ATI proprietary drivers.

Here's my xorg.conf:


```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
  
  # path to defoma fonts
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
  FontPath "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
  Load "i2c"
  Load "bitmap"
  Load "ddc"
  Load "extmod"
  Load "freetype"
  Load "int10"
  Load "type1"
  Load "vbe"
  load "glx"
  load "GLcore"
  load "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Generic Keyboard"
  Driver "kbd"
  option "CoreKeyboard"
  option "XkbRules" "xorg"
  option "XkbModel" "pc105"
  option "XkbLayout" "us"
  option "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Configured Mouse"
  Driver "mouse"
  option "CorePointer"
  option "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
  option "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
  option "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
  option "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
  Identifier "stylus"
  Driver "wacom"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "stylus"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
  Identifier "eraser"
  Driver "wacom"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "eraser"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
  Identifier "cursor"
  Driver "wacom"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "cursor"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
  Identifier "MonitorLeft"
  Vendorname "Samsung"
  Modelname "SyncMaster 940BW"
  HorizSync 30.0 - 81.0
  VertRefresh 56.0 - 75.0
EndSection

Section "Device"
  Identifier "ATI Radeon 9600 XT"
  Busid "PCI:1:0:0"
  Driver "radeon"
  Option "MergedFB" "On"
  Option "CRT2HSync" "30-83"
  Option "CRT2VRefresh" "56-75"
  Option "CRT2Position" "RightOf"
  Option "MetaModes" "1440x900-1280x1024"
  Option "OverlayOnCRTC2" "true"
  Option "MonitorLayout" "LCD, CRT"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
  Identifier "LeftScreen"
  Device " ATI Radeon 9600 XT"
  Monitor "MonitorLeft"
  DefaultDepth 24
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 1
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 4
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 8
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 15
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 16
    Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
  EndSubSection
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 24
    Modes "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    Virtual 2720 1024
  EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
  Mode 0666
EndSection
```Any ideas?


the reason why you've lost 3d acceleration is because you are using the open-source driver for your ATI card, and to my understanding, the open-source driver only enables 3d acceleration for few card models.

So if you want to have 3d acceleration and dual monitors, then I recommend trying out the Big-Desktop Guide.

Good luck!

p.s. please post in the thread that correspond with the method you have used (i.e. MergedFB => post in MergedFB thread)

Merry Christmas!

### Post by sincereheart on 2006-12-26
I am wanting to allow multiple users sitting in the same room to use one single machine but each have their own Monitors, Keyboards and Mouse.... So kind of like sharing the same computer ......  kind of opposite to KVM....
What would you recommend for that... "FREE NX" or some thing different - I prefer "Open Source".

In simple terms I want to economize on the use of Computers. So one shared computer running some simple basic tasks e.g. MS Word or Excel by each user ... logged in cuncurrently.. So no single user has their own PC. Rather every one is sharing this single pc while sitting in the same office.. They may be connected to this computer with their Mouse, Keyboard and Monitor through either USB or ethernet... etc...

It is not must to have a virtual server like XEN. Even Windows XP or Ubuntu etc is fine..

Any ideas or thoughts are wellome.

### Post by Ziox on 2006-12-26
@chainzz:
Your 3d acceleration is disabled because you are using the "Xinerama" method.  Like I suggested before, try the BigDesktop method. If that's done correctly, you can have dual monitors with 3d acceleration.  If you want to move your windows between the monitors, add this line to your "ServerLayout" section:

```
Option "Xinerama" "true"
```

@sincereheart
I'm not sure what you are asking.  However, a search using the command: "apt-cache search vnc" on the ubuntu repository results the following that I think might interest you:

vnc4-common - Virtual network computing server software
vnc4server - Virtual network computing server software
vncommand - VNC server which monitors a specified program
vncserver - Virtual network computing server software

I bet you can find more information on VNC if you google it.

### Post by FXWGBill on 2006-12-27
Well...  No one really answered my questions, however I did finally get it working.  I will post my xorg.conf file here in just a second.

To reiterate, I have an nVidia GeForce 6800 graphics card and am running two Dell 1905FP panel monitors. I have a Pent IV 3.2 Gig proc with a gig of RAM.  

What I finally ended up doing, was installing the 'test' drivers and setup gui from this:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=139264&highlight=HOWTO%3A+Latest+Nvidia+Drivers-+Testers+needed](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=139264&highlight=HOWTO%3A+Latest+Nvidia+Drivers-+Testers+needed)

Now... what I found was that it still didn't work.  BUT! (Always one of those...) What I discovered was: For one...  I ran the 'upgrade' on the xorg.conf that I had been manually editing; this led to it's own set of new problems!  So what I discovered was that I needed to go back to the ORIGIANL xorg.conf file that I started with; the totally clean, initial install file.  After doing that and running it again, I finally was gettin somewhere!!

With the new drivers and the GUI, I started making the changes to run 'separate x screens' and aceppted it and rebooted.  It kept coming up in 'twinview'; I tried all kinds of combinations to no avail.  

What finally dawned on me, was that signed in as a 'user', it wasn't going to work!! You have to be root to change those types of things.  So at that point, I exited the user mode and logged back in as root.  I made the changes in the gui setup proggy, and saved it, did a reboot and there it was!!  My two separate X-Screens...

I will go ahead and post my xorg.conf in case someone is having trouble.  As I said, my config file is for TWO SEPARATE X-Screens.....

 # nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Nov  9 17:56:12 PST 2006

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    Screen      1  "Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor1"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "DELL 1905FP"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 81.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 76.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "DELL 1905FP"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 81.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 76.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 6800"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 6800"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP: 1280x1024 +0+0; DFP: 1024x768 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard1"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: 1280x1024 +0+0; CRT: 1024x768 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection


Hopefully this will help someone.....

### Post by secular on 2007-01-04
FXWGBill, I've seen quite a few how-to's for setting up separate x screens, and I think I understand the basic concept.

I wonder how to open a window or program in the second x screen, though. I'm unclear on this.

If you could enlighten me, I would appreciate it.

---

### Post by FXWGBill on 2007-01-04
What you end up with, are two identicle, (for the most part), screens... If you are using Gnome and have the taskbars at the top of the screen and the bottom, that is exactly what you will have on both screens.

I like to customize my task bars in the order of importance / how often needed, as far as placing 'buttons' on each of the two sides, as well as how many desktops and WHICH desktop on each side that I open particular applications.  That is one of the main reasons I love having the two monitors as separate x screens, ie things that stay open but not used real often go to the higher numbered desktop and on the left monitor.  That way my 'work' stays on the screen directly in front of me and the other monitor is slightly off to the left and has my email proggy and RSS reader etc on it; things that need to be glanced at, from time to time. 

Each monitor is pretty independent and you can customize each side as you wish.  To me, it's almost like having two separate puters sittin side by side, but with the advantage of being able to copy/paste and things like that, between the 'two puters'.

Hope that helps....

---

### Post by secular on 2007-01-04
Thanks, FXWGBill. That's just what I needed to know. :)

### Post by IntuitiveNipple on 2007-01-10
I've managed to obtain two working profiles for dual monitors on the G450 on Ubuntu Edgy Eft 6.10 i386 32-bit SMP.
[list]
[*]Xinerama with Xorg free open-source drivers
[*]MergedFB with the latest 'unoffical' Matrox partially-free drivers
[/list]
**Xinerama**
Using the Xorg open-source (no proprietary Matrox binary mga_hal_drv.so) and Xinerama gives me two 1280x1024 screens.

Unfortunately this configuration cannot take advantage of hardware acceleration so games, applications like Google Earth, and DVD playback are painfully slow.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
# This configuration is customised for a Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead and Xinerama mode
#
# *** In order for this accelerated AGP4x to work, the ServerFlag ignoreABI
# *** must be added. If it isn't read and honoured in this file, add it to /etc/gdm.conf
# *** like this (find this section and add the -ignoreABI flag to the command):
# 	[server-Standard]
# 	command=/usr/X11R6/bin/X -ignoreABI -br -audit 0 

Section "ServerFlags"
Option "IgnoreABI" "True"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead"
	Driver		"mga"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Screen		0
	Option		"DDCMode"		"True"
	Option 		"AGPMode" 		"4"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead"
	Driver		"mga"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Screen		1
	Option		"DDCMode"		"True"
	Option 		"AGPMode" 		"4"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"XIOD 19 LCD"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"XIOD 17 LCD"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Primary Screen"
	Device		"0 Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead"
	Monitor		"XIOD 19 LCD"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Secondary Screen"
	Device		"1 Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead"
	Monitor		"XIOD 17 LCD"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Xinerama Layout"
	Screen		0 "Primary Screen"
	Screen		1 "Secondary Screen" RightOf "Primary Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Option		"Xinerama" "True"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```
**MergedFB**
Using the 'unofficial' Matrox drivers compiled and maintained by *tuxx-home.at* at [Unofficial Matrox Technical Support Forum](http://matrox.tuxx-home.at/viewtopic.php?t=17), I installed the 4.4.2 32-bit drivers.

This configuration has the advantage of being hardware accelerated, but if you want to use Google Earth you'll need to reduce the colour depth to 16 (**DefaultDepth 16**)
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
# This configuration is customised for a Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead and MergedFB mode
#
# *** In order for this accelerated AGP4x and MGA HAL to work, the ServerFlag ignoreABI
# *** must be added. If it isn't read and honoured in this file, add it to /etc/gdm.conf
# *** like this (find this section and add the -ignoreABI flag to the command):
# 	[server-Standard]
# 	command=/usr/X11R6/bin/X -ignoreABI -br -audit 0 

Section "ServerFlags"
Option "IgnoreABI" "True"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead"
	Driver		"mga"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	Option 		"AGPMode" 		"4"
	Option 		"HWcursor" 		"True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Dual Monitors"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Display Merged"
	Device		"Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead"
	Monitor		"Dual Monitors"
	DefaultDepth	24
	Option 		"MergedFB" 		"true" 		#Enable MergedFB function
	Option 		"MetaModes" 		"1280x1024-1280x1024" #Monitor Resolutions for Primary-Secondary monitors 
	Option 		"Monitor2Hsync"		"30-65" 	#Horizontal Sync of the Monitor (check your monitor's manual for correct values)
	Option 		"Monitor2VRefresh"	"50-75" 	#Vertical Refresh rate of the Monitor (check your monitor's manual for correct values)
	Option 		"Monitor2Position"	"RightOf"
	SubSection "Display"
		Virtual		2560 1024	# Set the combined horizontal and vertical resolution
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Virtual		2560 1024	# Set the combined horizontal and vertical resolution
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Display Merged"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

---

```

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Oct 16 22:13:07 PDT 2006

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

#

    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"

EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "i2c"
    Load           "bitmap"
    Load           "ddc"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "int10"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
    Option         "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
    Option         "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "stylus"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Generic Monitor"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 51.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600/GeForce 6600 GT]"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    Option "TwinView" "True"
    Option "TwinViewOrientation" "RightOf"
    Option "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
    Option "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
    Option "MetaModes" "1024x768,1024x768"
    Option "UseDisplayDevice" "CRT" #replace 'string' with either 'DFP' (Digital flat panel connected via DVI port), 'CRT' (any monitor that is connected via VGA ports), or 'TV'
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600/GeForce 6600 GT]"
    Monitor        "Generic Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       1
        Modes      "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       4
        Modes      "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       8
        Modes      "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       15
        Modes      "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       16
        Modes      "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

Edit: Fixed allready, RighOf should be LeftOf, so for anyone having this problem, you now know howto fix it ;)

### Post by visible on 2007-02-05
[[IMG]http://i103.photobucket.com/albums/m133/jconstein/th_ubuntu_twinview_2_5_07_01SM.jpg[/IMG]](http://i103.photobucket.com/albums/m133/jconstein/ubuntu_twinview_2_5_07_01SM.jpg)
I got this to work in a round about sort of way.  I followed the guide then added the following changes/options...

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Nvidia GeForce4 "
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"[COLOR="Red"]**AGP**[/COLOR]:1:0:0"
Option "TwinView" "True"
Option "TwinViewOrientation" "RightOf"   
Option "UseEdidFreqs" "[COLOR="Red"]**False**[/COLOR]"
Option "MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1024x768,1024x768"
Option "UseDisplayDevice" "[COLOR="Red"]**CRT,CRT**[/COLOR]" #replace 'string' with either 'DFP' (Digital flat panel connected via DVI port), 'CRT' (any monitor that is connected via VGA ports), or 'TV'
EndSection

I had to change PCI to AGP
changed UseEideFreqs to false
and made the display devices to what I was using, in this case, 2 regular crummy ol' monitors.

good luck y'all.  This was an excellent starting point.
THANK YOU!

I forgot.
I am on a dual boot with win2K
running Edgy
the video card I am using is a VisionTek XTASY Nvidia GeForce4 MX 440

---

### Post by Figgy on 2007-02-05
> **Ziox said:**
> To: mojoman, Webziz
Try adding the following line to your "Device" Section:
```
Option "ConnectedMonitor" "DFP, CRT"
```You can try switching the DFP and CRT.

To Figgy:
nVidia Twinview only works on a SINGLE graphics card that supports dual-output. If you wish to utilize both graphics card, visit my Xinerama guide in a week or so. I'm adding support for two graphics card to that guide soon.

EDIT: No need to wait that long! Xinerama Guide has been updated!

From what you said previously, I should post here because of the 2 video cards. I tried following the guide as-is but it didn't work for me.

Also, these video cards are 6600GT's with PCI-E.

	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB

	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection
###########

	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

Re: ATI Big-Desktop!!

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

Section "Files"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"

  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
  # path to defoma fonts

EndSection

Section "Module"

  Load "extmod"
  Load "freetype"
  Load "int10"
  Load "type1"
  Load "vbe"
  load "glx"

  load "v4l"

  option "CoreKeyboard"

  option "XkbModel" "pc105"
  option "XkbLayout" "gb"
  option "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

  Driver "mouse"
  option "CorePointer"
  option "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
  option "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
  option "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
  option "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

  Driver "wacom"
  Identifier "stylus"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to

  # for USB
EndSection

  Identifier "eraser"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "eraser"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver "wacom"
  Identifier "cursor"
  option "Device" "/dev/wacom"# Change to 
  option "Type" "cursor"
  option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
  # /dev/input/event
  # for USB
EndSection

Section "Device"
  identifier "Generic Video Card"
  boardname "ATI Radeon (fglrx)"
  busid "PCI:1:0:0"
  driver "ati"
  screen 0
  vendorname "ATI"
  option "MergedFB" "off"
  Option "DesktopSetup"  "horizontal" #Enable Big Desktop
  Option "Mode2"         "1024x768" #Resolution for second monitor

Section "Monitor"

  modelname "Flat Panel 1280x800"

  InputDevice "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

  Mode 0666
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

### Post by prog101 on 2007-02-08
In order to get my dual monitor setup running, I did the following (kind of mix of Twinview and Xinerama). I am running ubuntu dapper 6.06 LTS.

1. Install nvidia drivers
sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx linux-restricted-modules-`uname-r`
sudo nvidia-glx-config enable

Note: Do not seprately install nvidia-settings, since it's included in nvidia-glx, and may get a compatibility error.

Restart X or reboot, and dual display should be enabled.

Here's my xorg.conf file which works:
(make a backup of your original /etc/X11/xorg.conf file)

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
        FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
        FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load    "GLcore"
        Load    "i2c"
        Load    "bitmap"
        Load    "ddc"
        Load    "dri"
        Load    "extmod"
        Load    "freetype"
        Load    "glx"
        Load    "int10"
        Load    "type1"
        Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver          "kbd"
        Option          "CoreKeyboard"
        Option          "XkbRules"      "xorg"
        Option          "XkbModel"      "pc104"
        Option          "XkbLayout"     "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
        Driver          "mouse"
        Option          "CorePointer"
        Option          "Device"                "/dev/input/mice"
        Option          "Protocol"              "ImPS/2"
        Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"
        Option          "ZAxisMapping"          "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "NVIDIA Corporation NV40 [GeForce 6200?]"
        Driver          "nvidia"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "DELL E176FP"
        Option          "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Device          "NVIDIA Corporation NV40 [GeForce 6200?]"
        Monitor         "DELL E176FP"
        DefaultDepth    24

        Option          "TwinView" "true"
        Option          "TwinViewOrientation" "RightOf"
        Option          "Xinerama" "on"
        Option          "MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024"

        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           15
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Screen          "Default Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection

### Post by Figgy on 2007-02-09
I got my dual monitors working now. In case anyone wants, here is my xorg file.
```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder26)  Mon Oct 16 22:13:48 PDT 2006

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    Screen      1  "Screen1" LeftOf  "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "i2c"
    Load           "bitmap"
    Load           "ddc"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "int10"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "vbe"
    Load           "xtrap"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
    Option         "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
    Option         "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection


Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName   "AOC"
    ModelName    "Spectrum"
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor1"
    VendorName   "Name"
    ModelName    "Model"
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Card0"
    VendorName  "Nvidia"
    BoardName  "NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    BusID         "PCI:2:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Card1"
    VendorName  "Nvidia"
    BoardName  "NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    BusID         "PCI:5:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Card0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Card1"
    Monitor        "Monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

   EndSubsection
EndSection

    InputDevice "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Screen      0  "screen0" 0 0
    Screen      1  "screen1" RightOf "screen0"

        Option "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

### Post by Skeorx13 on 2007-03-01
I'm more or less brand new to Linux this week. I'm on Ubuntu 6.10 and I have two LCD monitors on an XFX GeForce 7600 gt XXX PCI-E card. One is 19" DVI (which I'm using as my primary screen) and the other is a 15" VGA plugged into a VGA>DVI adapter plug. The 19" is a ViewEra V192WD with max resolution of 1280x1024, Horizontal at 30-81 KHz, and Vertical at 55-75 Hz. The 15" is a KDS rad5 with max res of 1024x768, Horizontal at 30-60 KHz, and Vertical at 55-75 Hz. I ganked some code from my brother's setup (who is far more experienced than I with Linux) and tweaked my way through this thread. I got my monitors to work in twinview, but with a slight problem. The largest graphics mode I can get to is 2304x1024. Problem is my 15" monitor can only support 1024x768. So 256 pixels at the bottom of my small screen (ie the bottom task bar thingy) is cut off. I was curious if someone could recommend an edit to my xorg.conf file to correct the resolution so I am not losing this space (without messing with the Viewera 19" monitor's resolution. I was also wondering if there was a way to split the screens into two separate and individual desktops (like using the tabs at the bottom right corner to switch between them or what have you. I'd really like to have to separate desktop backgrounds (but isn't all that vital as I'm pretty comfortable with my graphical editing skills to make a wide spanning background file that merely incorporates two "individual" jpgs). This seemed WAY easier to setup in windows, but I'm sure that the Linux way is far more tweakable to personal preference... I'm also not sure if the display subsections for depth 1-16 are necessary, but they were in the file I got from my brother so I didn't want to delete something that could possibly have an ill effect. [strike]Also after editing this my volume icon in the upper taskbar thingy swapped spots with the date and shutdown button (which is now in the direct middle of my 15" monitor's top taskbar - I'd get a screenshot but I have yet to figure out how to do that...). What exactly caused it to do that is beyond me...[/strike] So anyone that can help me [strike]fix that[/strike] or merely edit my file to be more efficient and/or organized would be great too. Thanks a lot for putting up with my n00b questions. 

Edit: Figured out how to manipulate the task bars and moved the stuff that was locked in the cut off space to the top bar, so now it's just a matter of other applications getting cut off when loaded (errors in azureus are completely unseen).

### Post by Sam Lars on 2007-03-04
Well I've read through all 44 pages here and tried a bunch of different things to get my two monitors working.  I don't know if my hardware isn't recent enough or what...
It shows through the main monitor just fine, but it pretty much flatly refuses to do anything with the second monitor.  In the log it says it deleted Screen 0 because of missing config.
I tried to tell it to use the second monitor, but it still deleted a monitor and used the default one.
If I boot the computer using the second one, it complains that the first video card isn't initialized.
The closest I got (yet furthest, ironically) was removing the "Screen X" from both device sections.  This caused the second to display green with interlaced black pixels at the top (or something similar depending on other settings) and the main monitor to just display black, but I had to hard reboot afterwards.  The log, though, shows nothing that would indicate a problem.

### Post by Sam Lars on 2007-03-05
Okay, some interesting progress.  I switched to yet another different video card, but with the same general chipset, and it did the same thing.  Then I switched to a totally different card.
```

00:13.0 VGA compatible controller: Cirrus Logic GD 5465 [Laguna] (rev 03) (prog-if 00 [VGA])
        Subsystem: Cirrus Logic Unknown device 8000
        Flags: 66MHz, fast devsel
        Memory at 20000000 (32-bit, non-prefetchable) [disabled] [size=32M]
        Memory at 22000000 (32-bit, non-prefetchable) [disabled] [size=64K]
        Expansion ROM at 22010000 [disabled] [size=64K]
```
I also did some reading in the xorg.conf manual and found that with two separate cards, the "Screen" option in the "Device" section is not needed.
So with the Cirrus instead, the LCD did not display trash but said it couldn't display in that mode and suggested its optimum mode.  I did a reconfigure and got the LCD to work on its own, and then when I switched to both monitors (same xorg.conf) it went back to its error screen.
When it does that, as it is starting the login, I can move my mouse and it will leave the top of the screen.  However, the main screen shows all white except for what looks like a slightly darker login box, the whole thing freezes, and I have to restart.  I also tried startx, which started to open the GUI but the froze.  The log doesn't seem to mention this.

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier 	"Dell"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
	DisplaySize	337.9 270.3
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"ATI 3D Rage Pro"
	Monitor		"Avitron"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1152x864" "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Top Screen"
	Device		"ATI 3D Rage IIC"
	Monitor		"Dell"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Main Screen"
	Screen		"Top Screen" Above "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Attached is the log that is produced when the top screen doesn't like its settings.

---------------------[LEFT]*[COLOR=Red]**[FONT=Verdana]Now, we always want to have a clean slate, because it makes the work much easier, and there is less of a chance that some other arbitrary item will mess this whole thing up.  [/FONT]**[/COLOR]*[/LEFT]

### Post by memezaa on 2007-03-12
> **jmattos said:**
> 
when I get to this point, I'm walking through what appears to be a conf setup wizard, but what makes me nervous is that I just yesterday installed teh drivers for my Radeon X1800.

By doing this am i overwriting that?

Yes, I backed everything up thankfully...

If you backed it up, go ahead and follow the tutorial. When you get to the screen where you select your drivers, just make sure to select the ones you installed. This will just make sure you're seeing the same things he is telling you.

I've done this with my nVidia 7300 and got it working perfect, needed a little tweeking to get the right resolution on my widescreen, but it works. Thanks Ziox.
