```[/FONT][/LEFT]
### Post by Ziox on 2007-03-13
> **herbster said:**
> I have an ATI Radeon 9700 Pro, I am using an XGL session with Beryl now and it works great. So if I install Xinerama and get my 2 monitors going, will all my 3D go away and I have to use older video drivers? I'm confused.

Which one will let me use my 3D acceleration and all that with dual monitors on this Radeon 9700 Pro card (dual head) ??

If you were to use Xinerama, then your 3D acceleration will go away.  Not because you have to use older video drivers, but it is a function of Xinerama to disable 3D acceleration.  

Unfortunately, it is almost impossible to have both XGL w/ Beryl and Dual Monitors at the same time with ATI. (Nvidia is capable of such feat).  The ATI driver is considerably behind nvidia drivers.  3D acceleration will still be there on Dual Monitor, just not XGL w/ Beryl

Ziox

---

### Post by comrade.bronski on 2007-03-13
Hi There,

I've been through a lot of these discussions - seen a few people with similar problems but never a solution. I've got two identical monitors (ViewSonic VP2130b) running off a single 7300GT card. The problem is that the monitor I add using the TwinView set-up appears fuzzy or blurred. Everything else seems to work, including the 3D acceleration.

Here is my xorg.conf (the rest of the file remains unchanged from the single monitor set-up):

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option          "TwinView" "True"
	Option          "MetaModes" "1600x1200,1600x1200; 1280x1024,1280x1024"
	Option 		"SecondMonitorHorizSync" "31-82"
	Option 		"SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "56-76"
        Option          "TwinViewOrientation" "LeftOf"
	Option 		"UseDisplayDevice" "DFP, DFP"
EndSection

Thanks in advance for your help.

---

### Post by comrade.bronski on 2007-03-13
Ah ha - wasn't anything to do with the TwinView set up - was a problem with the DVI-I cable. For some reason the DVI-I cable was being recognised as a DVI-A cable and not as a DVI-D cable. This was therefore reducing the maximum resolution of my new monitor.

While I'm on the subject - it is possible to retain 3D acceleration and plug 4 monitors into 2 7300GT cards? I'm assuming then that I'll need to use Ximerama and TwinView in conjunction. I'm guessing this will knock out 3D acceleration...no?

Thanks

---

### Post by comrade.bronski on 2007-03-13
oh and my solution was to bend 4 of the pins off the cable - brutal but effective :)

---

Ok. After days of tweaking, I finally got this working. Now I can actually start to enjoy Ubuntu!

I tried three of the four methods mentioned here, and settled with Xinerama because it doesn't give me stretched windows like BigDesktop would, or a blank screen with a blinking cursor like MergedFB would. Obviously, different configurations will yield different results. Here's what I've got:

Dell Inspiron 600m Notebook @ 1400x1050
Acer AL2216W @ 1680x1050

And here's the appropriate sections from my xorg.conf, in case you were interested.
```

	Identifier	"0 ati radeon 9000"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		0
	Option 		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option 		"MonitorLayout" "LVDS, TMDS"
EndSection

	Identifier	"1 ati radeon 9000"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		1
	Option 		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "LVDS, TMDS"
EndSection

	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"

	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

	HorizSync	30-130
	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"

	DefaultDepth	16

	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1400x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1400x1050" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

```

At first, I had BigDesktop working, but whenever I was in Firefox, everything was stretched out, creating this awful blurring effect on the text in the window. All the fonts were stretched out horizontally on the Acer screen in any program as well, it's just that Firefox was the worst (the termal wasn't so bad). Now that I'm using Xinerama, all of the fonts are crystal clear.

Thanks for the guide, Ziox.

I set up my laptop screen as zero and my tv as 1 but when I load up my OS they get reversed and my icons and network icon get sent to my tv. Heres a copy of the relevent parts of my xorg.conf file:
```

    Identifier      "Xinerama"
        Screen          0 "Default Screen[0]" 0 0
        Screen          1 "Default Screen[1]" RightOf "Default Screen[0]"

    InputDevice    "Logitech MX310"

        Screen          1
        VendorName      "Nvidia"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
        Option          "RenderAccel"   "true"
        Option          "UseEdidFreqs"  "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

        VertRefresh     43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "CRT Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       28-130

EndSection

Section "Screen"

        Device          "Device[0]"

        DefaultDepth    16
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1280x800"

EndSection

Section "Screen"

        Device          "Device[1]"
        Monitor         "CRT Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    16
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1280x720"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

```
Snyper64
```

	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB

	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

EndSection

        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"

Section "Device"

	Mode         0666
EndSection

```
This works great for my KDE session without Beryl, But if I want to start a Gnome session with beryl I get 2 cloned screens
Can somebody please help? People are saying to me that it can work whit my resolution, but never tell how..:(

### Post by Snyper64 on 2007-03-25
Read my previous post, go to the Beryl forums and search for your problem, Beryl is beta software and thus you are very likely to have problems with it. If you don't see your specific problem there post your problem there, they will have alot more experience with it there than you will probably get here.

[Beryl Forums](http://forum.beryl-project.org/)

### Post by Jovec on 2007-03-28
[quote=PrivateVoid]Does anyone have dual monitors working with Beryl?[/quote]

Yes, using Twinview I have had Beryl working properly.

[quote=mayamaniac]One thing I noticed is the nVidia Settings panel does not save to the xorg.conf file. It has a Save to X Config File button, but it does not work. I have to press preview and manually copy and paste the entire text into a blank /etc/X11/xorg.conf and save it. Or else, after a reboot, all settings are reverted to default.[/quote]

Trying running "sudo nvidia-settings" from a terminal.  When launching it from the menu you won't have write privileges to the xorg.conf file (by default).

[quote=david.lotillo]I am unable to alt-tab and switch between windows display on different monitors. When I alt-tab, I can only access windows in side that particular monitor. I have not been able to find any additional informatiion regarding this problem.

I am pretty much using an old xorg.conf from Fedora modifed to run in Feisty, and while I was using Fedora, I never encountered this problem. I have two nvidia 6100 video cards and two vga monitors. Attached is my xorg.conf file, and there are no errors to report on, but the log is attached. Thanks in advance.[/quote]

Most likley you are running two seperate x screens, one per video card/monitor pair.  If you are using two video cards that are not in SLI mode, then I am fairly confident that this is your only option (as opposed to Twinview).

I honestly don't know if it is possible to alt-tab (or move) windows between two X screens once they are running.

[quote=mailbox]okay, i have two lcd's, one 1920x1200 and the other 1600x1200, running off an nvidia 7900gtx.
can i set them up as two separate screens? meaning, i'd like to be able to click the fullscreen button on a window and have it take up only the screen it was on, have windows snap to the monitor edges, etc.
is this possible? if so, which dual-display app do i need to be using?[/quote]

Yes, you can set them up as two seperate screens.  You do not want to use Twinview for this (but of course still use the binary Nvidia driver).  It can be done using the nvidia-settings GUI if you launch it with sudo from a terminal (so it can actually write to xorg.conf).
```

```
### Post by Snyper64 on 2007-04-03
If you are running a desktop than try adding this: Option "UseDisplayDevice" "DFP, CRT" . If you are using a laptop than you are out of luck on making it the default monitor due to a limitation of the Nvidia driver with mobile GPUs. As for you resolution problems try adding this: Option "MetaModes" "DFP: 1280x800, CRT: 1280x720" and change the resolution or the CRT and DFP to your resolution.

---
```

	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"0 Generic Monitor"

	HorizSync	31-115
	VertRefresh	55-160
	Screen      0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"1 Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	31-115
	VertRefresh	55-160
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"

	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1

```
---

### Post by Sam Lars on 2007-04-09
On your second device section, "Screen    0" should be "Screen    1"
Also, the "Identifier" of the Monitor section and the "Monitor" of the corresponding Screen section should match.

---
```

```
### Post by Snyper64 on 2007-04-10
He might be better off updating to the latest ATI driver before you try changing the type of driver. Changing the type of driver it uses if he doesn't have them installed would more than likely just crash his xorg.conf file on startup again. Update the drivers first and than add in the changes to the monitor and device sections leaving the new driver setting alone "should be called ATI now" and see if it works.

### Post by Snyper64 on 2007-04-10
Well if you want to be able to drag things between both displays or extend a window between the displays than you would use Xinerama. If you would only be using each display as it own monitor and not dragging things between both monitors than running dual desktops would probably be best. I run 2 desktops on my laptop, 1 on my tv for watching movies and the other on my pc screen for everything else. Each desktop has its own taskbars and the one has my icons on it. The only downside to this is you cannot run the same programs in each window(IE: you can't run firefox on both desktops) with the exception of browsing through your folders.

If you want to do a dual desktop I can help you with that, but if you want to run Xinerama I can give you some links but I will not be of much help otherwise if you go this route so you will have to seek out help from someone else.

---

### Post by Salo on 2007-04-10
dual desktops seems to be what I want. How do I do that?

---

### Post by Snyper64 on 2007-04-11
Look [HERE]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=2333719&postcount=453") for an earlier post and just copy in something similar to that. You will need to make 2 of each of the following sections: Device, Monitor, Screen and label each of them 0(for your primary display) and 1(for your secondary display), you will also need to set your correct outputs for each one(CRT or DFP) and finally add the in the orientation(Leftof/Rightof/Aboveof/Belowof) to your server layout.

If you have any more question post here or message me.

```
Section "Device"
  identifier "ATI Video Card"
  boardname "ATI Radeon (fglrx)"
  busid "PCI:1:0:0"
  driver "fglrx"
  screen 0
  vendorname "ATI"

  # SPECIFIC FOR DUAL DISPLAY!!

  Option "EnablePageFlip" "True"
  Option "AGPMode" "4"
  Option "AGPFastWrite" "True"
  Option "MergedXineramaCRT2IsScreen0" "True"
  Option "CRT2Position" "RightOf"
EndSection
```

### Post by HaaTa on 2007-04-17
I've been playing around with multi monitors in various Linux distrubutions for a while and have gotten most of them to work properly.

Currently I have a ATi 200m laptop with a 20.1" LG monitor attached to it. 

I have gotten it to work ALMOST perfectly with xinerama. Full hardware acceleration and indendent resolutions work great on both monitors. 

However, on the second monitor when using a hardware cursor the second monitor will corrupt the view of the cursor in the form of a box while the other monitor works perfectly. I can switch over to using a software cursor in the xorg.conf; however, the image of the cursor imprints all over the place, and oddly affects menus.

Here's the config file:

```

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "single head configuration"
	Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
	Screen         "aticonfig-Screen[1]" RightOf "Screen0"
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics" "CorePointer"
	Option		"Xinerama" "On"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	ModulePath   "/usr/lib64/xorg/modules"
EndSection

Section "Module"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Keyboard0"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "yes"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "Videocard0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option "SWCursor" "True"
	BusID       "PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	BusID       "PCI:1:5:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen0"
	Device     "Videocard0"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

```

### Post by HaaTa on 2007-04-22
Well, I upgraded Fedora's fglrx drivers up to the ones with the Catalyst Menu, which actually works but not to the extent I want it to. After fiddling with it for a while, I go back to my old configuration and turn off SWCursor and...suprise...it works!. 

BTW, with my config above, the new configurator will not even start up (probably due to xinerama or something) but that's not an issue for me!

Now I should get back to work.
```

```
### Post by ashgromnies on 2007-05-03
I have a Dell Latitude D810 Laptop, ATI x600 mobile card and 19" Dell LCD monitor.

So, yea. Ubuntu brought up this "HEY THERE ARE DRIVERS AVAILABLE!" message, so I said "COOL!" and let it do its thing.

After restarting Metacity...

ps -C metacity -o pid= | xargs sudo kill -1

I was greeted by a second monitor that no longer functioned as a second screen, but now as a duplicate of the primary. Fuuuuck.

Rebooted Xorg and the same thing was going on.

I looked in my xorg.conf and that ******* had changed it!

I noticed it changed the driver="ati" in the second screen to driver="fglrx". Nuts. I change it back to ati, restart Xorg...

Now it shows nothing. Uhoh.

So I change the driver to fglrx for each screen and end up having the monitors both working like they should, but now when I bring my mouse cursor into the second monitor, it appears as a big distorted square.

How do I fix that?

Summary: After installing fglrx, moving my mouse cursor in the second monitor makes it appear as a giant distorted square. I can't figure out how to get fglrx to stop being used. I did a complete uninstall of it and things were still broken.

### Post by Snyper64 on 2007-05-04
Either go into System/Preferences/Device Manager and look for your video card or open up a terminal and type in "lspci" without the quotes and post the output here.

### Post by ashoksagar on 2007-05-11
This is how I got my three monitors working.   Attached is my xorg.conf file.   I got rid of the MergedFB option and used only Xinerama.


# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	# path to defoma fonts
    FontPath 	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath 	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi:unscaled"
    FontPath 	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi:unscaled"
    FontPath 	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
    FontPath 	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
    FontPath 	"/usr/local/share/fonts"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection



Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Radeon0"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option        "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
  	Screen        0
	        
EndSection


Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Radeon1"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option        "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
        Screen        1
	        
EndSection


Section "Device"
	Identifier	"m1"
	Driver		"mga"
	BusID		"PCI:3:0:0"
EndSection



Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Acer AL1714-0"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync 31.5 - 80.0
	VertRefresh 50-75
	Option "UseEdidFreqs" "1"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Acer AL1714-1"
	Option		"DPMS"	
	HorizSync 31.5 - 80.0
	VertRefresh 50-75
	Option "UseEdidFreqs" "1"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Samsung"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync 31.5 - 80.0
	VertRefresh 50-75
	Option "UseEdidFreqs" "1"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen0"
	Device		"Radeon0"
	Monitor		"Acer AL1714-0"
	DefaultDepth     24	
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen1"
	Device		"Radeon1"
	Monitor		"Acer AL1714-1"
	DefaultDepth     24	
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"D1"
	Device		"m1"
	Monitor		"Samsung"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    	Option    "Xinerama" "true"
	Option "UseEdidFreqs" "1"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen        0 "Screen0" 0 0
   	Screen        1 "D1" LeftOf "Screen0"
	Screen        2	"Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"
	Option        "Xinerama" "On"	
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

---

Thanks in advance,

### Post by Ziox on 2007-05-13
> **peacekpr said:**
> Using this thread's tips with MergedFB, I was able to get my secondary LCD to work on my ATI amd64 laptop.  Now that I have the secondary/external LCD working, how do I disable the primary laptop LCD screen?  I don't want it to display anything.  How do I do that within xorg.conf?  It seems like there should be an option to do such a thing.

Thanks in advance,
Jason

Don't use MergedFB, just plug in your monitor and reboot.

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-14
Under Feisty, dual monitors can be run without any of the additional software (Xinerama et al).  So this is a new option, if you'd like to update the HOWTO.

It provides two desktops rather than one that crosses the two, but the mouse runs between them and the keyboad is sent to whatever window the mouse is on.

I actually prefer this arrangement to Xinerama as I can run vm's full screen on one monitor and something else on the other.

---

### Post by Unetwork on 2007-05-14
Here is how I solved my problem with Ati Radeon 9250 which is almost the same as radeon 9200.
Used a LG LCD 19 and a pro Sony 21 inch G500.
You first **MUST** know your refresh date for each monitor, this can be found with google, it took me 2 minutes only.

Here the xorg.conf for Ubuntu 7.04:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
# sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
 FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
 FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
 FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
 FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
 FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
 FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
 FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
 # path to defoma fonts
 FontPath "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
 Load "i2c"
 Load "bitmap"
 Load "ddc"
 Load "dri"
 Load "extmod"
 Load "freetype"
 Load "glx"
 Load "int10"
 Load "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
 Identifier "Generic Keyboard"
 Driver "kbd"
 Option "CoreKeyboard"
 Option "XkbRules" "xorg"
 Option "XkbModel" "pc105"
 Option "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
 Identifier "Configured Mouse"
 Driver "mouse"
 Option "CorePointer"
 Option "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
 Option "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
 Option "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
 Option "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
 Driver "wacom"
 Identifier "stylus"
 Option "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
 Option "Type" "stylus"
 Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
 Driver "wacom"
 Identifier "eraser"
 Option "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
 Option "Type" "eraser"
 Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
 Driver "wacom"
 Identifier "cursor"
 Option "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
 Option "Type" "cursor"
 Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4" # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier "ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200 PRO] 0"
    VendorName "ATI"
    BoardName "ATI Radeon"
    Driver "radeon"
    BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen 0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier "ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200 PRO] 1"
    BoardName "ATI Radeon"
    Driver "radeon"
    BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen 1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
 Identifier "L194WT"
 Option "DPMS"
         HorizSync 56-70
         VertRefresh 28-83
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
 Identifier "CPD-G500"
 Option "DPMS"
        HorizSync 30-121
        VertRefresh 48-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
 Identifier "Default Screen0"
 Device "ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200 PRO] 0"
 Monitor "L194WT"
 DefaultDepth 24
 SubSection "Display"
  Depth 1
  Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
 EndSubSection
 SubSection "Display"
  Depth 4
  Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
 EndSubSection
 SubSection "Display"
  Depth 8
  Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
 EndSubSection
 SubSection "Display"
  Depth 15
  Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
 EndSubSection
 SubSection "Display"
  Depth 16
  Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
 EndSubSection
 SubSection "Display"
  Depth 24
  Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
 EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Default Screen1"
Device "ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200 PRO] 1"
Monitor "CPD-G500"
DefaultDepth 24
      SubSection "Display"
            Depth 1
            Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
      EndSubSection
      SubSection "Display"
             Depth 4
             Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
     EndSubSection
     SubSection "Display"
            Depth 8
            Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
     EndSubSection
     SubSection "Display"
            Depth 15
            Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
            Depth 16
            Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
           Depth 24
           Modes "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
  Identifier "Default Layout"
  Screen "Default Screen0"
  Screen "Default Screen1" LeftOf "Default Screen0"
  InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
  InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
  InputDevice "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
  Option "Xinerama" "on"
  Option "Clone" "on"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
 Mode 0666
EndSection

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-15
> **peacekpr said:**
> I tried that months ago.  In fact, that was the first thing I tried since it was the easiest and involved the least amount of work.  The external monitor is a widescreen whereas the laptop LCD is not.  I just want the laptop LCD to be disabled when the external monitor is hooked up! heh.  Now way to force it in xorg.org?

IMHO you should:

Start by upgrading to Feisty.  It really is significantly better with dual monitors.

Then set up your xorg.conf with descriptions of both monitors and get the simplest dual head working - with no xinerama, twinview or mergedfb.

From there decide whether to set up two xorg.conf files - one for laptop only, the other for external only.

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-17
> **dvwyngaa said:**
> Has anyone tried to get Dual Monitors to work with the Intel 945GM chipset using the 810i driver on Kubuntu 7.04? I have a Tishiba A100-233 with INtel 945GM on-board VGA and a external LCD Screen trying to get Dual Monitor setup working with now avail. If you have done this, please send me more info of thread or post it on the forum.

Thanks

Dawid

I have that specification  working nicely under **U**buntu 7.04.  One thing to note is that the latest xorg (in 7.04) doesn't need xinerama or clone to run two monitors.  Without them you get a different desktop on each monitor.  This might help you get it going to start with.
```

```
### Post by veloce on 2007-05-26
> **pneaveill said:**
> Have I missed the update?  Been wanting dual monitors for a while now. Someone suggested that I switch to KDE, as it would find them and set them up for me -- it did but left me with some other issues. Thus, I switched back to ubuntu fiesty from a fresh install.  This thing has been kicking my tail since about page 4 of this forum thread.:confused:

Don't get me wrong, you still need to massage xorg.conf to get dual monitors.  You still need the dual sections (for device, monitor and screen) and a server layout section that puts it together.  You just don't need to add the xinerama option.

> 
```
 lspci
00:00.0 Host bridge: VIA Technologies, Inc. VT8363/8365 [KT133/KM133] (rev 03)

```
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "cursor"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "cursor"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"         # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Option "DDCMode" "True"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS, LVDS"
        Identifier      "Radeon Xpress 200M Primary"
        Driver          "ati"
        BusID           "PCI:1:5:0"
        Option          "UseFBDev"              "true"
        Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Option "DDCMode" "True"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS, LVDS"
        Identifier      "Radeon Xpress 200M Secondary"
        Driver          "ati"
        BusID           "PCI:1:5:0"
        Option          "UseFBDev"              "true"
        Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Dell 1501 Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       28-51
        VertRefresh     43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Secondary Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       28-51
        VertRefresh     43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen Primary"
        Device          "Radeon Xpress 200M Primary"
        Monitor         "Dell 1501 Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           15
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen Secondary"
        Device          "Radeon Xpress 200M Secondary"
        Monitor         "Secondary Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           15
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Screen          0 "Default Screen Primary"
        Screen          1 "Default Screen Secondary" RightOf "Default Screen Primary"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Option          "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection
```

EndSection

```
### Post by veloce on 2007-06-14
[QUOTE=syxbit;2838451]i'm sure i followed the guide exactly, but get the error "Screen not found"
any ideas?

The following line is wrong, there shouldn't be a space after the comma.  

Yes, that mistake is also in the guide. Ziox, you listening?

Option "MonitorLayout" "DFP, DFP" 

When there is a space after the comma, X interprets it as a null monitor.

Also, DFP doesn't work for me - I think it is designed for **digital** connectiongs rather than the usual analog monitors (but don't quote me on that).  In any event, my Dell lcd screen wont work as DFP.

---

### Post by Ziox on 2007-06-15
> **veloce said:**
> [quote=syxbit;2838451]i'm sure i followed the guide exactly, but get the error "Screen not found"
any ideas?

The following line is wrong, there shouldn't be a space after the comma.  

Yes, that mistake is also in the guide. Ziox, you listening?

Option "MonitorLayout" "DFP, DFP" 

When there is a space after the comma, X interprets it as a null monitor.

Also, DFP doesn't work for me - I think it is designed for **digital** connectiongs rather than the usual analog monitors (but don't quote me on that).  In any event, my Dell lcd screen wont work as DFP.

Yeah...I'm listening and I'll change it ASAP.  As for DFP, I believe that the monitor is considered digital only when the monitor is connected through a DVI port.
```

```
### Post by metallicamaster3 on 2007-07-22
Just got me a new old computer. Bit of problems im a total user at.

Specs:
512MB RAM
Ubuntu 20GB HD (IDE 2, channel one)
nVidia MX 4000 PCI 128MB
nVidia MX/ MX 400 AGP 32MB

Got everything down except for Xinerama or TwinView. SInce i use two monitors through two cards, I think I understand that TwinView isnt possible. 

BUT! I did get Xinerama to work through nvidia-settings. Everything works except for the fact that Terminal does NOT start up. It hangs for about 5-10 seconds, and the item on the gnome panel disappears. Terminal never comes up. I think this is also a problem for other pieces of software, but i haven't tested to see if it was true, although I do have reason to believe it is the case.

Here is my xorg.conf:

```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Nov  9 17:56:12 PST 2006

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    Screen      1  "Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Gateway"
    HorizSync       31.0 - 68.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 85.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor1"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Gateway"
    HorizSync       31.0 - 68.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 85.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce4 MX 4000"
    BusID          "PCI:1:13:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce2 MX/MX 400"
    BusID          "PCI:2:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: 1280x1024 +0+0; CRT: 1280x960 +0+0; CRT: 1024x768 +0+0; CRT: 832x624 +0+0; CRT: 800x600 +0+0; CRT: 720x400 +0+0; CRT: 640x480 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Videocard1"
    Monitor        "Monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "metamodes" "1280x960 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

Hopefully someone will be able to help me. 

By the way, that xorg.conf is the one I am using now. It is dual monitors, but each is running a different X session, which I do not want. I need the desktop to spand to both monitors w/ Xinerama, if someone can help me.

I would really like to ditch windows altogether, and if I can get this right, that will come true..

Thanks in advance everyone!
```

    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Gateway"
    HorizSync       31.0 - 68.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 85.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor1"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Gateway"
    HorizSync       31.0 - 68.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 85.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce4 MX 4000"
    BusID          "PCI:1:13:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce2 MX/MX 400"
    BusID          "PCI:2:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: 1280x1024 +0+0; CRT: 1280x960 +0+0; CRT: 1024x768 +0+0; CRT: 832x624 +0+0; CRT: 800x600 +0+0; CRT: 720x400 +0+0; CRT: 640x480 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Videocard1"
    Monitor        "Monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "metamodes" "1280x960 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```Hopefully someone will be able to help me. 

By the way, that xorg.conf is the one I am using now. It is dual monitors, but each is running a different X session, which I do not want. I need the desktop to spand to both monitors w/ Xinerama, if someone can help me.

I would really like to ditch windows altogether, and if I can get this right, that will come true..

Thanks in advance everyone!

Change the value of Xinerama to "1" in order for xserver to span both monitors:

```
 Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "**1**"
EndSection
```

### Post by metapaso on 2007-08-09
> **chance2002iu said:**
> I kind of got dual monitors working with xinerama. The problem is that I can't completely drag windows onto the second screen. The window gets stuck in the middle and won't go any further. I could really use some help or insight into what's causing this. The devices are the onboard Intel and a PCI nVidia GeForce4 4000. I'm running Edgy Eft with the following xorg.conf:


I have this same problem on a Dell laptop with integrated i810 video.  After about 4 hours I sucessfully have two monitors in a side-by-side configuration, but I cannot fully drag windows onto the external monitor. There appears to be some limiting factor as to how far gnome's default window manager will let me drag windows off the main monitor.  This limit applies even when I set the xorg.conf file to place the second monitor RightOf, LeftOf,  Above, and Below.

Has anyone else had this problem, (and hopefully solved it?)
Damon

### Post by metapaso on 2007-08-11
> **metapaso said:**
> I have this same problem on a Dell laptop with integrated i810 video.  After about 4 hours I sucessfully have two monitors in a side-by-side configuration, but I cannot fully drag windows onto the external monitor. There appears to be some limiting factor as to how far gnome's default window manager will let me drag windows off the main monitor.  This limit applies even when I set the xorg.conf file to place the second monitor RightOf, LeftOf,  Above, and Below.

Has anyone else had this problem, (and hopefully solved it?)
Damon

I found the solution to my own problem.  Metacity has a very strict constraints.c file that limits how far off the screen windows can be moved.  Apparently there is a method for compiling Metacity to be Xinerama aware (something about Xinerama USE flags, but I couldn't find any additional information).  My solution is to download Openbox, which under Feisty is pre-compiled to be Xinerama ready.  From a terminal I just type: 
damon@arclight%>**openbox --replace**

and then I can move the windows as I see fit.

I'm sure this is documented somewhere....

---

They startup but I can not use the effects. like wobelling windows and fading, and cube desktop. Is there a way to fix that?

N.B: my G.card is ATI Radeon x700. I tried using the ati proprietary driver but no luck, the same problem no compiz nor beryl.

thank you for your time.

Well I was fast in posting this reply, sorry, I found out that xinerama doesn't support direct rendering, so I switched to mergedFB. 

Linux rules
```

```
### Post by BinaryBrother on 2007-09-07
I really don't know what's going on. I've been trying to goto a Linux based OS for years, and have recently found Ubuntu. Anyways, I had Ubuntu installed perfectly, and was trying to get Dual Monitors working, (using this 'HOWTO' of course) and my Linux crashed with a 'Kernel Panic' error. I think it is somehow related to the fact the CD was slightly outdated, and had 137 updates for software, and a distro update, I tried the distro update, and it give some sort of authorization error, so I just decided to update all the software. I then proceeded to find this 'HOWTO' on multi-monitors, so I switched over to a new workstation and began my work. After I thought the xorg.conf looked right, I tried to save he file, and the system was apprently locked. I tried to switch back over to my main workstation, and of course, it was locked solid. So I managed to close FireFox, and get the xorg.conf saved and closed, although the taskbar was not updating that the applications had been closed, and their icons were still there. So I remembered a quick ctrl+alt+backspace, keyboard shortcut to merely restart X, and I applied it. All went well until I got a really nasty error from an old blue and white based screen saying the 'Main Monitor' was not identified, and was specified in the 'screen' sections. I then hurridly clicked away to get back to the command shell. It never loaded, so I proceeded to do, the only thing I could... A hard power-off. I then received the 'Kernel Panic' fault, and I am now reinstalling Ubuntu. Any of you have any ideas as to why this happened? Also, any way I could get some help on the whole dual monitors thing? Thanks a lot, BinaryBrother.

P.S. I am trying to run dual monitors with 2 different cards, one is an Intel Graphics integrated, and the other is an old 32mb  PCI nVidia. I also noticed something else odd.. When I ran that command to gather BusID's.. My ID's did not look like PCI:0:2:0... They looked like 02:0.a0

### Post by Jovec on 2007-09-07
> **BinaryBrother said:**
> P.S. I am trying to run dual monitors with 2 different cards, one is an Intel Graphics integrated, and the other is an old 32mb  PCI nVidia. I also noticed something else odd.. When I ran that command to gather BusID's.. My ID's did not look like PCI:0:2:0... They looked like 02:0.a0

Just wanted to comment on this specific part of your post.  Many motherboards will automatically disable the on-board video when a dedicated video card is installed.  If the computer is from a well known manufacturer, then you might not be able to disable this since they want the simplicity.

### Post by BinaryBrother on 2007-09-11
Ok, you guys gotta hear about this... I installed Feisty, and all the latest updates after bringing it out of the X crash... I totaled the xorg.conf trying to dual mon. So I, having NO KNOWLEDGE of Linux or Ubuntu, started with 'help', and ended with dual mons.

I booted into Ubuntu just to realize my xorg.conf was fried, so I decided instead of bringing back a back-up, I would look around a bit, and see what I could do. Mainly because I forgot to back it up that time... TERMINAL

binarybrother@binarybrother:~$cd /etc/xX11

So once I got there, I realized I did not know the name of the editor, so I just tried edit... No luck, so error... So then I had read earlier that X will create a new conf file if the default or specified one is not found... So I tried... delete, erase, er, del, remove, burn, deltree,kill,**** on... I tried everything to remove that file... until I keyboard messed up, and I accidentially typed rm for remove I think, and it gave an authorization error... I was happy because I knew the command had tried something... So I 'sudo rm xorg.conf' and entered my password. I tried X and it said no screens or devices with the correct BusID were found. So, I 'help Xorg' and found 'Xorg -configure' it caused both of my monitors to blink, and it completly cought me by surprise, because I was only trying to fix my primary... it said the location of the new config file, and what command to use to test the configuration.. I typed the command, X booted, but for some reason my primary was active, but entered stand-by, and my secondary was just a blur... So, I rebooted, and odly enough my X conf was defaulted... with a broken default, so I entered terminal... and o finish this long drawn out story.. I typed...

Xorg -configure

It gave the a command to try the new configuration with the new conf file, so I just copied the new conf file to /etc/X11/xorg.conf

told my BIOS to make PCI primary, and booted.

Now here I am, with two mons. but my 'screen resolution' option is broken, when I click it from 'system' it never loads... :P

### Post by jctweb on 2007-09-21
No Xinerama.  

10 minutes after I posted (I worked on this thing for about an hour prior), I noticed my xorg.conf had this to say:

Screen      1  "aticonfig-Screen[1]" LeftOf "aticonfig[0]"

It was supposed to be this:

Screen      1  "aticonfig-Screen[1]" LeftOf "aticonfig-Screen[0]"

Notice the difference?  The second screen was definitely to the left of something - but nothing that I actually defined!

The only other thing bugging me at the moment is my second monitor really wants to start up at 1600x1200@70hz.  While this is fine and dandy, I prefer 1280x1024@85 for my old-man eyes.

Now that I'm comfortable with the dual head setup, I'll get that going.  I'll post my final xorg.conf when I'm finished to possibly help someone else out :)
```

```
### Post by Rocketmac on 2007-10-16
FYI, this is a working conf file for a Dell d620 running 7.04 (intel Video) with 2 Dell 1907FP's (CRT=VGA, DFP=DVI).

> # /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Express Integrated Graphics Controller 0"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen		0
	Option		"MonitorLayout"	"DFP, DFP+CRT"
	Option		"DevicePresence"	"yes"
	Option		"DDCMode"	"True"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Express Integrated Graphics Controller 1"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen		1
	Option		"MonitorLayout"	"CRT, CRT+DFP"
	Option		"DevicePresence"	"yes"
	Option		"DDCMode"	"True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Dell 1907FP"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"L90D+ D-SUB"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Left Screen"
	Device		"Intel Express Integrated Graphics Controller 0"
	Monitor		"Dell 1907FP"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Right Screen"
	Device		"Intel Express Integrated Graphics Controller 1"
	Monitor		"L90D+ D-SUB"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Dual-Monitor Layout"
	Screen 0	"Left Screen" 
	Screen 1	"Right Screen" RightOf "Left Screen"
	Option		"Xinerama" "True"
	Option		"Clone"	"False
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"DefaultServerLayout"	"Dual-Monitor Layout"
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

TwinView worked fine for me, with Gutsy and the Restricted nvidia drivers :)

The only problem is that applications always seem to start in the middle space, rather than in the middle of each monitor, and fullscreen applications go fullscreen accross both monitors, rather than the monitor which the application is sitting on.

nvidia settings gives me the option for "Seperate X screen" as well as twin view.

What exactly does this mean?
Will I still be able to drag apps accross from one screen to the other? Will fullscreen work correctly?

I would blindly try it, but I just don't want to totally mess up X, and have no gui.

### Post by Rocketmac on 2007-10-18
> **Lord C said:**
> TwinView worked fine for me, with Gutsy and the Restricted nvidia drivers :)

The only problem is that applications always seem to start in the middle space, rather than in the middle of each monitor, and fullscreen applications go fullscreen accross both monitors, rather than the monitor which the application is sitting on.

nvidia settings gives me the option for "Seperate X screen" as well as twin view.

What exactly does this mean?
Will I still be able to drag apps accross from one screen to the other? Will fullscreen work correctly?

I would blindly try it, but I just don't want to totally mess up X, and have no gui.

If it messes up, you can always hit "ALT+CTRL+F1", type 'sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg' to restore it to a basic state... You can also open up a terminal window and do 'sudo cp -p /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf_working'.  If it messes up, do the "ALT+CTRL+F1", type 'sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop', then 'sudo cp -p /etc/X11/xorg.conf_working /etc/X11/xorg.conf', run 'sudo /etc/init.d/gdm start'.

---

### Post by Lord C on 2007-10-19
Thanks.

Enabled seperate X screens, and ticked Xinerama.

Works like a charm :)

I even see the nvidia splash screen now too.

Now I'm faced with a new problem. No Desktop Effects. Not even the "Normal" ones :/

[Edit]
Okay, back to TwinView.
After searching round, it seems many people are having problems enabling desktop effects on dual displays with Xinerama.

In a weird twist of fait, I can now maximise to individual screens using Twin View :D
[/Edit]
```

```
### Post by Ziox on 2007-10-19
> **Lord C said:**
> Thanks.

Enabled seperate X screens, and ticked Xinerama.

Works like a charm :)

I even see the nvidia splash screen now too.

Now I'm faced with a new problem. No Desktop Effects. Not even the "Normal" ones :/

[Edit]
Okay, back to TwinView.
After searching round, it seems many people are having problems enabling desktop effects on dual displays with Xinerama.

In a weird twist of fait, I can now maximise to individual screens using Twin View :D
[/Edit]

You can now maximize to individual screens because a X restart is needed to get Twinview setup correctly.  If not, then, like what happened to you, all the windows would span both screens. But congrats!

@twistedtwig:
dual wallpaper support is based on the DE.  Gnome unfortunately, as far as I know, do not support multiple wallpapers.  XFCE, however, do.  So you can give it a try.

@pneaveil:
You are mixing MergedFB and Xinerama method together.  I would suggest trying MergedFB first, because it'll support 3d acceleration.  If that doesn't work, then try Xinerama.

@ CWolly:
are you using Xinerama for dual monitor? Also, the problem could exist within emerald.
Although, I would suggest reinstalling the system, which will clean the junk and conflicting software and configuration files.  Then try again.

Good luck

### Post by pneaveill on 2007-10-26
> **pneaveill said:**
> First, welcome to the forums and to ubuntu.  Not sure if you realized from my posts that I am not using dual-head cards, but agp and pci ATI cards. I do have several of the Matrox 450 dualies laying around, but have not played with them very much recently.

All right, I broke down and pulled the ATI cards from the machine and installed the G450 card instead. I have two views of the same screen instead of the huge screen that I prefer so much.

If someone could help me split the monitors, I would appreciate it.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
# This configuration is customised for a Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead and MergedFB mode
#
# *** In order for this accelerated AGP4x and MGA HAL to work, the ServerFlag ignoreABI
# *** must be added. If it isn't read and honoured in this file, add it to /etc/gdm.conf
# *** like this (find this section and add the -ignoreABI flag to the command):
#     [server-Standard]
#     command=/usr/X11R6/bin/X -ignoreABI -br -audit 0 

Section "ServerFlags"
Option "IgnoreABI" "True"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    # path to defoma fonts
    FontPath    "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load    "bitmap"
    Load    "ddc"
    Load    "dri"
    Load    "extmod"
    Load    "freetype"
    Load    "glx"
    Load    "int10"
    Load    "type1"
    Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "CoreKeyboard"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "us"
    Option        "XkbOptions"    "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
    Option        "Protocol"        "ExplorerPS/2"
    Option        "ZAxisMapping"        "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead"
    Driver        "mga"
    BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
    Option         "AGPMode"         "4"
    Option         "HWcursor"         "True"
    Option        "backingstore"
#    Option         "Overlay"         "8,24" 
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Dual Monitors"
    Option        "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Display Merged"
    Device        "Matrox Millenium G450 DualHead"
    Monitor        "Dual Monitors"
    DefaultDepth    24
    DefaultBpp    32

    Option         "MergedFB"         "true"         #Enable MergedFB function
    Option         "MetaModes"         "1280x1024-1280x1024" #Monitor Resolutions for Primary-Secondary monitors 
    Option         "Monitor2Hsync"        "30-65"     #Horizontal Sync of the Monitor (check your monitor's manual for correct values)
    Option         "Monitor2VRefresh"    "50-75"     #Vertical Refresh rate of the Monitor (check your monitor's manual for correct values)
    Option         "Monitor2Position"    "RightOf"
    SubSection "Display"
        Virtual        2560 1024    # Set the combined horizontal and vertical resolution
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Virtual        2560 1024    # Set the combined horizontal and vertical resolution
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
    Screen        "Display Merged"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
    Mode    0666
EndSection
```

### Post by F W Adams on 2007-10-29
I almost got the above to work but now need help ( please ).

Setup
Ubuntu 7.04. NVIDIA 5200 with two monitors, one analogue. Resolution 1280x1024 on both. X window resolution shows as 2560x1024. Set up as TwinView. Used "gksudo nvidia-settings" and then arranged screens ( eventually ). 

Once powered up and running it all works.
Switch off using "Shutdown" and on again, everything is still fine.

Switch off using "Hibernate" and it won't start up.
Starts:
  Ubuntu symbol with orange bar underneath just starting to show then both screens blank.
  After about 10 seconds a message "out of range" then complete hang up. This message is in the same style as the message "analogue" that always shows as the monitor starts and isn't from Ubuntu.
  Resetting manually to cure hang up, then Ubuntu  discovers a corrupted root, which the system cures and then restarts again. Then apart from message about not shutting down property everything is fine again.

The failure only occurs with "Hibernate" but it still causes the same symptoms whether 4 applications are running at the time or none at all prior to hibernating.

I am new to Ubuntu and Linux so may not understand anything that requires lot of Linux experience.

### Post by abhiroopb on 2007-11-08
I posted this before but thought this thread was more appropriate:


I am having a very curious problem with my monitors.

I have the following computer with an LCD Screen (15.4 inches, max res: 1280x800):
100gb HD
1.6ghz core duo
1gb ram
256mb nvidia geforce go 7400.

I also have an additional 19 inch monitor (max res: 1440x900)

My room was arranged in such a way so that my laptop monitor was directly in front of me and the 19 inch monitor was to the right of my laptop monitor (old set-up).

I changed it so that now my laptop monitor is where my 19 inch monitor used to. So now the 19 inch monitor is in front and the laptop monitor is to the right of it - basically switched the two around (new set up).

Previously I had used nvidia-settings to configure TwinView which worked fine. Even with the new setup twinview doesn't have any problems.

The problem is when I disconnect my laptop and take it somewhere half the screen is not there (possibly because it is on the other monitor which is not there). In the old setup the laptop would reconfigure to fit everything on one screen.

I think I figured out why this problem happens. Basically whichever monitor is on the left is taken as the "primary" monitor, and the other one is taken as the "secondary" monitor. So, if the laptop is on the left then it resets but if its on the right it stays the same.

I like my set up right now so any thoughts on how to overcome this weird issue?

my xorg.conf is:

```


# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Sep 12 14:30:30 PDT 2007


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "SAMSUNG LCD"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 81.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 75.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce Go 7400"
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP: 1280x800 +1440+0"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +1280+0, DFP: 1280x800 +0+0"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP: 1280x800 +1440+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection


```

and my xrandr:

```


Screen 0: minimum 2720 x 900, current 2720 x 900, maximum 2720 x 900
default connected 2720x900+0+0 0mm x 0mm
   2720x900       50.0* 

```

So, what I have concluded is that the monitor thats on the left is always taken as the defualt, and the problem is that Twinview takes the desktop as one big desktop so there is essentially no way to set a default as I have effectively one BIG desktop (with Twinview)

Any thoughts?

regards

### Post by konni on 2007-11-11
hey!
thanks to your nice howto, i finally got 'BigDesktop' working and its pretty cool ;)

but still one thing bothers me, most windows open in the exact middle - meaning half on the left and the other half on the right screen. 
just like maximize uses both screens...

i'm quite new to linux/ubuntu, maybe you got some advice?

---

### Post by Jovec on 2007-11-11
> **konni said:**
> hey!
thanks to your nice howto, i finally got 'BigDesktop' working and its pretty cool ;)

but still one thing bothers me, most windows open in the exact middle - meaning half on the left and the other half on the right screen. 
just like maximize uses both screens...

i'm quite new to linux/ubuntu, maybe you got some advice?

I can probably point you in the right direction.  BigDesktop is for the proprietary ATI drivers, similar to Twinview for Nvidia?

You need to enable a Xinerama extension, which tells the xserver that you dual monitor setup is really two monitors, and not one big one, and doing things like maximazing across both screens isn't for the best.

Nvidia's driver provides it's own Xinerama extension which is enabled by default.  I would guess that ATI's provides one too, so you'll have to check their documentation.  There is also xorg's Xinerama, but I have no clue how it will interact with ATI's driver (probably won't).

---

### Post by sagesparrow on 2007-11-12
I've read through some of this forum and I'm not sure what path to take given my equipment.
I'm using a Dell laptop (latitude X300) with 1 gig RAM, video card is Intel 82852/82855 GM/GME.  This supports dual screens.  The external monitor is Dell 1704 FPT 17".  In WinXP it all works fine in various modes including as an extended desktop with primary and secondary status.  In Ubuntu both the laptop screen and the monitor work simultaneously, but they are just mirrors of each other.  I'd like to get it so they can function as one unit as in XP.  Will Xinerama work with the Intel card?  I need to be steered in the right direction.

Thanks

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-13
> **sagesparrow said:**
> I've read through some of this forum and I'm not sure what path to take given my equipment.
I'm using a Dell laptop (latitude X300) with 1 gig RAM, video card is Intel 82852/82855 GM/GME.  This supports dual screens.  The external monitor is Dell 1704 FPT 17".  In WinXP it all works fine in various modes including as an extended desktop with primary and secondary status.  In Ubuntu both the laptop screen and the monitor work simultaneously, but they are just mirrors of each other.  I'd like to get it so they can function as one unit as in XP.  Will Xinerama work with the Intel card?  I need to be steered in the right direction.

Thanks

No, this will not be nearly as easy as in XP (with XP, MS finally caught up to a 1980s Mac, but linux is not there yet).

The answer to your question depends on a few things:

What version of Ubuntu are you using? If you are using gutsy then you would use xrandr rather than Xinerama. See [http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12](http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12)

Do you want 3d graphics?  Your intel card will not run an extended desktop with 3d unless the total virtual desktop is <= 2048 in both directions.

I'm no longer using extended desktop.  In fact I've attached my external screen to an old machine and am using synergy to "extend" my desktop. This gives me 3d on my laptop and my second screen is usually rdp-ing to another machine anyway.

---

### Post by sagesparrow on 2007-11-13
using Gutsy
don't need 3D graphics (i assume gaming is the purpose of 3D) or do i need that to run compiz effects?
thanks

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-13
> **sagesparrow said:**
> using Gutsy
don't need 3D graphics (i assume gaming is the purpose of 3D) or do i need that to run compiz effects?
thanks

Yes, you need 3d graphics to run compiz effects.

The other thing you could do is have scripts to switch btw dual screen and single screen with 3d (I think this should work).  You'd definately use xrandr for that.

---
```

```
### Post by jeffmills on 2007-11-18
After reading this thread's opening post I decided to go with MergedFB.

I have an ATI Radeon X850XT and like how the open source drivers work since Ubuntu 7.10. So the option of MergedFB looks perfect. Because it lets me keep 3D and video on both screens and I use Desktop Effects.

Only this part scares me a bit:

> **Cons:** MergedFB only works with the open sources drivers (mga, ati, , which lack direct-rendering support for many Radeon models. MergedFB also limits 3D contexts to 2048x2048.

On both screens I run the resolution of: 1280x1024...
That means a total of: 2560x1024

Could anybody explain how this will effect my desktop experience?

**Edit: **

I just read a link to: [http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12](http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12) in the reply above this one.

This explains that I can safely remove the following lines from xorg.conf:
     HorizSync from the Monitor section
      VertRefresh from the Monitor section
      Modes from Display subsection in Screen section
      ModeLine from the Monitor section (but this one is often useful)

Is this true? Is there no consequence at all?

And:

> As of today, the following drivers/boards support RandR 1.2:
the ATI driver on r200/r300/r400 boards (since 6.7.191) where RandR 1.2 replaces MergedFB,


Does this mean that in my situation (or anybody for that matter) wanting to use MergedFB, better use RandR 1.2?

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-19
> **jeffmills said:**
> After reading this thread's opening post I decided to go with MergedFB.


You probably should have looked at the date of the opening post - getting a bit dated.

> **jeffmills said:**
> 
On both screens I run the resolution of: 1280x1024...
That means a total of: 2560x1024

Could anybody explain how this will effect my desktop experience?


Depends whether your graphics board supports 3d at that size.  My intel board only supports 3d at a maximum of 2048 in either direction. Try it and see.

> **jeffmills said:**
> 
I just read a link to: [http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12](http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12) in the reply above this one.

This explains that I can safely remove the following lines from xorg.conf:
     HorizSync from the Monitor section
      VertRefresh from the Monitor section
      Modes from Display subsection in Screen section
      ModeLine from the Monitor section (but this one is often useful)

Is this true? Is there no consequence at all?


Now you're getting up to date with your version of Ubuntu!  And you are right - xrandr will allow you to set these things as you want them if required.

> **jeffmills said:**
> 
Does this mean that in my situation (or anybody for that matter) wanting to use MergedFB, better use RandR 1.2?

Definitely.  UNLESS you are trying to use two graphics cards.

### Post by Lothas on 2007-11-28
Ok, here's the deal. I'm running Gutsy with NVidia 8600 and it's sweet. I have 2 displays and I managed to make them work in 2 different ways but each one with it's own problems:

1. I can enable separate Xs for each screen with Xinerama and I'll be able to play 3D games fullscreen on one of the screens. The cons, Compiz won't run.

2. I can use separate Xs or Twinview (but without Xinerama) and Compiz will work pretty good on both screens, water effects and all. The cons, each fullscreen game I've tried to play appears on the far right side of the right screen so I can only see about 40% of the image.

I haven't found any workarounds yet for these problems... any ideas? suggestions? experiences?
```

```
### Post by Jabb3r on 2007-12-07
I'm having exactly the same spanning issues which I can't work out howto solve yet and even when xinerama wasn't enabled it still boots up things like quake wars accross the 2 screens and not even in the propper way which makes things pretty much unplayable.
I read something about programs need to be compiled with the xinerama use flag and then they should span to whichever monitor there on, but I think there must be more to it so will keep digging. Will post back if I find the solution.

Btw on most of theese configs I see this

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

For xinerama to be enabled it should be

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Hope that helps someone.
```
